# Thread: Wild Side of Crystal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-side-of-crystal.31106/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wild Side Blue — 2025-06-13T04:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-side-of-crystal.31106/post-354586>

My name is Crystal and here is my story..... I was born to fight. And at 43 I'm still fighting. Here's how.

My grandmother Fitzpatrick skin type 2.
My mother Fitzpatrick skin type 2.
I'm Fitzpatrick skin type 2.

Grandmother from West Virginia.
Mother and I are from Virginia.
Family (grandmother- my mom’s mom) came from Ireland. Grandfather (my mom’s dad) was Scotch Irish if that matters.

Grandmother loved the sun. Growing up she was a sunriser. She was an artist too. She had 4 girls. She smoked and died of lung cancer. No issues except her teeth hurt so in her 50s she got all her teeth pulled out. My mother and I don’t think she had EDS.

My mother lots of issues. She is not a sun riser. Centralized medicine is doing a number to her now with meds and surgeries. She will not listen to me on any advice on how to get better and that’s on her. My mom does not have EDS.

Me EDS hypermobility. However my father (dead beat) was a drug user and alcoholic and kicked out from the military after he served Vietnam. His night life would be a contributing factor. He’s no longer a part of my life for a long time now. He's a worthless homeless crackhead.

At birth, the umbilical cord wrapped around my neck and had to be delivered by forceps. To the first year non stop febrile seizures. Every time I hit a fever I had seizures and the only way to stop was my parents putting my in ice baths. The pediatrician told my mom ice cold baths work and eventually seizures would go away in a year. It did and poor mom (dad wasn't much help). Did any vaccines in 1982 cause this or was this luck of the draw from my parents?

Also, born with both cervical ribs. However they did not fully developed but are like little nubs. My present doc from story below calls them angel wings.

Fast forward. I was a very alpha female and loved surfing. I was very good at it. Then....

In highschool into college. Surfed in the day and went to the clubs at night to breakdance. Yes, I was in a breakdance crew. Lol. In college, I did work late nights waiting tables. Night time was my jam. I know now that was not a great thing.

Age 20 (March 2002) in Virginia. Car accident. My right hip hit my emergency brake. No seat belt. Body flew into passenger side and face went through passenger window. All was okay except my lower back. SI Joint pain.

I never recovered. More pain. Went to my general doctor, Dr. Lynne Stockman D.O. and she did osteopathic OMT and said go to PT. So I went.

Went to this shit MD, Dr. Arthur Wardell Orthopedic Surgeon (fuck him) at his PT clinic. I was the biggest test subject known to man to him. Multiple rounds prolotherapy in my SI Joints, epidurals, his chiropractor (who strongly over adjusted me), and I couldn't get through PT. Dr. Wardell put me on the following because he said it would make me better as I go through PT. AM meds - somas muscle relaxer, vicodin, celebrex. PM meds - percocets, flexerils, and some kinds of anxiety meds to keep me from not killing myself. I could no longer walk and the pain was unbearable for my back. On top of the chiropractor adjusted me. I begged Dr. Wardell that this was not working and to take me off all meds. He said stay on them. Final straw, my knees started hurting. I brought my mom with me and told her to draw on my back to mark the area where it hurt. His fucking answer was your back hurts because your knees hurt. And now we need to do exploratory surgery on your knees. I said so what are the odds that I can walk again. His said that's risk he would take. I walked out and never came back to him. 9 months of fucking hell with him.

I went back for a follow up to Dr. Stockman. Told her everything and handed over all my meds. She said OMG what has he done to you. My SI joints were out and she adjusted my back then quickly my knees were fine. She said my back was out and putting stress on the knees. She sent me to another doc for a better PT. FYI I NEVER had any withdrawals from all those meds.

I found a good doctor next. Dr. Lisa Barr, M.D. She looked at my giant stack of files from Dr. Wardell. She said oh my god. What did he do to you? She said his prolotherapy was incorrect. And she couldn't put me through hell. She said see my PT. I did pretty good afterwards for years.

Overtime SI joints kept slipping out so I eventually got a Serola belt. I wore it while surfing. I never went to a follow up.

\*Side note either 2005 or 2006. Another Doc named Dr. Koduri talked me into the Garasil jabs. Her exact words was get it so I wouldn't get cervical cancer. Biggest mistake at getting those 3 jabs at 26 years old 6 months apart. I fired her afterwards but wish I never listened to her.

In 2010, I moved to the Outer Banks from Portsmouth, Virginia. Age 28 just 3 weeks after I moved there. I was surfing past the sandbar with my great friend Rascoe and low tide came quick. Surfed a wave outside and came into the inner sandbar. Jumped off the board and dove head first. My head hit the ocean floor. I hit so hard I saw light and thought all my teeth got chipped. Teeth were good. Rascoe said it hit the floor really hard. Days later (idiot) went to ER because I couldn't swallow food and the pain in my neck was bad. CT Scan showed only small tissue damage (whiplash) and nothing major. Back to PT but in North Carolina. My neck never got strong and got no answers why but PT saying I'm flexible. However, I'm not really bendy flexible or double jointed. Oh well. I went to many years of chiropractor and no relief. No instability.

Age 33 (2015) in Outer Banks. Bicycle accident. Left ankle level 2 sprain and sprained both wrists. Here's where the shit show begins.

2016\. I decided to go back to Dr. Lisa Barr to get answers. She does regenerative medicine and owns her own practice for 40 years. She is not owned by a hospital or insurance company. Dr. Barr didn't remember me from back in the day but she said kid you have EDS hypermobility. And I told her I was going to have the ankle surgery. She said you better not do that and get an MRI immediately. I said the Dr. Nicholas Midis, MD didn't need one. He knew where to go. I didn't listen to her and it was the biggest mistake of my life.

After surgery she cussed me out in her patient room. I remember that fully (we laugh at it now). She said next time you fucking listen to me you better because now we have a problem. I scar badly with EDS hypermobility and the ankle scar was a major fucking problem. Luckily she studied under a Doctor from New Zealand and did neural prolotherapy and it worked my ankle scar and other scars eventually. That ankle scar was the biggest problem for me on gait but let's just to the prolotherapy story with her. Dr. Barr studied prolotherapy under Dr. Gustav Hemwall himself (one of its originators) in the early 90s. And this was the start with her on the journey of a wild ride.

For the record. I was a hot mess when I saw her in 2016. She calls me the stray cat who clawed at her door and never left. Thats true!

What finally made me go see her again was because I had a ten year plan. And it scared me. 5 years disability and where would I live and who would take care of me (I'm single). The next 5 years I would have ended it. Although the suicide paln was 10 years out, it was a point of my life that I had to change fast. Real fast! I laugh at it now but that was the reality back then. Although it pisses me off I lost so much time and many of things I wanted to do. I wish I could get that time back.

\*Side note 2015 melanoma in situ on my back. Got cut out and had a lot scaring and nerve pain. What totally fixed that was neural prolotherapy (aka scar tissue injections). 2016 melanoma on my neck. Once again neural prolotherapy for scarring. 2022 basil cell on my face. Once again neural prolotherapy for scarring. I knew during this time my vitamin D was low because had it tested and it was around 30s. I was even tan until this. Then the cancer thing scared me to tan (thanks dermatologist). Fuck that now. I’m in full sun.

2015 - tooth implant after 2nd round of braces and a Maryland bridge gave out after 15 years. No bone graphing. Tooth #7

2016 - lots of nerve pain in tooth #8. Tooth pulled and no bone graphing. Second tooth implant. Big mistake here (scroll down why).

From the years to current. I have had head to toe injected with prolotherapy. Dr. Barr spent many many hours with me after hours or weekends at her practice. She literally put a lot of time into me. She told me never to give up nor think that this won't work. She said I must do my part in strengthening. She wasn't going to tolerate me not doing my homework. I listen to her now.

When I say a doctor saved my life it was her. She fully saved me. When I say how much prolotherapy I had, it was a lot. But I was a patient of hers that had EDS hypermobility with the most injuries she ever seen. I will only mention to Dr. Kruse privately if ever asked how this was possible.

2023 had to move back to Virginia Beach, VA. My wedding videography business closed, I had no where to live anymore in Kill Devil Hills, NC, and I need to be closer to Dr. Barr for treatments. One of the most depressing times was to leave Kill Devil Hills and come back to Virginia. I hate Virginia.

June 2025. I just had a great follow up with Dr. Barr. Present mechanically and externally she says I'm great. And she said nice tan. (Thanks Uncle Jack for drilling about sun). She thinks the pelvic floor dysfunction on my left side could be internal. So I'm seeing a pelvic floor PT (not owned by insurance or hospitals). She actually went to the PT for a soft opening last week and spoke to the owners. She said they may do something internal but go see them. I'm going.

Fall 2025, it’s definitely the left inside leg. Almost like the adductor muscle is trying to kick in. Somehow it’s like a twist feeling in that area. Foot pattern has changed from it. I think we are going in the right direction. I’m still limited to driving past 20 minutes. Long distance driving will cause some issues.

\*side note - 2024 and 2025. Had to get a tooth implant reversal, bone Ridge augmentation, and new implant repositioning with new crown on tooth #7. And new crown to tooth #8. That was hell of a surgery for bone ridge augmentation. Turns out old surgeon needed to bone graph and positioned it incorrectly. Also had to get Invisaligns due to my pelvic floor being out and my bite shifted. That nightmare of teeth is now done as of Oct 2025. Thank god!

The best doctor and patient relationship we have is communication. Ask anyone knows me. They say as a patient I can explain gait patterns like no one ever. I'm so intune and that many PTs and random medical people jaws drop when I tell them. Oh I have drawings too. Many different patterns depending what major area of prolotherapy has been explained. Dr. Barr looks forward to me sending video updates of my shift patterns. I send her and my old PT, Ellen (from Outer Banks) every update by text. Ellen says kiddo you nail it each time on progress videos and you are a showcase to EDS of what Dr. Barr has done. Ellen is in her 60s and owns her PT (not owned by insurance or hospitals). Ellen says you stand out by any EDS patients she’s so proud of me not giving up. Ellen at Seaside Physical Therapy in Kitty Hawk, NC is the fucking the bomb and gets it.

Let's get to another part. The last part of this EDS fucking journey is going to be Uncle Jack. That is the missing key. But sun is going to save me. I will not listen to another dermatologist saying stay out of the sun. Also, I bought the Daytime and Nighttime prescription Lucia Eyes. I wear them a lot and ditched the sunglasses and sunscreen. All 2025 summer, no sunscreen and no sunglasses and did not burn. I tanned!!!!! Big step for me but I know there is more. And probably a slap in the face by Jack is coming. What a learning journey for me. It’s been great.

Guys thank you for reading. I'm a fighter since 1982 and I'm loud and will stand my ground. Maybe that's why I'm still single. LOL. Too alpha. Uncle Jack thoughts. I know I want to move out of Virginia. Each day El Salvador is looking real good. Hoping to come on vacay there end or this year to scope it out depending on this pelvic floor strengthening.

If not, Kill Devil Hills may be an option to go back too. I hate the military area in VB with the 5Gs everywhere at the oceanfront. Oh goal is to surf again, get back to playing drums, and more sun. What to do now is learn on Bitcoin and lessons from here. Nothing will stop me.

\*\*\*\*Side note Google Sheets of Uncle Jack podcasts. I keep this updated as well. Cheers!

### [Kruse](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vA-OPXzFk99DS1NwvptMevxoWDTgrO2fJKw5lv3lzSw/edit?usp=sharing)

 docs.google.com

Been in the sun as much as possible this summer and feel great. Seeing the sunrise about 4 times a week. Goal is to be every day. Had another round of Prolotherapy near my left tail bone where the hamstring attaches and feeling a gait change. Also, just got my Vitamin D and K checked on 8/6. Was hoping my D would be more for the amount of sun I have been in. But it’s better than it used to be.

I'm happy to hear that prolotherapy has been helpful.. I have a bit of training in some other forms of Prolotherapy.. there are all sorts of concoctions that are used under the umbrella of Prolotherapy..

I have decided to not pursue its use as I've increased my quantum biology knowledge.. the idea behind the therapy, I believe, is to create a bit of inflammation to get the body to repair itself... so there is some validity to its use. As Dr. Jack has written about recently EDS in depth on the blogs... EDS is an environmental/nnemf/light problem... this degrades mitochondrial power and creates from oxidative stress in the form of ROS(reactive oxygen species)... so adding more inflammation to an already stressed out system could potentially cause more problems.. not saying they will, but its theoretically possible. in my mind Prolotherapy is bit of a half truth...

Eastern Virginia is where the Navy keeps their Atlantic fleet.. lots of nnEMF in your area.. the fact that you've been in the sun all summer and your Vitamin D is only 49 is cause for concern, but it might also mean you just need to spend more time in the sun too!

have you had a Building biologist check your Living space? that might be a good idea if not.

### Wild Side Blue — 2025-08-09T18:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-side-of-crystal.31106/post-357895>

Hey Doug thanks for the info!!! I know Jack has many articles on EDS in his doctor membership. I’m hoping one day to read more on it. I did read his article about EDS being hypersensitive to EMFs and Bluelight. I have not gotten anyone to check my place nor was planning anything on it. I rent and was not hoping to be here long. Even if I did get it checked my landlord wouldn’t do anything and extra funds is a no for me. I’m barely getting by as a single female and high rent. The small amount I have is going to Bitcoin. I do not want to be here long term. So to mitigate that would be way more sun and sunrise every day!

My goal is to get the heck out of Virginia within a year. Last time I got my Vitamin D checked was 2 years ago and it was at 32 during the spring. But when I got scared from the dermatologist for staying out of the sun due to melanoma that was the reason why for being so low. I don’t listen to dermatologist now and laughed when I just went for a follow up last month. I was ruled out with no concerns but dermatologist said you look tan. Have you been wearing sunscreen? lol. I laughed and told her I’m doing things differently now and I feel physically better from it. Near the end she said well make sure you get at least 15 minutes of sun and wear sunscreen afterwards. That’s one doctor I’m not listening too. My body was a wreck for staying away from the sun. She just proved to me I’m no threat to skin cancer after this checkup. I Had mos skin cancer removal on my face (basil cell) and had 2 melanomas on my back and second rib. And to think it was having EDS and a lot of injuries that kept me inside as well. I would love to know a hell of a lot more from Jack on those blogs so hoping he will release more of them. I just need a game plan and keep stacking small on Bitcoin to get out of the area.

Also another update since July. I went to a pelvic floor PT and had 2 internal evaluations. No issues with tail bone injury nor prolapse issues (no kids but EDS tend to have it). I’m strong. However PT noticed left side had more tension from pubis bone to center in vaginual examine. I mentioned my left adductor muscle pulls downward out of pelvic area. She said why the pulling forward and down of the hip was the hamstring attachment to the sit bone. After two sessions the hamstring pain got worse. So that’s when I got Prolo. I’m now at my 4th PT visit and after another vaginal exam, no tension behind the pubis bone but slight in the center. That means the Prolo is working. Now PT excerises is getting easier. I can feel the fascia pulling around from my spine to my neck to check bone. I still can’t drive for longer than 15 mintues due to the pelvic floor pull which leads into the right side of my neck pulling it down and hitting on the vagus nerve. Not a good feeling because it’s almost like a panic attack from it. I know this is a
Musculoskeletal issue due to the wreckness of my past. The goal to be able to be in more sun, more sunrises, playing drums (not electrical), ride a damn bicycle, walk longer, and drive for fucks sake longer than 15 minutes with a strong pelvic floor, and basically be 20 years old again. Is that hard to ask? Lol. Anyways anyone out there, keep fighting and don’t give up. Never give up!

### Wild Side Blue — 2025-09-01T23:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wild-side-of-crystal.31106/post-359017>

New book read. Progress for tomorrow’s Prolotherapy. Went do Dr. Barr and explained to her my new gait change from 3 weeks ago of Prolotherapy at my left sit bone. Although good improvement but my left side is still pulling forward and dropping down which pulls my left psoas. Dr Barr examined my gait and noticed the problem, inferior ilosacral sheet with strain to SIJ ligaments and sacrotuberous ligament. I can’t wait for this left side to strengthen in the glute and pull facia to ground normally in the heal of the left foot. It’s starting to ground real good in the foot (finally) but the strain and pain around that SI is giving me instability where I need it. Although not looking forward to the Fluro C Arm machine to get Prolo so sun weekend it is to help with that. And sunrise. Keep everyone posted with progress. ; )

### Wild Side Blue — 2025-12-24T18:08:52+0200

Update. I noticed my either my left hamstring or glute was in a lot of pain now. Over time it was slight but lots at this point. Last week I went to a follow up with Dr. Barr and she noticed it was my hamstring that was very sensitive by touch. Also she performed a hamstring test and noticed it was an issue. She proloed my left medial hamstring tendon insertion.

So far I’m noticing a big gait change. I think this hamstring got hurt 10 years ago from the bicycle accident. It still will take a few weeks for the Prolo to tighten up but I’m noticing my hamstring exercises are no longer loosely and over tired. I can do more exercises and no pain. However the muscle needs a lot PT since it was so weak from the strain from the accident.

Also, for Christmas I bought myself a Daylight computer. I’m very stoked on how amazing it is. Now I’m stacking the lessons on Decentralized Medicine. And planning on purchasing Kruse for Dummies in January. Hell yeah!!!

### Wild Side Blue — 2026-01-02T16:12:14+0200

I watched this podcast few days ago and Jack mentioned this Life Extension to get labs ordered.

12/30 - I went to [https://www.lifeextension.com/](https://www.lifeextension.com/) to get my Vitamin D order. Paid $47. In less than 12 hours, I got my order. 12/31 - I went to Labcorp and they said there is no additional charge to get it drawn since I already paid to get the order. 1/2- I got t my results today. Winning!!

Also note the prolotherapy in my medial hamstring tendon insertion was a huge success. My gait is changing rapidly and strengthening is getting much easier. My deep pelvic floor muscles are engaging and no longer twisting and having a major tug of war with left and right side. I can’t believe 10 years ago my life plan was shit.

2016 I was in trouble
2021 I thought I would be on disability and have no where to live
2026 I would have ended my life

I have beat of the odds. Now it’s time to up my game to understand as much as I can with Jack. Happy New Year!

# Thread: Eating like a great white shark and the tca cycle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2025-06-14T23:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354645>

There is something I am not fully understanding. The blogs and many webinars emphasize the importance of eating like a great white shark and entering the PPP. But I am also reading the newer blogs where Uncle Jack emphasizes that we must also use the TCA cycle. Can you use the TCA cycle if you eat like s great white shark? And if not, at what point in the journey do we need to no longer live in the PPP?
Any insights you all on putting these pieces together in my head is appreciated

### Mat.I — 2025-06-15T02:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354650>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=354645)
>
> There is something I am not fully understanding. The blogs and many webinars emphasize the importance of eating like a great white shark and entering the PPP. But I am also reading the newer blogs where Uncle Jack emphasizes that we must also use the TCA cycle. Can you use the TCA cycle if you eat like s great white shark? And if not, at what point in the journey do we need to no longer live in the PPP?
> Any insights you all on putting these pieces together in my head is appreciated

My basic understanding is (keen to hear others thoughts on this) -

The PPP (Pentose Phosphate Pathway) and the TCA cycle are not in conflict, they’re just used differently depending on your redox state and environmental inputs like light, season, and latitude.

The PPP is a repair and survival pathway, it makes NADPH, which rebuilds your redox system, immune function, and antioxidant capacity. You live here when your mitochondria are struggling (from stress, nnEMFs, trauma, etc.).

The TCA cycle, on the other hand, is your energy production pathway, it works best when your redox is strong and your mitochondria can handle full electron flow through Complex I–IV without generating excessive ROS.

You start in the PPP to rebuild the terrain. Once redox is repaired (you’re sleeping better, handling light, cold, and stress more efficiently), you re-engage the TCA cycle, still eating like a shark.

Same food, different mitochondrial processing depending on where you are in the recovery..

PPP = Redox Rehab (winter, cold, stress, early healing)

TCA = Redox Thrive (sunlight, summer, restored coherence)

So yes, you can use the TCA cycle while still eating like a shark, but only once your mitochondria are ready. The food doesn’t change, your mitochondrial function does.

### Emmanuela — 2025-06-15T23:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354677>

Thanks for your reply! I will do more reading on understanding the TCA cycle as well.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-17T09:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354748>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=354677)
>
> Thanks for your reply! I will do more reading on understanding the TCA cycle as well.

No worries.. I'm still learning too.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-17T11:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354749>

Kreb's bicycle posts are not new at all on Patreon. Some of them are 6 years old.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-18T03:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-354784>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=354749)
>
> Kreb's bicycle posts are not new at all on Patreon. Some of them are 6 years old.

Thanks Jack. I went over the QT 8, 9, 11, & 12 blogs.

Just a few further comments to what I already posted above..

Re: the circadian oscillation between PPP & TCA - what about the diurnal and light driven oscillations that could occur during one day e.g. IR at sunrise supports PPP and NAPDH regeneration, UVB at solar noon signals full TCA engagement & anaplerosis & Sunset (red/NIR) shifts the terrain back into recovery mode.. As metabolism is photonic and circadian the mito fuel usage and TCA engagement would not be binary as they oscilate in real time modulated by the solar angle , spectral quality, and hormonal entrainment etc..

Re: Complex II. Maybe this is addressed in other blogs, however the focus was CI & III in the blogs as main sources of ROS / redox collapse etc.. There is the CII bypass which funnels succinate through FADH2 w/o pumping protons.. This is used when CI is impaired or NAD + is depleted.. This could partially preserve TCA under redox stress or the succinate could become a ROS amplifier.

Re: NADPH from PPP & NADH from TCA - when NADPH supresses NAD+ regeneration it knocks CI offline as CI flux requires steady input of NADH + sink of available NAD+.. Basically high NADPH signal mito not to oxidise NADH so NAD+ drops, NADH builds up = CI stalling no electron flow and ATP from NADH.. Any condition that elevates ROS, NO or slows NAD+ regeneration will push towards NADPH dominance via PPP..

Re: cataplerosis vs anaplerosis - VDR mTOR and redox signalling regulate "permission" to engage cat/ana , however wouldn't NAD + ultimately control this flow? As even if the cataplerosis is allowed by VDR or mTOR the TCA cycle will stall if NAD + is unavailable? So ultimately no NAD + = no flux through TCA = results in no entry or exit??

Is my understanding of this correct?

NAD + = the clutch
VDR & mTOR = brakes
PPP = reverse gear
TCA = drive gear
CII = neutral gear / emergency override

### eve001 — 2025-09-16T15:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-359594>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=354645)
>
> There is something I am not fully understanding. The blogs and many webinars emphasize the importance of eating like a great white shark and entering the PPP. But I am also reading the newer blogs where Uncle Jack emphasizes that we must also use the TCA cycle. Can you use the TCA cycle if you eat like s great white shark? And if not, at what point in the journey do we need to no longer live in the PPP?
> Any insights you all on putting these pieces together in my head is appreciated

To my current understanding - feel free to adjust if wrong:

B1 depletion due to nnEMF and blue light will cause problems with both PPP and Krebs/TCA cycle.
Both PPP and TCA produce resp. NADPH/H and NAD/H which are important input molecules for the ETC.
If they don't deliver the input I wonder if the ETC can run nicely and attract oxygen at CCO to make DDW.
PPP is linked to the glutathion redox system - which is likely to get depelted if you get the fenton reaction and hydroxyl radicals from nnEMF exposure. PPP is also important in RBC. These do not have mitochondria. RBC need lots of B1.

The early Hypoxia blogs (1-5) are about this.

### Emmanuela — 2025-09-17T01:14:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-like-a-great-white-shark-and-the-tca-cycle.31110/post-359622>

I smell a reading spree coming my way.
Hypoxia blogs and QT 8,9,11,12

On it!

### AntonisK — 2025-06-16T13:32:39+0300

Hi,
I have a friend who despite receiving lots of sun exposure without any "protection" has a very low vit. D3 levels in the bloodstream. His doctor told him that some people have a genetic difficulty absorbing/creating vit. D and gave him D3+K2 along with folic acid to enhance absorption.
Since his upper body is fully covered with tatoos, I wonder if this has anything to do with this.

### Johan Lindøm — 2025-06-16T15:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-problem.31114/post-354692>

Your skin and the light you get need to be natural to be able to produce vitamin d. Years with the wrong light will ruin your surfaces. It's possible to regenerate the skin's ability, but The tattoos is a lasting problem. Also your lipid panel, your hormones and medicine will affect how you are able to utilize the light you get.

### MTA321 — 2025-06-16T17:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-problem.31114/post-354698>

Could be several factors at play. Tattoos can diminish vitamin D production in and of themselves. May have a vitamin A problem. Vitamin A is yoked to Vitamin D. When Vitamin A is low, Vitamin D follows suit. What destroys Vitamin A? Isolated blue light and nnEMF. nnEMF also causes dehydration and when you do not have properly hydrated cells that can impair the body's ability to turn LDL into Vitamin D. A big reason why people that live in cities bathed in nnEMF have lower vitamin D levels.

### AlbertD — 2025-06-16T18:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-problem.31114/post-354701>

Grounding is a prerequisite for proper POMC cleavage (Reference: Centralized Medicine #35)

### Kirsten F — 2025-06-16T21:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kirstens-optimal-journal-take-2.31121/post-354714>

Hi everyone, this is my second foray into an optimal journal. I have been learning for over a year and benefiting greatly from this community. A couple of months ago, I wrote a comprehensive optimal journal, then quickly chickened out and deleted it. While I consider myself an open person, I have intentionally created a low profile online, and this online sharing doesn't feel comfortable. But it is needed to process my thoughts, and perhaps get insight from the community. So here goes.

I will start with my current health rabbit hole, **H63D Homozygosity (the Hereditary Hemochromatosis genetic defect that is considered "insignificant"**)

\- Diagnosis confirmed 5 years ago after abnormalities incidentally detected on routine labs
\- No known family hx
\- Was told by two centralized doctors that it is clinically insignificant and not to have serial iron studies
\- I am now concerned about it due to the Fenton reaction and free radical formation and neurodegeneration
\- Hepcidin problem: decreased hepcidin -> increased iron absorption, especially non-heme Fe. The risk is increasing NTBI (non-transferrin bound iron) that is not directly measured by lab tests. The NTBI would be responsible for the Fenton reaction. A surrogate for NTBI levels is TSat (transferrin saturation) > 55-60%. TSat > 60% = Fenton reacion.

Recently, I was found to have TSat at 62.3%, then donated blood because Hb and Ferritin were adequate.

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>April 2025</td><td>June 2025 *** 2 months post blood donation</td></tr><tr><td>Hb</td><td>13.3</td><td>12.7</td></tr><tr><td>Ferritin</td><td>77</td><td>36</td></tr><tr><td>Fe</td><td>166</td><td>158</td></tr><tr><td>TSat</td><td>62.3%</td><td>55.5%</td></tr><tr><td>TIBC</td><td>267</td><td>285</td></tr></tbody></table>

My goals are to minimize the need for donation and slow the rate of iron accumulation.

Some things I have learned about Fe content in food:
\- _Shrimp and oysters_ are very high in Fe! I had been consuming a lot of shrimp and now will reconsider
\- _Beef and pork_: avoiding
\- _Chicken_, cooked in beef tallow: wondering if Jack would consider this an exception to his "fowl is foul" advice, because it would be a source of protein with a bit of good fat and lower iron
\- _Vitamin C_: avoiding (increases Fe absorption)
\- _Dairy_: binds to and inhibits absorption of Fe, I have started having a cup of unpasteurized milk with an iron heavy meal
\- _Green Tea_: would be ideal to inhibit Fe absorption, but I avoid it because high in fluoride

My current conclusions are:
\- Will consider donation again once TSat > 60% AND Hb > 13 AND Ferritin > 40. I think you can only donate 3 times a year in ES, so I need to slow this down as the levels are ticking up quickly after 2 months
\- Adding more wild-caught salmon and tuna to my diet. ChatGPT identifies them as having a high DHA:iron ratio. Will be a big change from grass-fed beef and shrimp, which were big staples.

Have a great day!

Hope you don't mind me commenting.. Just a few things come to mind -

\- Can't too frequent donation make this worst though due to a drop in Hb and ferritin as Hepcidin would be suppressed even further that would result in a disordered rebound (with even more absorption)? Maybe temp lowers Fe but trade off is the increase in oxidative risk..
\- Your numbers show Tsat only dipped slightly, serum iron barely moved (high absorption?), Fe went to half (stores used but not stabalised), Hb still down (RBC mass not full restored), TIBC went up (rebound response as detailed above.. e.g. body trying to bind more iron = absorption wide open)
\- Have you got your MetHb% measured? as with the H63D I would have thought your buffering capacity would be reduced e.g. potentially oxidizing faster than you are reducing?
\- How is your environment in terms of nn-emf levels? (I wrote a recent thread in my EMF journal about how VGCCs activation lead to excess NO which oxidises Fe2+ to Fe3+) & knocks CCO offline..
\- I assume you are well versed in how low dose MB may help you here?

You could check the MetHB non-invasive with Co-oximetry devices such as Masimo Rad-57, Radical 7 etc (with rainbow set sensors and firmware upgrade enabled).. You can get refurbished units or just go somewhere to measure it.. They will have other metrics such as COHb, O2Hb, HHb, etc.. that may be helpful particularly the O2Hb & HHb with MetHb in your case..

### Mat.I — 2025-06-17T08:22:41+0300

You can get refurbished units or just go somewhere to measure it.. They will have other metrics such as COHb, O2Hb, HHb, etc.. that may be helpful particularly the O2Hb & HHb with MetHb in your case..

Sorry for all the posts.. I'm just putting more things together after I read what I post.. I wanted to add that even COHb as a non-smoker may be a useful metric as we can produce endogenous CO as a by-product of heme degradation via heme oxygenase (HO-1).. E.g. Fe2+ > Biliverdin + Fe3+ + CO.. It will bind quite easily as CO has ~ 250 x higher affinity for hemaglobin than O2..

I only know about this as I was helping a work colleague who I could see that was heavily FE3+ shifted just based on skin tone and after talking he confirmed his longstanding fatigue and other issues that could not be explained by the centralised Docs. So I went down a few rabbit holes for him..

I appreciate all of the comments. I did blood work 1 week post-donation (to quantify the effects), and the Hb was 12.4, Hb 115, TIBC 278, TSat 41.5%. Ferritin not done. Interestingly, freeT3 was only 2.48 and then 2.98 two months post donation (increased free iron interfering with conversion from T4?). I agree that if the free iron and TSat shoot back up and the ferritin and Hb lag behind, donation isn’t a viable strategy. Had unsuccessfully searched for blogs and forum content covering donation, please let me know if you have any suggested reading material.

I had thought that many of the redox principles that Jack talks about pertain to heme-based iron, not NTBI, so I will need to think about it more from this lens. MetHb measurement would be useful. Yes, MB might be helpful but i don’t want it orally (bactericidal) and am not sure how durable the effects of IV MB are?

In terms of nnEMF, I am doing quite well. Optimized by building biologists, hardwired internet minimizing cell use. Though always room for improvement.

Thanks again for taking the time to write. Your comments are helping me shift from a biochemical to a more quantum perspective.

### Kirsten F — 2025-08-26T22:15:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kirstens-optimal-journal-take-2.31121/post-358777>

Monthly iron status labs show that the risk of non-transferrin bound iron (NTBI) has returned, since transferrin saturation now > 60%. With NTBI, Fenton reaction occurs.

Overall, I am pleased that the time interval has been > 4.5 months since last donation, and my ferritin and Hb have recovered enough to allow another donation, which I did yesterday.

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>April, pre-donation</td><td>June</td><td>July</td><td><b>Aug, 4.5 m post-donation</b></td></tr><tr><td>Hb</td><td>13.3</td><td>12.7</td><td>13.9</td><td><b>12.7</b></td></tr><tr><td>Hct</td><td>39.2</td><td>38.2</td><td>41.9</td><td><b>38.4</b></td></tr><tr><td>RBC</td><td>3.56</td><td>4.01</td><td>4.44</td><td><b>4.08</b></td></tr><tr><td>TSat %</td><td>62.3</td><td>55.5</td><td>52.8</td><td><b>60.4</b></td></tr><tr><td>TIBC</td><td>267</td><td>285</td><td>336</td><td><b>279</b></td></tr><tr><td>Iron</td><td>166</td><td>158</td><td>177</td><td><b>168</b></td></tr><tr><td>Ferritin</td><td>77</td><td>36</td><td>33.7</td><td><b>70.5</b></td></tr></tbody></table>

Most recent labs likely affected by overhydration and hemodilution. New goal is to encourage better erythropoiesis, so excess iron can be used in Hb and RBC mass.

Have been cold plunging up to my neck 5-7 times/week, now 25 minutes, 54-57 F. In full sunlight (which feels glorious). Have recently started breatholding during CT, now for 100 seconds about 5 times per session.

Going to try to leverage the breath holds to increase EPO production and increase RBC mass. WIll continue to monitor labs monthly, and would be amazing to see Hb and Hct rising higher than they have previously, to put the excess Fe to good use. Need to research the best resources and best protocol for breath-holding to encourage erythropoiesis.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
Otherwise, I have improved my understanding of H63D homozygosity. Including my notes here for future reference

\- In HFE gene, there is a D substitution (aspartic acid) instead of H (histidine) at the 63 position of the HFE protein.
\- HFE protein interacts with TfR1 (Transferrin receptor 1) on cell surface
\- When plasma iron is high, transferrin (iron carrier) binds TfR1, and HFE is displaced to interact with TfR2, which helps to signal to the liver to increase hepcidin production. Hepcidin then binds ferroportin (only know Fe exporter) on enterocytes and macrophages, causing it to be internalized and degraded --> less iron absorption and release into plasma
\- In H63D, HFE's interaction with these receptors is impaired but not destroyed. **LOWERED HEPCIDIN results, with INAPPROPRIATE LEVELS OF FERROPORTIN**

\-Ferroportin: hepcidin inhibits iron absorption by binding to ferroportin, leading to its degradation. Found in enterocytes but also macrophages. Macrophages export recycled iron from cells to blood after RBC breakdown
\- high ferroportin can cause hepatocytes to inappropriately increase Fe export from their stores
\- In some with H63D, Fe is exported too quickly from hepatocytes and macrophages via unregulated ferroportin
\- because Fe isn't retained inside storage cells, ferritin doesn't accumulate, despite elevated serum iron and transferrin saturation
\- So: iron overlad in blood and tissues (eg. heart, pancreas), but normal or low ferritin in lab tests. Especially seen in early-stage H63D overload in lean individuals, or people with concurrent metabolic or inflammatory factors
\- Vit D has some indirect regulatory effects on iron metabolism and inflammation
\- circadian rhythms modulate hepcidin expression and light (esp 480 nm) via SCN
\- RBC production/erythropoiesis is tightly regulated and not driven by Fe availability alone
\- **EPO - produced in kidneys in response to hypoxia <--
breathwork
**\- bone marrow activity (hematopoietic stem cells)
\- iron supply
\- therefore, erythropoises is deman driven, not supply driven
\- Fe has high oxidative potential, especially when high transferrin binding capacity and NTBI forms
\- tissues most affected are those that take up Fe vs. non-transferrin pathways or have slow turnover
\- liver
\- pancreas (B cell)
\- pituitary - hypogonadism
\- joints - synovial deposition, oxidative damage with arthropathy
\- muscle, skin thyroid IF overload is severe
\- Mechanism:
\- excess dietary or recycle Fe --> overwhelmed transferrin --> NTBI
\- NTBI taken up by cells via:
\- ZIP14 and other specific metal transporters
\- calcium channels - in heart, pancreas
\- endocytosis and scavenger receptors
\- once inside, Fe catalyzes the Fenton Rxn: Fe2+ +H2O2 --> Fe3+ + .OH + OH- ie. generates hydroxyl radicals -->
DNA, RNA, protein damage
\- Fe stored as ferritin or hemosiderin, but when stores
overflow, oxidative stress and cell injury occur
\- CNS somewhat protected + iron handling in CNS tightly
regulated. Still, Fe can accumulate with age, disease or
dysfunction, esp in certain brain regions
\- Fe enters brain mainly via transferrin-bound iron through
transferrin-receptor mediated endocytosis. Some NTBI
may enter through DMT1, ZIP transporters, Ca channels
(under stress).
\- After Fenton reaction, Fe3+ can be reduced back to Fe2+, especially in a redox active environment, creating a
continuous Fenton cycle. This is esp. dangerous with
there's excess ascorbate, NADPH, glutathione, or other
reducing agents
\- If Fe3+ NOT reduced, it is sequestered by ferritin, as Fe3+ core. Forms insoluble aggregates (Hemosiderin) or binds
to molecules like ATP, citrate, phosphate

### Kirsten F — 2025-08-27T17:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kirstens-optimal-journal-take-2.31121/post-358803>

I understand that melanin plays a significant role in chelating iron and helping buffer the ROS generated through the Fenton reaction. I’m well-melanated, at least externally, so I assume this offers me protection.

That said, I’m curious about where the threshold lies — how much iron is “too much”? Since melanin degrades in the process of chelating iron, would the buffering effect be sustainable as long as I am able to maintain my tan?

I also question how reliable the centralized lab data is for determining what TSat levels are considered dangerous, since it is likely based on populations with lower melanin levels and weaker redox systems. For context, about 5–6 years ago my TSat was 73%, but it normalized on its own over time, in to 30s until recently.

### tarascribbins — 2025-06-17T19:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maternal-lineage-and-some-updates.31128/post-354763>

I completely forgot to address my mother and grandmothers.

My mother is 70, and I don’t think really cares to be alive. She never went to a doctor until she nearly died of stage 4 uterine cancer. She’s so mean that the cancer gave up and she beat it. The cancer most likely stems from years of smoking and alcoholism. She’s is one of the most unhealthy people I know, but is just fine now. I inherited my apoe/2 gene from her.

Her mother, died at age 40 of breast cancer. I don’t really have many details about her, due to not have a close relationship with my mother.

Her mother lived to be 90, and I was told it was old age. She was a bad ass.

My labs look good other than my bun/creatine ratio ‍♀️ I just ordered the body voltage kit and I’m going to install kill switches. I also ordered a magnetico.

Next up my thyroid medicine. I was taking 30mg of NP Thyroid for only the last year. I quit taking it 6 days ago. I am going to do a thyroid panel in a week or two, what is the best blog for optimal thyroid panel? Any increase in thyroid med over 30mg would give me SVT. Ami correct in thinking that getting my dehydration figured out, will improve my electrical system and hopefully my thyroid will optimize?

I’m still taking Zyrtec for my new stubborn allergies. I would love to quit it, as I’m sure it’s also very dehydrating. Next mission…get grassed bones for survivor soup.

Still eating seafood daily, and being barefoot outside as much I can.

Optimal is close. I know it! ☀️

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/melkersson-rosenthal-syndrome-any-suggestions-besides-light-water-and-magnetism.31142/post-354879>

### [Melkersson-Rosenthal Syndrome: Symptoms & Treatment](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/23115-melkersson-rosenthal-syndrome-mrs)

Melkersson-Rosenthal syndrome is a rare disorder that affects facial nerves. It causes episodes of facial swelling, facial paralysis and tongue furrowing.

 my.clevelandclinic.org

This leads to changes in proton gradients and the creation of water, impacting local charge environments. A larger EZ from DDW, with its net negative charge, might modulate inflammation or nerve function, offering a decentralized therapeutic angle outside conventional treatments like steroids.

LNPs and Environmental Triggers: If MRS flare-ups are triggered by environmental factors (e.g., infections or allergens, as speculated in some cases), LNPs with varying charges (e.g., neutral in certain regions) could interact with cellular potentials, exacerbating or mitigating symptoms.

Systemic Granulomatous Link: The connection to Crohn’s and sarcoidosis suggests a systemic charge or metabolic imbalance. DDW’s potential to alter cellular charge and improve the Zeta potential should influence granuloma formation, aligning with my idea that quantized electromagnetic processes (e.g., UPEs) are driving this disease process.

In my decentralized thesis, MRS could serve as a case study where centralized medicine overlooks charge and water dynamics. In contrast, a decentralized model, leveraging personalized mitochondrial and EZ water interventions, addresses the root causes rather than the symptoms. The rarity and variability of MRS support a need for individualized, non-uniform approaches.

# Thread: EMF Notes - Suggestions Sought!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TB14 — 2025-06-19T23:09:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354899>

Hi All,

Relative newbie here, wanted to post info about my EMF measurements and mitigation procedures to date at my homes in the Washington, D.C. and Ocala, Florida areas, plus seek some advice and float some ideas. Hoping this can potentially be useful to others and also would be very grateful for any suggestions.

**Magnetic Fields**

_Magnetic Fields in D.C. House_: AC magnetic fields are thankfully lower than average suburban homes in the areas of the D.C. house where I spend the most time, about 0.1-0.2 mG in my bedroom and 0.2-0.4 in most of the rest of the house (as measured by the TriField TF2). I had a plumber put in a PEX insert near where my water main enters my house to cut off what seemed like maybe current coming in on it (potentially from neighbors' grounding systems that are connected to their pipes, which were then conductive all the way to my pipes until putting in the non-conductive PEX insert), though haven’t noticed much difference in magnetic field readings since doing that. I also had an EMF specialist and electrician fix wiring errors at my electric panel, though again without measured reductions in magnetic fields.
There does seem to still be some current on the ground system; any suggestions on where this might be coming from would be awesome, because I and the EMF specialist I’m working with are both kinda stumped at the moment. Have considered the metal gas line, copper A/C refrigerant line, cast iron drain pipe, cable internet/old TV/phone lines, electric panel, and ground rod being too close to the A/C compressor, utility meters, and/or electric service lines outside as possible sources, and it could just be an amalgamation of things, but curious if anyone with current on their ground was able to discover the source and/or resolve it in some way I haven’t thought of yet.

_Magnetic Fields in Florida House:_ Unfortunately AC magnetic fields are higher than I’d like in the Ocala house and coming from the buried utility power lines, which I believe is generally treated as all but impossible to meaningfully address. They get as high as ~1.5-2 mG around mid-day in the summer in the rooms where I spend the most time. At night, however, even in summer, they aren’t nearly as bad, though still not ideal, mostly ranging from 0.3 to 0.6 mG. I measured in winter too and don't think it was nearly as bad even mid-day, but didn't write down my measurements or the times of day at which I took them, so will have to plan to do that again later this year. The TriField TF2 gives pretty much identical readings for weighted and unweighted magnetic fields at the Florida place, which I think is at least a good sign that the magnetic fields here aren't particularly dirty. There doesn't appear to be current on the water main. Haven’t had an EMF specialist out to the place in Ocala yet to check for wiring errors.
Thinking the solution to the powerline mid-day in summer high magnetic field issue is probably just to limit time at the Florida place in summer and use it as a winter sun place, and maybe find something to do out of the house on the summer afternoons that I do spend here. My understanding is there's really nothing you can do about magnetic fields from power lines except leave the house, but if anyone has any experience or even just a notion that contradicts that, I would of course love to hear about it. Not sure if anyone has ever tried or thought about putting MuMetal or other magnetic field blocking material around the circumference of buried power lines outside a property - I don't know if that would be "allowed" under utility terms & conditions or local easement/zoning ordinances, I guess, but in general the area around your power line is your property, since title extends to air above and ground below; plus you wouldn't be interfering with the service or damaging the utility's equipment; and realistically who would ever find out you even did it (especially if the line is buried) unless the power company came to service the part of the line in front of your house, and how often does that happen? I imagine that could be prohibitively expensive for most, and there could be some other reason I'm unaware of why it wouldn't work even if you had the money, but still interesting to think about, especially when the alternative is "move".

**Electric Fields**

_Electric Fields in D.C. House_: Body voltage measurements ("BVMs") laying in my bed in the D.C. house with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference range from 0.06 to 0.1 V. With the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I use an EMFSafeSwitch to remotely cut the circuits before I use the bed. I actually have no idea where the residual 0.06-0.1V is even coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

_Electric Fields in Florida House_: BVMs laying in my bed in Ocala with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference are roughly 0.1 V. As in the D.C. house, with the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I don’t have an EMFSafeSwitch in Florida because I don’t need to use the circuit that most impacts BVMs for anything, so I just keep it off. As with the D.C. house, I have no idea where the residual 0.1V is coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

**Dirty Electricity**

_Dirty Electricity in D.C. House_: DE is quite bad in the D.C. house, as measured by both plug-in meters and by the EMF specialist with a Picoscope 2408. Seems to be coming in from the outside, as the levels measured actually increase as circuits are cut within the house (I guess since the DE isn't getting spread out as much to the circuits other than the one from which the measurement is taken). I don’t currently use any DE filters as I don’t want to risk making the situation worse than it already is and all of the filters I’ve seen on the market appear to have certain frequency ranges of DE that they actually amplify rather than mitigate. I’ve been told DE filters are a developing area of technology and that new and potentially better solutions could be available soon - could be cool to hear about this from anyone who’s more keyed in on it.

_Dirty Electricity in Florida House_: The Ocala house seems to have about half the levels of DE as the DC house (per the Alpha Labs EMI100 plug-in meter), which still isn’t great, but seemingly significantly better. I need to evaluate DE at the Florida house more thoroughly, but I also suspect it’s possible DE could be coming from inside rather than outside (too many goofy modern electronic appliances that came with the house) and thus that levels could potentially be lowered further without the use of a filter.

**RF**
_RF in D.C. House_: Big issue in the D.C. house. High-intensity pulses, probably from radar at Reagan Airport and military bases, are pretty much constant, every five seconds or so. Inside the house, on the second floor, the pulses are typically about 4,000-6,000 microwatts/m^2 (as measured by the Safe and Sound Pro II). They’re about 1,000-2,000 microwatts/m^2 in the semi-subterranean basement, but that’s still obviously way too high. In parts of my back yard, which I unfortunately largely avoid for this reason, the radar pulses can get over 100,000 microwatts/m^2. I sleep within a Swiss Shield Naturell bed canopy with floor sheet that seems to live up to its claim of ~99% RF intensity reduction and brings the measured peak intensity of the pulses down to consistently under 100 microwatts/m^2, usually somewhere in the ~20-50 microwatts/m^2 range. I have no Wi-Fi router in the house and only use Ethernet. I also opted out of my power company SmartMeter, which was sending out strong pulses once a minute, but that didn't seem to make much of a difference in the measurements within the house.
I’m considering painting the entire exterior of my house with Y-Shield or something similar (then grounding it and covering it in some kind of finish to maintain it) and/or lining the floor of my attic with some other RF-reflective material (probably not just solid aluminum foil, to avoid trapping moisture). If anyone has any experience with anything like that or suggestions, like materials used, techniques, maintenance, efficacy, I’d love to hear about it.

_RF in Florida House_: Fortunately the RF situation seems to be much better in Ocala. Levels where I spend most of my time during the day seem to peak at less than 100 microwatts/m^2. In my bedroom the peak is consistently below 20 microwatts/m^2. Interestingly the building materials used for the Ocala house (I think probably thick concrete and/or possibly old lead paint) seem to play a significant role in keeping RF out, as the levels in the back yard and in the lanai (sunroom with windows for walls) are significantly higher (in some areas over 2,000 microwatts/m^2) than inside most of the home. The high RF levels out back combined with the high magnetic field levels from the power line out front however unfortunately mean there’s currently no area of the property that's both super-low RF and relatively low AC magnetic field where I can get sun, which I would like to change if I can. I keep the Wi-Fi feature of the router in the Florida house turned off and only use Ethernet. The house came with a number of smart appliances but fortunately I’ve succeeded in removing the WiFi chip from the stove/oven, turning the microwave’s Wi-Fi feature off, exchanging the washer + dryer for non-smart versions, and finding a TV from which I was easily able to remove the Wi-Fi chip without losing the ability to stream directly from Ethernet or otherwise impairing functionality (2023 Sony XL77; it’s also touted as one of the very few TVs that are flicker-free at all brightness levels by TV evaluation sites, which I haven’t independently confirmed, but could be another selling point for it if true).
I think one of the main sources of RF in Ocala could be my power company SmartMeter, but I haven’t opted out yet, which I’m thinking I probably should do. I might do some window shielding in the Florida house to see how close I can actually get to undetectable levels in certain areas, so if anyone has any window film recommendations or tips, I’d love to hear them.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-20T08:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354906>

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> here does seem to still be some current on the ground system; any suggestions on where this might be coming from would be awesome, because I and the EMF specialist I’m working with are both kinda stumped at the moment. Have considered the metal gas line, copper A/C refrigerant line, cast iron drain pipe, cable internet/old TV/phone lines, electric panel, and ground rod being too close to the A/C compressor, utility meters, and/or electric service lines outside as possible sources, and it could just be an amalgamation of things, but curious if anyone with current on their ground was able to discover the source and/or resolve it in some way I haven’t thought of yet.

Quite straight forward to go through a process of elimination on site to narrow down the cause.. What were the current readings with main switch on vs off?

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> _Electric Fields in D.C. House_: Body voltage measurements ("BVMs") laying in my bed in the D.C. house with the circuits off and using an outdoor ground stake as ground reference range from 0.06 to 0.1 V. With the relevant circuits on and nothing plugged in in the room, they’re about 2.2 V. I use an EMFSafeSwitch to remotely cut the circuits before I use the bed. I actually have no idea where the residual 0.06-0.1V is even coming from (maybe the ground wire?), but if anyone has any ideas or suggestions how to get rid of it, I would love to know.

Body Voltage **alone** is not good to rely upon.. It has many flaws to say the least. Very basic examples
\- it measures voltage (not field strength) i.e. pressure differential between you and the reference point.. By product of capacitive coupling (not direct field measurement)
\- Static accumulation skews readings
\- Low BV does not always = low electric field
\- You can get very low BV whilst still immersed in EF e.g. lying on a foam mattress (very poor conductivity + a dielectric insulator) = body is like a balloon floating in an electric field..

Physics - if capacitance is low (e.g. body floating on foam) then the induced charge is also low (low BV). Coupling governs how much BV appears, however the biological effects are driven by the presence and characteristics of the field it self (not just whether you collect measurable charge)...

BVM only measurements are like testing the depth of the swimming pool with your toe only..
BVM is not wrong, it is just incomplete if that is all you are relying upon.

BV is not an EMF meter. It's a bio-coupling indicator. It tells you how much your body is absorbing, not how strong the field is or how damaging it might be. So it is useful, but only when:

-   You understand its limits
-   You aren’t grounded during testing
-   You use it for comparisons, not absolute field strength

Measure with a decent 3D axis meter or a decent single axis and understand how to measure each axis and calculate the sum e.g. E total = square root of Ex2 + Ey2 + Ez2 (root mean square)

> [TB14 said:](/goto/post?id=354899)
>
> _Dirty Electricity in D.C. House_: DE is quite bad in the D.C. house, as measured by both plug-in meters and by the EMF specialist with a Picoscope 2408. Seems to be coming in from the outside, as the levels measured actually increase as circuits are cut within the house (I guess since the DE isn't getting spread out as much to the circuits other than the one from which the measurement is taken). I don’t currently use any DE filters as I don’t want to risk making the situation worse than it already is and all of the filters I’ve seen on the market appear to have certain frequency ranges of DE that they actually amplify rather than mitigate. I’ve been told DE filters are a developing area of technology and that new and potentially better solutions could be available soon - could be cool to hear about this from anyone who’s more keyed in on it.

I assume the BB mapped, isolated and identified the dominant frequencies on each line for this already..

Cutting the circuits reduces the total sink, hence the concertation.

You will need a whole house "decent" filter within the attenuation range and energy demand.. [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) - could help you out with this..
Just make sure you have identified the dominant harmonic bands and that the selected filter/s will take care of these (quite straight forward) then install and re-measure..

### Mark1969 — 2025-06-20T13:13:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354912>

I’ve been dealing with hot summer nights too—no central AC, just blackout shades, ceiling and floor fans, and sometimes a window unit. I’ve been making some changes and will be working more with Anthony soon, but I wanted to ask:

Would it be _better or “cleaner”_ to run my fans at night off a Bluetti battery backup instead of grid power? I’m considering a relay disconnect system to isolate the circuit. I’d love thoughts on whether this makes any difference in terms of EMFs, power quality, or sleep impact—especially in a low-EMF setup.

Awesome, it hadn't occurred to me that BVM was only one part of the electric field picture but that makes a lot of sense. Thanks very much for pointing that out to me.

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354906)

Do you have any recommendations for a good 3-axis electric field meter? Have been thinking about going in on the NFA1000 for a while but not sure if there's anything cheaper that could still get the job done.

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=354906)
>
> I assume the BB mapped, isolated and identified the dominant frequencies on each line for this already..

He did not, unfortunately. He brought the Picoscope 2408 with him and used it both times he came, but the only data I was ever given from it was three iPhone photos of a laptop screen displaying graphs labeled 0.1 MHz - 2.0 MHz, 1 MHz - 14 MHz, and 1 MHz - 60 MHz. The graphs don't indicate if the measurements were taken with all or any circuits cut off or not. They all appear to be from one outlet, so presumably just one of the two lines was tested. It's also just one graph (I assume voltage), whereas I think (though could be wrong) the frequency of the current can/should also be measured to check for dirty magnetic fields. I did ask him specifically before his second visit to measure the frequencies in preparation for selecting a DE filter, and while he was here he hooked up the Picoscope 2408 to the same outlet again and basically just told me he couldn't tell if anything had changed since last time and provided no additional data. I reluctantly gave him the benefit of the doubt and assumed he did this because it was impossible to identify the frequencies to the level of specificity that would be helpful in selecting a filter, but it sounds like you're saying that what I had requested was something that not only can be done, but actually should be expected to be done as part of an EMF evaluation. That tracks both intuitively and because he was incompetent in a number of other ways, too, so I'm now looking for another EMF specialist to work with for this and other reasons.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-21T02:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354932>

Body voltage does not give the whole picture but it is a useful tool to see if a water pipe, gas pipe, Ethernet cord etc is grounded or not. Hook up the BV meter (with shoes on) then touch the pipe or ethernet cord. If the reading goes down it is grounded. If the reading goes up it is carrying a field (maybe even current). Again, not the whole picture but useful in tracking things down.

Did an inspection on a house. Turned off the whole house and still had 2700mV BV in a child’s room. WHY?
Turns out the Sonos sound system had an automatic 120V power back up. When the main power went off it kicked in and the speaker wire and speaker, in the child’s flood, was putting out a 2700mV field under the child’s bed.

I’ve seen stuff like this on water pipes, ethernet, phone lines etc. all found with a BV meter.

### Mat.I — 2025-06-21T03:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-354934>

Hook up the BV meter (with shoes on) then touch the pipe or ethernet cord. If the reading goes down it is grounded. If the reading goes up it is carrying a field (maybe even current). Again, not the whole picture but useful in tracking things down.

You are brave Anthony.. why not just use a multimeter as intended? Why do you have to use your body as a probe which can be very harmful and dangerous in some situations where there is enough current on the line.. This can be totally avoided by using a proper meter or the multimeter as intended (without your body as the probe)..

### TB14 — 2025-07-25T21:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-356968>

> [PIAZ said:](/goto/post?id=356932)
>
> We wanted a meter to measure surroundings and ourselves - but this sounds too advanced for simpletons!

My first meter was the TriField TF2, which I think is a great intro meter. There is also the Cornet, but I can only speak to the TriField in terms of starter meters. The TF2 does a bit of almost everything at a relatively low price and I still find myself using it on occasion even after purchasing many other much more expensive meters. The TF2 measures:

(1) **AC magnetic fields**
\- 3-axis
\* This makes it easier and quicker to get an idea of exposure from all directions at a given point
\- Frequency Range: 40 Hz - 100 kHz
\* This is a good range; it has the same upper bound, for example, as the much more expensive Gigahertz Solutions ME3851A, and actually a higher upper bound than the Alpha Labs UHS2 (which tops out at 75 kHz)
\- Intensity Range: 0.1 mG - 100.0 mG
\* Comparable or superior to much more expensive meters along this metric as well

(2) **AC electric fields**
\- 1-axis
\* Harder and more time-consuming to get an idea of exposure from all directions at a given point than with a 3-axis meter, but even most more expensive AC electric field meters are 1-axis; plus electric fields are the hardest of the big three to measure in general anyway because the person holding the meter interferes with the measurements; but this can still sometimes be helpful for broad exposure measurement, keeping the limitations in mind. Single-axis AC electric field meters can also be helpful for tracing (live, unshielded) wires in walls.
\- Frequency Range: 40 Hz - 100 kHz
\* This is a good range; it has the same upper bound, for example, as the much more expensive Gigahertz Solutions ME3851A
\- Intensity Range: 1 V/m - 1,000 V/m
\* Comparable or superior to much more expensive meters along this metric as well

(3) **Radiofrequency**
\- Frequency range: 20 MHz - 6 GHz
\* The lower-end of the RF frequency range that the TF2 can measure is one reason I still use it, as my generally superior RF meter, the Safe and Sound Pro II, has a higher lower-end of its frequency range, 200 MHz; meaning, if the producer specifications are accurate, that the TF2 can pick up RF between 20MHz and 200MHz that the Safe and Sound Pro II would miss.
\- Inability to Detect Radar Pulses: Just thought I should note from experience that the Safe and Sound Pro II is capable of picking up high-intensity fast radar pulses that the TF2 misses, which is one major reason (among others) why multiple meters are necessary if more-comprehensive analysis is the goal.

(4) **Dirty Magnetic Fields**
\- The weighted magnetic fields measurement on the TF2 uses a formula under which from frequencies 60 Hz to 500 Hz, sensitivity increases proportionally to frequency, while at frequencies above 1 kHz, sensitivity decreases proportionally to frequency. The implication here would appear to be that magnetic fields with frequencies between 500 Hz and 1,000 Hz (1 kHz) are more harmful to human health than frequencies above or below these levels. I'm not sure of the accuracy of that notion, but comparing unweighted and weighted magnetic field measurements can help you get some idea of the dirtiness of a particular source of current (current produces magnetic fields). For example, I've found that incandescent lightbulbs have roughly equal standard magnetic field and weighted magnetic field measurements, while compact fluorescent lightbulbs ("CFLs"), in addition to generating higher levels of magnetic fields generally than incandescents, also have a bigger gap between the standard magnetic field measurement and weighted magnetic field measurement, indicating the presence of dirty current.

(5) Dirty Electric Fields
\- The weighted electric fields measurement on the TF2 uses a formula under which from frequencies 60 Hz to 500 Hz, sensitivity increases proportionally to frequency, while at frequencies above 1 kHz, sensitivity decreases proportionally to frequency. The implication here would appear to be that electric fields with frequencies between 500 Hz and 1,000 Hz (1 kHz) are more harmful to human health than frequencies above or below these levels, and while I'm not sure of the accuracy of that notion, comparing unweighted and weighted electric field measurements can help you get some idea of the dirtiness of a particular source of voltage (voltage produces electric fields).

### TB14 — 2025-07-26T23:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-357014>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=356982)
>
> You can also use a portable oscilloscope.

Any reccs for a relatively affordable oscilloscope set-up for measuring DE? My understanding is you need the following:

(i) The Oscilloscope itself;
(ii) An attenuator of 10x or greater to read the line voltage of 120 VAC (since the oscilloscopes are only generally capable of handling a max of +/- 20 V);
(iii) A 60Hz filter;
(iv) A differential probe with which to measure voltage (some appear to have the attenuator built in);
(v) A current clamp to measure current

Anything I'm missing?

Thinking about the following set-up:

\- **Picoscope 2208B**:
Bandwith: 100MHz
Sample rate: 1 GS/s
Memory: 128 ms
Channels: 2
Is there any reason why 4 channels would be much superior to 2? In theory it seems like it could potentially be useful in order to simultaneously monitor some combination of both hot and neutral, and/or both phases, and/or both voltage and current, but is there anything you can really determine with 4 channels that you couldn't catch with 2? There's a model of Picoscope (the 2408B) that seems nearly identical to the 2208B except that it has 4 channels instead of 2, but it's over $500 more expensive.

\- **Graham-Stetzer Ubiquitous Filter**:
Filters out: 60Hz

\- **Hantek HT-8100 (Voltage Differential Probe)**:
Attenuation: Yes, 50:1 or 500:1, which I think should be over the 10:1 necessary for the Picoscope to be able to handle the 120VAC
Am I correct that because of this attenuation by the voltage differential probe, I wouldn't need a separate attenuator for the Picoscope (as long as I was measuring voltage using this probe, not current)?
Bandwidth: 100MHz
Max Waveform Capture Rate: 30,000 wfm/s

\- **Fluke i1000s (Current Clamp)**
Maximum Voltage: 600 VAC
Usable Frequency: 5 Hz - 100 kHz
This is a more limited range than the voltage differential probe and oscilloscope - perhaps more research needed into the availability of current clamps with a higher maximum of its frequency range
Am I correct that because this clamp doesn't mention attenuation, an attenuator would be necessary when taking measurements using this clamp?

\- **Hantek HT-201 (Attenuator)**
Attenuation: 10:1 - should be sufficient for getting 120VAC down to below 20VAC
Bandwidth: 10 MHz
10MHz is beneath the bandwidth of the Picoscope 2208B (oscilloscope) and the Hantek HT-8100 (voltage differential probe), but I don't think it would be necessary when measuring those, only when using the current clamp, which has a max usable frequency of 100 kHz, so I don't think the 10 MHz bandwidth of the HT-201 should pose any limitations on the set-up since it would only be used with the current clamp with an even lower max frequency detected - does that seem correct?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-09T05:11:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-357850>

The easiest way to reduce RF-EMF exposure + reduce environmental stress… Put a faraday bag/beanie and aluminum foil over your Wi-Fi router. 20-30 fold reduction in emission on the spot

### Mat.I — 2025-08-09T06:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-357858>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=357850)
>
> The easiest way to reduce RF-EMF exposure + reduce environmental stress… Put a faraday bag/beanie and aluminum foil over your Wi-Fi router. 20-30 fold reduction in emission on the spot

Hopefully, no one connects to the network & has wifi off on all devices (while shielded) due to the adaptive power control. As they can be worse off dynamically vs static conditions.. Your 20-30 fold (under static conditions) reduction can actually result in a net increase of total RF exposure (with greater packet re-transmissions & power ramp up at router + local device) if not managed correctly. For that reason IMO this is not a good idea. Just get rid of the wifi all together.. It is not needed and there is no reason for it period.. Not to mention the fire risk (void your house insurance policy) as this would be further driven by the adaptive power control ramp up (to full transmit power to bridge the low signal).

### TB14 — 2025-08-14T04:45:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-notes-suggestions-sought.31146/post-358201>

Ethernet-only at both places as well. Have been thinking about how best to block the electric fields coming in from on the Ethernet a bit recently. What I currently have that I'm not entirely satisfied is a powerbank-powered fiber optic buffer - the Ethernet from the router runs to a Ethernet-to-fiber-optic converter, then there's a short run (like three inches) of fiber optic cable, then a fiber-optic-to-Ethernet converter that's powered by a DC powerbank, and from there the Ethernet cable runs to the computer. The fiber optic cable disrupts the flow of AC electricity from the grid powering the modem, router, and first Ethernet-to-fiber-optic converter because it's made of glass (communicates using IR at 850nm) rather than copper like Ethernet cables. I have to change out the powerbank every morning before getting online. I know some people just ground their Ethernet but my DC house's grounding system is dirty (another area of recent focus), and I have to wonder how full-proof grounding it is even if I were to ground to the outside. I think totally interrupting the AC coming to the laptop by using the fiber optic buffer is likely better in theory.

The problem I'm now grappling with on that though is that although the battery pack is DC, it has a SMPS in it, specifically a boost converter to take the 3.7V and ramp it up to a steady 5V for the sensitive USB electronics (iPhone, etc.). I'm pretty sure the way it chops up the current is effectively creating AC. There is a measurable impact of plugging in the Ethernet at the laptop - AC electric fields go up from about 7V-8V when not plugged in to Ethernet to about 10V-11V when it is plugged in. That could also be from some internal component of the fiber optic to Ethernet buffer or I guess even from something my Mac is doing when it gets the Ethernet plugged in. But my top guess right now is it's the SMPS in the powerbank. My understanding of the market is that all the powerbanks these days have these boost converters in them. The next thing I could think of was just a 4.8V alkaline battery pack but I did the math and with four AA batteries drawing as much power as my fiber-optic-to-Ethernet converter is, four AA batteries would last me about six hours, which is not gonna cut it. More research to do here.

Many basic ways to address this. Do a quick check first - ground your laptop to a clean earth (designated peg outside) then measure this again with ethernet plugged in.. Just get a spare USB cable confirm continuity with shield to connector (when stripped) and make your own cable to ground. Then post the measurements pre and post. I assume you are not using your laptop plugged in? plus I assume you cannot use your AC ground.
