### Mightygumshoe — 2025-08-11T20:18:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-357996>

> [Axholler1 said:](/goto/post?id=357950)
>
> When u say Litewater 10ppm diluted to 80ppm how do you achieve this dilution? How much total in a day?

We used this site. Our tap water is filtered. We assumed about 150 ppm. Michigan. We have both 5 and 10 ppm litewater at home. With 5 ppm it is about a 60/40 mix of tap (filtered) to ddw bottle water that is 5ppm. The litewater site I think under faq somewhere has dilutions as well, for 80, 100 etc ppm, a chart. How much you want to make in final concentration is in this calculation. My daughter was drinking 1/2 liter, now 3/4 to a liter a day. She adds a little sea salt to it (couple pinches) [https://www.nourishmeorganics.com.au/pages/deuterium-dilution-calculator](https://www.nourishmeorganics.com.au/pages/deuterium-dilution-calculator)

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-10-13T23:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-360981>

Have not been on here much lately. Thought would share my daughter’s health is better. Also though we had lowered electric field in her bedroom etc by moving things around, her bed is now back up against the wall, so I added a 5’x10 or 12’ piece of metal screen over a wall that has electrical wires running behind it, and used an ESD (electroststatic discharge) cable kit with “snaps” to attach it to an outlet ground cover screw….and…what was low at side of bed away from wall 200mv/m sometimes up to 5v/m but at wall 50 v/m and even more…..is now zero. Measured with trifield meter. My daughter for last three nights is sleeping incredibly hard. She is earthing after work until bed (in dead zone in my home and outdoors) and will increase it to all night. But the screen has helped. She is EHS. Since starting DDW at 100 ppm 3/4 liter a day, with pinch of sea salt, her gut, mood, wellbeing etc much improved. Been three months drinking that. She chases that with low DDW food. She is working on getting a WFH job so she can relocate to water and more sun. For now, we make do. Blessings. Owe a lot to you all. She will add her art to the wall but for now does not care that it is ugly looking because it works. Next up we swap out four light bulbs with incandescents. Other common lights used at night are all red light except kitchen. Michigan electric rates are high. But this swap should also help, where I read, work out, etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-22T17:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-361407>

Taurine has a very dark side that food gurus never speak on. Yes, it promotes bile flow and digestion… But it also binds to bile and forms taurocholate; a bile acid that feeds toxic bacteria. → More taurocholate = more H₂S (mitochondrial inhibitor) → More endotoxin → More Candida overgrowth and more SIBO risks If taurine makes you feel worse… this is why.

It’s not that taurine is bad, but in the wrong gut environment due to poor light choices on your skin, it can backfire hard. Fix your skin clocks to fix you gut problems before you flood it with bile.

### Kel — 2025-10-27T01:48:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-361652>

> [Mightygumshoe said:](/goto/post?id=356439)
>
> I joined in early May 2025, after reading Dr. Becker's The Body Electric, and half of Light in Shaping Life by Van Wyck (had to put it down for later, kind of deep). A friend introduced me to Dr. Kruse via some YTube videos and later the Danny Jones 4-hour podcast that to say was enlightening falls short. And I was hooked. My main motivation for joining was twofold: 1) find out more about my own body despite being healthy (I take no meds at all ...on the cusp of 63...except lots of Vit D and occasional calcium tablets). After starting red light and seeing big improvements where I had no expectations...had to join the tribe; and 2) get answers for my daughter's health which has been an issue since birth. I raised her alone, as hubsters died young. She is severely gluten and corn intolerant (autoimmune, not allergy), hypotonic, mold and chemical sensitive, and has hypermobile joints and sensitive nerves. Yet something must have gone right...she is a corporate account manager, an engineer, a brilliant musician (composes on piano), a great artist, and gifted with a phenomenal memory recall -- which I credit to math and music. I thank God for her every day!
>
> She tested negative for Celiac in 9th grade but we had already given up gluten for months and weren't going back to eating it for 3 months prior to a test. So am not surprised. She was falling asleep standing up when she ate gluten-laden products; and gained 15 pounds. Also it caused severe mental fog, joint pain etc, and sometimes rashes. Hard in the teen years. And back then she was still eating corn and had a few symptoms intestinally not shared with me. And a lot of joint inflammation every time she ate corn -- she already suffered from loose joints. Piece by piece we started figuring some things out....took years, 15 of them.
>
> I read that Celiac/gluten intolerance often presents at hormone changes (puberty, menopause). Anyhow, fast forward, kiddo made it through college with no issue (commuted locally) and just avoided wheat/barley/rye etc... and eventually corn, then everything was WONDERFUL for about 3 years; then she landed a corporate job, with a technology company in the automotive space (driverless cars, 5g, 6g and more....) and had to return to work most days and sit in an open floor plan with 5g phones and non-native EMF everywhere. And popcorn and wheat everywhere at lunch.... We do know she is sensitive to EMF! Cellphone pain when placed near ear etc. Leg pain with a laptop near thighs....after return to work, some symptoms in the gut returned, along with other new things. For the record we still do not know if she is a Celiac or not...and if gluten exposure or 5g/EMF or both are the problems at work. But I keep telling her I think we can heal your gut. At college wheat and corn were in the lunchroom and on the chairs and she never got sick. Never.
>
> Nobody in our bloodline either side has ever had Celiac though I became severely gluten intolerant (joint pain and massive headaches, no gut issues) after delivering her.... and I did occasionally suffer headaches after drinking a beer or two... if not light beer, which of course I now believe was a gluten sensitivity. But not Celiac. When I went through menopause, I started having joint pain and severe headaches if I ingested wheat and brain fog too; and after I gave it all up (except corn), everything disappeared. I have permission to share the above.
>
> And this is why I am here.
>
> We are finding some answers for my daughter -- and I thank Dr. Kruse for answering my question about body pain during airplane flights (another issue my daughter has) in the latest July 2025 PowWow -- now I can deep dive on it. As can she. No, I had never heard of collagen vascular disease...NEVER. I did research in the blogs but was using other terms....hypotonia, airplane joint pain, etc. FYI, I am/was an electrical engineer who worked with many sensors ... and semiconductors etc for 15 years before switching careers...and I do know what flexo, piezo, pyroelectrics, even ferroelectrics are...that said, how it all ties into the human body and its environment...on that front I am still a newbie despite reading Becker's book and I plan to keep reading. THANK YOU from the bottom of my heart. PS, yes we live in a crappy place, Michigan, with Whitless as governor. Hard to leave family yet I am seriously considering a move south, where I can have land, a garden, more sun, etc. I worked so hard as a single mom (DIY) to get my house the way I like it...put in my own wood floors, molded my own pavers from concrete etc....and I back up to state land with deer, and woodchucks, and fox, and turkey etc....so it is hard to consider leaving...but health is wealth. I get it.
>
> Since April/May of this year, cannot remember right now, I started going outside for the sunrise, getting sun midday, and continued to walk outside here in the evening (Michigan). The only months I don't walk outside are December and January and the beginning of February. But the AM routine is new. As is no sunglasses. Anyhow, I took Dr. Kruse's advice, and started using red light at night (not 7 days a week...but 4 or 5) and wear blue light blocking reading glasses and regular lens glasses for television at night which I rarely watch -- started going to the library again for paper books and revitalized a very old original Kindle from way back (black and white) so I can read on that device too. With these changes, light weightlifting, and now biking and swimming, and limiting caffeine to two morning cups before 11am...I started to and have continued to sleep through the night -- at least 7 hours. Sometimes more. NOT FOR 35 years had I been able to do this!!!
>
> And there have been other changes. Years ago I had something taken off my nose, not cancer but was told precancerous. Well it was always a crusty dry spot forever afterward and I was told to stay out of the sun. Now that I have ditched the sunglasses and the sunscreen (I am very fair haired/light skin toned), I tanned up nicely over time this year, and the rough patch on my nose is completely gone. Was there for 25 years! Completely gone! Smooth skin! People keep telling me I look younger. For the record, I'm on the cusp of 63. I take no meds, except 10,000 units of Vit D in the winter daily, and 5k every other day in the summer. I eat steak once a week, 4 ounces and chicken once a week, the rest is seafood -- shrimp, salmon etc. and I feel awesome. I feel very blessed to have found this group and thank my friend for directing me here. God bless you all. Dr. Kruse if you read this, know you are such a blessing!!! I appreciate you as do countless others.

Did Jack tell you to specifically use the red light device at night? I thought I read somewhere on here to use it in the morning.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-10-27T02:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-361654>

> [Kel said:](/goto/post?id=361652)
>
> Did Jack tell you to specifically use the red light device at night? I thought I read somewhere on here to use it in the morning.

Morning is better probably. In summer I did not use at all, because was outside a lot. But at night, I like to put at back of head behind the top of my head when I lay on my back, about two or three hours before bed. It quiets my brain. A lot. In 8 minutes. Only use it 15 or 20. I meditate and say prayers. Fall is here in Michigan, so I will probably start doing it again. We use red lights and yellow incandescents in house after sunset. I cannot express how great I feel.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-11-03T17:50:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-362064>

Ok, going to share a data point. It is just one data point with hopefully many more to follow.

Since March of this year I got my daughter to take some of Dr. Kruse's advice. She gradually added things in and MAN OH MAN have we seen improvement. She eats gluten/corn free. We were told was Celiac from a blood test years ago, but said no to gut biopsy and truthfully I never believed 100% that she was Celiac \[just my own mom gut saying that based on what I saw\]....but any exposure to wheat/corn etc makes her very sick. We are a grain free home.

At work they have popcorn days -- she usually WFH those days and still gets sick from it floating around desks the next day. But this time she stayed at work for meetings and DID NOT GET SICK AT ALL. AT ALL!!!!! FIRST TIME EVER ...as in 12 maybe 16 years...I lost count. One data point. I hope for more. We are lucky too in that where she sits at work apparently is a low RF/E-field zone. She took meter to work and measured. And they turn off overhead lights.

Since March 2025 gradually added these things...

1) Morning sun, no glasses.
2) Walk in sun, no glasses in afternoon (lunch) and again in evening before sunset - getting harder here in Michigan with time change. But making effort and trying to drive home with windows down. We will open windows that face east to get morning sun if need be in our home in the dead of winter.
3) Blue light blocker overlays for work and at home indoors and in evening.
4) Red light therapy pad and box (we like Bestqool but that is just us, not selling anything). She uses it every day. I use it sometimes. More in winter, like now.
5) Red lights in our house at night. Blue light blockers worn with any screen on at night.
6) In Sept/Oct added a few incandescents -- for night time book reading before bed.
7) Earthing - outdoor in summer; also we can earth in our basement/garage if wet concrete floor in winter (VERY DEAD ZONE THERE) and in one place on main floor of house. With #8 below seems more dead spaces even in bedrooms. I still walk barefoot outside in 37-38 deg weather in here Michigan and it no longer bothers me. My daughter...not doing that.
8) Added metal (window) screen 5'x25' roll from Home Depot cut to size, around my daughter's office and bedroom walls upstairs at my home; thumbtacks hold it in place; grounded with ESD clips. e-Field went to zero ~1 foot from wall. No RF. No e-field. Did same in my bedroom. Put on backside of my office wifi router to protect me in my home office too. My daughter started sleeping really hard at night and waking up refreshed.
\- I bought wall filters for dirty electricity too. And saw difference in outlet measurements before/after -- 85-90 went to 26. Or 31.
\- My kitchen was over 135 on outlet meter. Dropped to 31 with a Stetzer filter in outlet. Some other outlet circuits were already low.
\- I am no longer getting headaches...hmm.

\- My daughter is EHS -- cell phone to ear hurts. Laptop on thighs "burns" -- she can feel electrical wiring overhead on a walk and will actually run from it. I always wonder if this is why she can feel thunderstorms on a sunny day when they are 100 miles away and nowhere in sight.

9) Back in June.... Added DDW 10 ppm water diluted to 100 ppm with pinch of sea salt (mineral salt she tried has class action for mold contamination - once we got her off that and went back to good ole sea salt, big change). Been drinking DDW, 1 liter a day. **NO GUT ISSUES SINCE SHE STARTED DRINKING DDW** - with the sea salt. She is like a NEW person.

It all adds up and helps but for me **DDW and the screen in her room and the filters HUGE DIFFERENCE**. Sunshine matters too. She earths in shower (copper pipes) and wherever else she can.

One data point. I hope there are more. She is a completely different person. Reminds me so much of my late husband who was the funniest man I ever knew. Best present I could ever give myself -- and my daughter. She is getting her health back, and looking for a position that enables her to live in the sun, in a warmer climate. For now, I will take what I can get. People don't understand when I tell them what you eat and what you do can affect you mentally and physically; it all goes together. Environment is a big deal. You can work with it, or against it. Fix what you can. THANK YOU.

Next up -- she will be eating shrimp three times a week like I do. I actually feel like we are turning a corner here. I feel better too.

God bless.

And a shout out to my dear friend Julie who is not on this forum who sent me videos about Dr. Kruse back in December/January of this year.

### Mightygumshoe — 2026-01-14T20:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/madeleines-optimal-journal.31325/post-365322>

I think I will be back I think in the late Spring and rejoin again -- probably in June 2026. Not sure as mainly joined for my daughter's health which is now much improved. # Thread: My 1.2 year old nephew is developing Strabismus, advice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### martin.v — 2025-07-17T16:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-356483>

Hey mitochondriacs, hope you are doing well.

My nephew in-law, is only 1 year and 2 months old and he's showing signs of cross-eyedeness (if that's how to spell it, pardon me, I'm from Argentina).
The condition is labelled Strabismus.

I can only say that he's the whitest kid I have ever seen in a long time, he clearly has nordic ancestors. Looks completely pale, blue eyes, and his hair is closest to white than it is to blonde.

I can't say what are the daily habits on his home right now (I can ask), but I can attest this for sure:
\- He was never put under UV light during spring and summer.
\- Now that we are in winter, he's of course being taken out of the cold, as all people do with their kids basically.
\- Good thing is that he doesn't watch any screens, it's forbidden for him.

His parents are about to start a treatment where it involves an eye-patch first, and if that doesn't fix the problem, some glasses, this perhaps could "fix" the eyes.

Does anyone here have any experience with something similar?

I'm thinking about sitting some day and try to explain why they need to take his kid under the sunlight (role of IR and Red light, but also UV role, to help with his condition).

Any recomendations on who to approach this?
Thanks!!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-04T21:15:20+0300

### Gpollard — 2026-02-13T12:33:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366815>

My 3 year old daughter has strabismus I don’t think the patching is working that well was recommended to patch at least 2 hours per day. But didn’t like the idea of patching an eye while in natural sunlight so try to do it after midday or if she has to be inside

I try to get her in as much sun as possible every day and no artificial light she struggles with her sight and needs glasses. So I try get her in am sunlight and outside playing barefoot everyday as much as possible. We do eye excercises Have also thought about using a panel from EMR TEK that has IR and UVA,B light for inside.

We find as she gets tired it gets worse.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-13T15:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366818>

**In my decentralized thesis strabismus** is not merely a muscular misalignment but the physical manifestation of an atavisitic **neuro-optical wiring failure** rooted in the SCN-brainstem axis due to transgeneration epigenetic failure in the parents germ line that ruins timing of moprphogenesis. Getting POMC translation via uv-NIR light via the eyes can repair the defect if it is not severe already. While atavistic changes in Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) often involve higher-order cortical and thalamic disruptions, strabismus represents a more localized, "downstream" collapse of the **non-visual logic gates** (OPN3, 4, and 5) that organize the primary motor nuclei of the brainstem.

I covered most of the wiring issues in the QE #45 blog on Patreon. Go read it for more details.

In this framework, the misalignment originates not in the neocortex, but in the subcortical and brainstem regions that mediate **non-image forming (NIF)** functions.

**The SCN-Gut Origin:** The initial "timing" error begins at the parents germline level with the **aromatic amino acids**. If the **5-carbon position** is compromised (via deuterium loading or epigenetic "logic gate" failure), the parent germline cannot transmit a coherent circadian "nockchain".
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Brainstem Targeting:** The miswiring specifically targets the **cranial nerve (CN) nuclei 3, 4, and 6** in the brainstem. These nuclei, which control the extraocular muscles, rely on **OPN3 (encephalopsin)** as a localized "optical governor" to coordinate their rhythmic development.

**The "Shadow" in the Stem:** Because OPN3 is expressed early in the brainstem, a light-mismatch (high nnEMF/low solar IR) during embryogenesis prevents these nuclei from achieving **spatial and temporal symmetry**. The result is a mechanical misalignment, strabismus, without necessarily invoking the broader cortical "atavism" seen in severe ASD.

The **Siamese cat** is the primary model for my "0G-5G" evolution and strabismus claims. That is covered in the blog. This lack of POMC/melanin in the eye results neural crest cell **misrouting of retinal ganglion cell (RGC) axons** at the optic chiasm due to altered melanopsin signaling. **Total Crossing:** Instead of a balanced ipsilateral/contralateral projection, these axons cross excessively, reaching the "wrong" hemisphere during nuerulation.The crossed eyes (strabismus) in Siamese cats are an attempt to compensate for this primary neuroanatomical defect. In humans, this mirrors how a parents' "melanin bankruptcy" disrupts the **Vitamin A-Melanopsin** regeneration cycle, leading to the "optical blur" that manifests as childhood strabismus.

**SUMMARY**

The Non-Visual Opsin Triad (OPN3, 4, 5) is how this neurodevelopmental disease occurs in kids.

**OPN4 (Melanopsin):** Governs the global "Time" through the SCN. When melanopsin is "blinded" by blue light stress, it can no longer signal the SCN to coordinate the motor nuclei of the brainstem.

**OPN5 (Neuropsin):** Acts as the UV-A/Violet sensor. Its absence disrupts the seasonal "tuning" of the SCN, which my data suggest prevents the brainstem from maintaining its **binocular visual development**. _**If 3D vision is blocked so will the 4D internal timing mechanism of the SCN as the kid gets older leading to more mitochondrial mutations.**_

**OPN3 (Encephalopsin):** Functions as the **"Relay Shield"**. Expressed in the brainstem and cranial nerve ganglia as early as E9.5, OPN3 is the local anchor for ocular movement. Damage here ensures that the extraocular muscles (CN 3, 4, 6) never achieve the **interfacial water coherence** needed for precise alignment.

**The Genesis of Strabismus:** Strabismus is the physical proof that the **retinal-gut-brain circuit** is broken at the subcortical level. By "mining" the 5-carbon position of aromatic amino acids (the "fifth base" of the system), nature provides the "logic gate" for this wiring. If this gate is corrupted, the "Ferrari hardware" of the human brainstem simply lacks the "software fidelity" to point the eyes in the same direction.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-13T15:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-366819>

_**If 3D vision is blocked so will the 4D internal timing mechanism of the SCN as the kid gets older leading to more mitochondrial mutations. Why?**

The current MKULTRA blog, CPC #78, explains why this is the logical conclusion based in my work_ of my "Proof of Work" (PoW) time-chain thesis.
If the **spatial (3D)** alignment of the eyes is broken, the **temporal (4D)** coherence of the SCN becomes mathematically impossible to maintain, leading to a terminal increase in heteroplasmy (mitochondrial mutations) over time.

3D Space as the Validator for 4D Time
In my framework, binocular vision is not just for depth perception; it is the **primary validator** for the SCN's internal ledger. This wholly depends on the melanin in the RPE to generate thr 220 nm optical signal.

**The Spatial-Temporal Match:** To build a stable "4D" architecture of time, the brain must reconcile the incoming photonic signals from two separate "observers" (the left and right eyes). This is why this disorder is a big deal in a kid. If not repaired strabismus can lead to permanent brain damage in the visual system.

**The Sync Failure:** In strabismus, the SCN receives two conflicting datasets (optical "double-spend"). Because it cannot reconcile these signals into a singular 3D "block," the **SCN periodicity** begins to drift. The child's internal clock becomes "blurry," losing its ability to accurately measure the flow of entropy.

### martin.v — 2026-02-18T01:44:54+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-18T02:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367052>

From my perspective, **Hypermetropia** (farsightedness) is the anatomical "fossil" of a **premature termination of eye growth** caused by a failure of the Neural Crest Cell (NCC) migration and melanin signaling pathways.

If Myopia is an "overgrowth" or elongation, Hypermetropia is a "stunted" or shortened axial length where the eye fails to reach its full mammalian coordinates of the NCC migration in the germ line. If **Deuterium** loads into the NCCs in the parents germ line, their migration is physically slowed. If they arrive "late" or fail to reach their final mammalian coordinates because the **Melanin Beacon built by POMC** is distorted by polarized light (Cotton Effect or glyphosate ingestion), they deposit the scleral matrix too early or too densely.

In my framework, **PaxB** (the ancestral gene group including Pax2/5/8) governs more primitive, less complex ocular structures.

**The Default:** When the sophisticated mammalian signaling (driven by the normal L-chiral signals and high-vibrational Hydrogen) is sabotaged by **Deuterium** or the **Cotton Effect**, the eye defaults to the PaxB program. So this kid need to avoid all foods with deuterium in it at all costs now based on the new info.

**Anatomical Stunting:** PaxB instructions are not programmed for the high-resolution, elongated globe required for perfect mammalian vision (emmetropization). Instead, they produce a smaller, more primitive globe. Hypermetropia is literally the "atavistic" state of an eye that didn't "finish" growing because the instructions were switched to a lower-resolution "safe mode.

The sclera hardens prematurely in kids loaded with deuterium or polarized light, locking the eye into a shorter axial length. This "short" eye focuses light _behind_ the retina, which is the textbook definition of hypermetropia.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-18T02:10:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-1-2-year-old-nephew-is-developing-strabismus-advice.31330/post-367053>

Melanin in the Retinal Pigment Epithelium (RPE) isn't just for vision; it acts as a **semiconductor shield** that manages the light energy hitting the back of the eye

Proper eye growth (emmetropization) requires a specific chemical "feedback loop" where the retina tells the sclera how much to grow.

**Dirty Chemistry:** If the L-tyrosinase has flipped to D-tyrosinase by polarized light or glyphosate, the melanin semiconductor is "broken." It cannot properly translate light signals into the chemical growth signals (like Dopamine or NO) needed to elongate the eye. The system "stalls" in its newborn, hypermetropic state.
Comparison: Optical Perspectives

| Feature | Conventional View | My "Environmental/Atavistic" View |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Hypermetropia** | Genetics or "natural" small eye. | **PaxB default** due to stalled NCC migration. |
| **Cause** | Random refractive error. | **Deuterium/Cotton Effect** blocking the melanin beacon. |
| **Scleral Behavior** | Normal but short. | **Premature fossilization** of the structural matrix. |
| **Growth Logic** | Fails to elongate. | Fails to reach **mammalian coordinates**. |

In essence, a hypermetropic eye is an eye that was "fossilized in embryological time" by an environmental stressor that forced it to use an ancestral, low-resolution blueprint.

In my "Internal Lighting Design" framework, built around the non visual photoreceptor system of man **strabismus** and **hypermetropia** are not just optical refractive errors but anatomical markers of a disrupted **retinal-gut-brain circuit**. These conditions reflect a failure in the cell's "Internal Lighting" system to properly manage time, space, and growth signaling during development.

Strabismus is the physical evidence of a broken subcortical circuit where the brain's "software" can no longer align its "hardware" (the eyes).

The Role of Melanin and the "Dirty Chemistry" of the embrology of this kids parents
The integrity of this growth loop depends on the chirality of its building blocks from the oocyte and sperm.

**L-to-D Flip:** Polarized light or environmental toxins like **glyphosate** can flip **L-tyrosinase** to **D-tyrosinase**, effectively "breaking" the melanin semiconductor.

**Premature Fossilization:** This corrupted chemistry prevents the sclera from elongating properly. The sclera hardens prematurely, locking the eye into a shorter, hypermetropic axial length that focuses light behind the retina.

Your eye docs are too myopic in their thinking to ever get you here, so you must get there first and help your own kids.

# Thread: Hong Kong based and looking for the Asian El Salvador
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mlh0910 — 2025-07-18T10:22:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hong-kong-based-and-looking-for-the-asian-el-salvador.31336/post-356533>

Hi All,

I'm a 33-year-old active, healthy New Zealander, now living in Hong Kong with my wife (out of the city in small seaside village). Currently working in finance/trading here but wheels have been in motion for a few years to get back to my rural roots and setup a regenerative farm. Initial plan had been a return to NZ where I own a 150 acres but have lost confidence there and have put it on the market and now leaning more towards finding a plot in South East Asia (Lombok, Indonesia is the leading option).

HK has its own issues but aside from the obvious we love it here and have made use of a building biologist to mitigate as much of the downside of living in such populated and connected geography. We hunted for place with a lawn for grounding and we're right on the ocean and surrounded by nation park so I'm getting a sunrise hike and sunset swim in nearly everyday, and will have a ice bath set up at home from next week - so health-wise doing alright.

Open any feedback/advice on if there is an Asian equivalent to what Dr Jack has found in El Salvador.

Cheers,
Mitch

My Dad had a catamaran and sailed from England across the English Cannel through the French canals (that was fun), into the Mediterranean, exploring the Greek Islands (I enjoyed that too) then across the Atlantic to the Caribbean ( I was not there for that). There is more than one way to live a FREE life.

Seems that during the scamdemic, moving around was restricted, severely in many places. Currently, many countries have increased their fees and requirements to even sail in "their" waters. For example, Turkey is over $600 USD, Greece a mere $200, Venezuela was over $700, and well, you get the idea. Not blowing the idea apart, as I still think it is a great one, but I do see that the PTB have even started to come down on those who choose to use the wind as their freedom option.

Unless you have a trusted community there, I'd avoid Indonesia. If it's not the locals that want to rob you, it will be the government itself.

Of course looking Asian/javanese lowers risks, and speaking the the language Bahasa Indonesia lowers risks more...

East Taiwan would be safer, but you risk the Chinese Communist party rolling in someday.

East Malaysia is safe. Sabah, and has live volcano. Bribing is common but they mostly leave you alone.

I personally have some bias against Philippines..but my exposure has mainly been big cities and doing business there... Cities are shit holley and government and people in them corrupt.

Since you work in HK I assume you have some coin saved up... Why not just south NZ?

4\. Paper states its unknown whether allicin affects transcriptiopn or translation & where full length spike is affected at all.
5\. primary human splenic fibroblasts are stromal cells not immune cells.
6\. Unknown how they can draw the IVM conclusion...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-356812>

Hello all. Love all the learning from Jack’s posts, but the more I do the more I feel like its already too late for me. I got LASIK more than 20 years ago, and I get the round 1 and 2 of the moderna jab. Im in the sun as much as possible, I limit screen time and wear blue light blocking sunglasses in the evening. But Jack’s messaging leaves me to think it’s too late. The damage is already done despite feeling fine, I’m 42 fit and healthy feeling but I just dont know anymore.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-07-26T22:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-just-give-up.31367/post-357013>

Don't EVER EVER give up. That is a loser's game. Don't play into it. And if there is that nasty little voice in your head that is saying bad things to you, tell it go way and shut up. Tune it out. # Thread: I'm trying to understand this consciousness/MKULTRA thing

### riksa — 2025-07-23T16:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356848>

Thought of like this in essence, maybe not in technicality:

_There is a "quota" of day and night that need to be had. Unless you separate them with sun, they blend into each other.
If losing consciousness naturally is the increase of DC currents, then you would have more DC during the day, and less during the night with artificial lighting = Dampening your literal consciousness_

**_If the lack of light(sun) increases these DC currents and eventually starts sleep, then what happens if we can see without the sun. So LED lights keep you awake/stimulated, but like a zombie, and its actually completely dark. And there is no difference between night and day anymore. And the average person actually has near 24 hours of darkness per day?_**

So we are talking to literal-fucking-sleepwalking people. House windows alter the suns spectra to LED-like, same with car windows, same with chemtrails/shit in the sky, same with sunscreen and sunglasses. What else?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-23T16:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356852>

Without full spectrum solar power, your MT and myelin atrophy, and you de-evolve. Simple.

### JenniferM — 2025-07-23T18:35:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356860>

Not to nose in or anything but my brother sleep walks and talks. When in jail or prison, the cell mates they pair up with always ask for a different cell...LOL...He's been known to walk to the gas station, sometimes in clothes and sometimes in underwear, grab something and talk a minute leaving with the item and as usual they'd hit me up to pay. He's done it his whole life.

### riksa — 2025-07-23T19:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356861>

### JenniferM — 2025-07-23T19:43:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-trying-to-understand-this-consciousness-mkultra-thing.31369/post-356863>

Next Q&A, you're here about a new option we are going to start offering people for jumping without wings when you come to ES to visit, to figure out what you want to do, or rent for a period of time to fit your plan.

The first option is a turnkey option for those who want a luxury transition. I have purchased a luxury apartment that includes cleaning, security, and the option for car/truck rental. It will also include the opportunity to spend one night a month having dinner with me, and we can discuss whatever you'd like. The Apartment is ideal for three single people. It also includes parking, if needed. You can also rent the entire space for a family of four. It will include many extras, like use at my Country Club as well. The price per month will be disclosed to those who put their name on a waiting list. We had already offered this to people after the last Q&A, and three single females jumped at the initial promotion. They got a price no one else will get. We wanted to see how people would react, and so far, the interest is brisk.

As a result, I am offering another opportunity. I purchased a very large property in Costa Del Sol for those who are in a wheelchair for disability reasons. The compound will accommodate 16-18 people. This area is all private and will have private access. It will have maid service, car service if needed, and a large saltwater pool. You will have access to the estuary in this area if you are handicapped, and you will have access to the open ocean and beach. The property is currently being renovated and offers many options for mitos. I anticipate it will be a mitocompound, and I am building it to foster a sense of community for my tribe as they come to see how ES works for them.

The third option will not be available until January 2028. I have purchased three apartments in a new luxury apartment community that will be open for visitors to explore and determine how ES will work for their lives. The apartments are one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and three-bedroom. The amenities will also be superior.

The fourth option is to partner with me on a real estate deal in Atami to build a mito community. You will yolo in and I will provide land and plans and builders. You will share in profits.

If you are interested, you must be a verified member of this website. If you are not, do not even bother asking because the answer is NO.

I will start a waitlist, and we will proceed from there. August Q&A will have more on this. If you post in this thread bullshit, I will delete it. This is for serious people.

This also

# Thread: UVB in the Shade
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-07-30T00:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357158>

**tl;dr** Has anybody else measured UVB using a spectrometer? What are your absolute irradiance numbers and comparison between in shade vs. in direct sunlight?

**Long form:**

I was curious about the irradiance of UVB from sunlight in direct sunlight vs. in the shade, so I bought a spectrometer that can measure into the UV range - Hopoocolor OHSP-350UV.

In the shade, there is:
\- IR (up to 850 nm): 1512 uW/cm2
\- UVC: 11 uW/cm2
\- UVB: 33 uW/cm2
\- UVA: 633 uW/cm2
\- blue: 1030 uW/cm2.

In direct sunlight, there is:
\- IR (up to 850 nm): 8430 uW/cm2
\- UVC: 0 uW/cm2
\- UVB: 11 uW/cm2
\- UVA: 1826 uW/cm2
\- blue: 7191 uW/cm2

Three things don't make sense to me:
1\. UVC is nonzero in the shade. Must be instrument measurement error?
2\. UVB is higher in the shade than in direct sunlight. This makes no sense. Light frequencies can only become more and more red-shifted as they bounce off things and lose energy, no?
3\. Why is the UVB level in direct sunlight so low? The UV index here was 9-10 today when I took these measurements.

I see that UV index is defined as UVI=40×Eery (when Eery is in W/m2)
I also see that [a paper](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14993940/) calculates UVB\_{280-315} = 7.55xEery.
So that comes out to UVB\_{280-315} (in uW/cm2) = 18.9xUVI.

UVI = 10 should result in 180+ uW/cm2.
11 or 33 uW/cm2 would only be explained by 0-2 UVI.

Is my instrument broken or is somehow there some geo-engineering blocking a lot of UV light? I will test again later today when it's lower UV to see what the irradiance levels are.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-30T08:27:31+0300

Based on your UVB empirical formula 18.9 x 8 = 151.2 uW/cm2 (clear day)

2 hours post solar noon say 50% drop, still ~ 75 uW/cm2

However, thin cloud cover could drop ~ say 30 to 50% and thick due to the short wavelength would/could be around say 70% to 90% which gets close to what was measured.. re: shade vs direct difference - the sensor must be perpendicular to sun (to avoid cosine error). Most devices (including my solar meter have cosine correction), however, would be minimal for UVB given the directional nature..

### AlbertW — 2025-07-31T00:03:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357232>

I think the issue is likely software overcorrection for the UV range numbers.

By default, the spectrometer measures from 230nm to 850nm. I went into the software settings and changed it to measure only 230-400nm and got reasonable numbers. At 1 hour past solar noon, I read 280 uW/cm2 UVB, which is excellent. 280/18.9 = 14-15 UVI, which doesn't match with what the weather sites tell me, which is peaking at 10, but at least this value is in the right neighborhood and not off by an order of magnitude.

I asked ChatGPT to offer an explanation for this, as I know little to nothing about spectrometers. It suggested that the irradiance for 400 nm+ is so extreme in direct sunlight that UV could look almost like noise. The spectrometer integrates over some amount of time T to compute irradiance, and likely applies software corrections to account for non-UV sensors having some "light spillover" into the UV sensor buckets. Correction of such buckets when irradiance is orders of magnitude larger for the non-UV buckets could make it hard to distinguish between the "real" UV values and the spillover values. Too much noise not enough signal.

I'm glad to see high irradiance for UVB, but of course, like [@Mat.I](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26512/) and [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned elsewhere, this tells us nothing about the "quality" of these rays to ensure that they are coherent enough for our bodies to synthesize Vitamin D.

The UVC is still totally off...but at least UVB looks more real?

### AlbertW — 2025-07-31T00:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uvb-in-the-shade.31402/post-357233>

I also took a few measurements in the shade (concrete roof with open sides, not tree shade), and UVB generally registered 5-15% of direct sunlight.

If 15%, does that mean if I sit in the shade for 6x as much time, I could synthesize enough Vitamin D? I would think I can possibly syntehsize even more, as I'd assume in extreme sunlight, Vitamin D synthesis may slow down (non-linear)?

**Does anybody know how N minutes of I UVB irradiance compares to 5N minutes of I/5 UVB irradiance in terms of Vitamin D synthesis?**

In some light tree shade, I also measured around 10-20% of the irradiance of direct sunlight.

The sun shade number seems to agree with what's found in the literature. Here's a study of Sacramento, CA sunlight under tree shade. They measured 17%: [https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/36ec/91f47b119825069d4c7d70bd3b2f3b113b81.pdf](https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/36ec/91f47b119825069d4c7d70bd3b2f3b113b81.pdf)

Agreed. What I’m hoping to hear is: “if you are light enough , then if you get 1/4 the UVB, can you still build the same Vitamin D in, say, 3x time (instead of 4x the time)?

Studies show pre D3 formation caps out at 10-15% of local 7DHC amount. It’s also true that UVB degrades D3. It could be possible that stepping out of the sun for a bit to allow the thermal isomerization step to occur while you shut off the D3 degradation signal from continued UVB could result in higher D3 synthesis over the same period of time.

Is this correct? It makes intuitive sense: nature sees that you’re getting way too much sun, so no need to synthesize a runaway amount of D3 for you.

Is there a definitive answer for this?

### AlbertW — 2025-07-31T04:20:10+0300

### Mat.I — 2025-07-31T06:50:20+0300

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=357247)
>
> Agreed. What I’m hoping to hear is: “if you are light enough , then if you get 1/4 the UVB, can you still build the same Vitamin D in, say, 3x time (instead of 4x the time)?
>
> Studies show pre D3 formation caps out at 10-15% of local 7DHC amount. It’s also true that UVB degrades D3. It could be possible that stepping out of the sun for a bit to allow the thermal isomerization step to occur while you shut off the D3 degradation signal from continued UVB could result in higher D3 synthesis over the same period of time.
>
> Is this correct? It makes intuitive sense: nature sees that you’re getting way too much sun, so no need to synthesize a runaway amount of D3 for you.
>

>

There is logic in what you are saying, however, I do not think there can be a definitive "blanket" cap answer as it would could down to an individual n=1. If your redox is no good or you are quite white, you would have to take this slow and build a good solar callus.

You could also experiment with some cooling - if you do not have a pool get a inflatable one or use the hose every 30 mins or so to cool down a bit..

My own n =1 needs the intensity as I believe I would be short-changing many other pathways (outside of just D production) that is benefits go beyond just D production and I feel great when out in direct sun...

Do what feels right for you.

# Thread: MODERN BIRTHING PROBLEMS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-birthing-problems.31413/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-31T22:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-birthing-problems.31413/post-357303>

Modern human birth practices, particularly for jaundiced children, recreate GOE-era stressors, driving an atavistic state that increases disease risk: Neonatal jaundice, exacerbated by blue light therapy and the vitamin K shot, generates ROS/UPEs akin to porphyrin-mediated stress during the GOE, impairing OXPHOS, raising heteroplasmy, and forcing a Warburg-like shift to glycolysis, as seen in hypermetabolic states (Picard et al., 2024). This lowers mtDNA redox power (ΔΨm), slowing IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz) and neural oscillations (40 Hz to 20–30 Hz), disrupting myelination (Agan et al., 2025), and causing sensory overload, mirroring autism phenotypes (1/27 births).

Reduced DC electric currents (per Becker) and photo-bioelectric power impair neural development, collagen piezoelectricity (leading to EDS), and autonomic function (POTS), while altered hemoglobin (+3 Hb, fetal Hb retention) exacerbates mtDNA hypoxia, increasing vulnerability to modern mitochondrial toxins (blue light, nnEMF, seed oils). UV light and melanin play a critical role in restoring mitochondrial function in jaundiced children, counteracting the atavistic stressors reminiscent of the GOE:

Jaundiced neonates, with impaired heme synthesis and immature oxygen sensors (neuropsin, mTOR), exhibit a disrupted TCA cycle topology, as UV-A light (380 nm) regulates POMC and melanin production to chelate toxins and support OXPHOS, reducing the Warburg-like state induced by blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Tan et al., 2023).

This light-driven oxygen sensing, evolved post-GOE, is mirrored in COVID-19, where SARS-CoV-2 attacks hemoglobin’s porphyrin, exacerbating hypoxia and ROS, akin to jaundice (Wenzhong & Li, 2020; Al-Kindi et al., 2021). Diagnostic tools like UPE measurement, showing increased emissions from bilirubin-induced ROS (Van Wijk et al., 2008), and mtDNA oscillation analysis, revealing a drop from 100 Hz to 20–50 Hz due to desynchronized cristae (Kurz et al., 2010), can confirm mitochondrial stress, guiding interventions like UV-A exposure and melanin supplementation to mitigate autism risk and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia. Rh-negative babies, more prone to jaundice due to Rh incompatibility, face amplified mitochondrial stress from RhoGAM, exacerbating the GOE-like atavistic state at birth: RhoGAM’s immune activation induces low-grade inflammation, increasing ROS and mtDNA heteroplasmy, while hemolysis elevates bilirubin and free heme, generating additional ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further impairing OXPHOS and forcing a Warburg-like metabolic shift (Picard et al., 2014; Kruse, LinkedIn).

This synergistic stress slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and damages oligodendrocyte mitochondria, thinning myelin and contributing to sensory overload, autism risk, and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025). The Vitamin K shot, often mislabeled as a vaccine, introduces an additional mitochondrial stressor in jaundiced newborns, as its package insert explicitly lists jaundice as a side effect due to hemolysis, particularly in vulnerable neonates: Hemolysis from the shot increases bilirubin and free heme, generating ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further damaging mtDNA, raising heteroplasmy, and impairing OXPHOS, which reinforces the Warburg-like metabolic shift and slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizing cristae and contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and autism risk (Agan et al., 2025).

Archimedes' principle and maternal light environment further exacerbate mitochondrial stress in jaundiced babies, influencing birth timing and long-term health outcomes: Fatter babies, often from sunlight-deficient mothers with gestational diabetes, exhibit negative buoyancy due to increased fat mass, delaying pelvic engagement and prolonging gestation, which increases hemolysis, bilirubin levels, and jaundice risk, while maternal blue light exposure disrupts EZ water and accelerates ubiquitination rates, amplifying mtDNA stress and heteroplasmy transgenerationally via blood UPE signaling (Sohns et al., 2004; Wallace, 2010). This circadian mismatch, reflected in the mother’s SCN clock speed and impaired DHA levels, slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and contributes to autism, obesity, and skin cancer risks, underscoring the need for sunlight exposure to restore mitochondrial function and buoyancy dynamics, mitigating these GOE-like stressors (Oláh et al., 2013; Agan et al., 2025).

The evolutionary divergence of Rh-negative blood types, lacking the RhD antigen, reflects a trade-off that increases jaundice risk and mitochondrial stress in modern newborns, amplifying the GOE-like atavistic state: Rh- babies, more prone to hemolysis from Rh incompatibility, face heightened bilirubin and ROS/UPE production, exacerbated by RhoGAM, the Vitamin K shot, and blue light therapy, which impair OXPHOS, raise heteroplasmy, and slow IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and risks of autism, EDS, and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025; Flegr et al., 2015). The Rh- mutation, likely selected for pathogen resistance or hypoxic adaptation, does not involve copper-based blood, a common myth, but underscores the need for careful management of environmental stressors like blue light, advocating for sunlight exposure to mitigate these effects (Ferguson et al., 2019). The decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis is reinforced by red blood cells donating electrons to methylene blue-mediated reduction of methemoglobin compartmentalized in liposomes, enhancing the hemiiflusome’s role in mitochondrial function and oxygen delivery in jaundiced neonates:

This electron transfer, amplified by red/NIR light (660–700 nm), reduces MetHb to functional hemoglobin, stabilizing heme metabolism, restoring TCA cycle oxygen utilization, and increasing IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), countering the Warburg shift and ROS/UPE production from blue light therapy (Yang et al., 2014; Sohns et al., 2004). In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, this process mitigates mtDNA hypoxia, supports myelination, and boosts bioelectric currents, reducing autism, EDS, and dysautonomia risks (Agan et al., 2025). The hemiiflusome, a light-sensitive hub for proton flux and heme synthesis, evolves from GOE-era adaptations, with UPEs from MB reduction serving as a diagnostic marker to guide decentralized interventions like sunlight exposure (Ferguson et al., 2019). The confirmed existence of hemiflusomes, newly identified recycling hubs within mitochondria, and the role of thanatotranscriptomic genes enrich the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, linking light-driven bioenergetics to neonatal health:

Hemiflusomes, integrating proton flux, heme synthesis, and TCA cycle activity, suppress thanatotranscriptomic gene expression during the day to manage photonic/metabolic chaos and minimize UPE over-emission, while at night, low-level expression complements melatonin’s repair, reflecting an evolutionary adaptation to diurnal cycles (Ferreira et al., 2018; Nunnari et al., 2019). In jaundiced neonates, hemiflusome dysfunction from blue light therapy and hemolysis (e.g., RhoGAM, Vitamin K shot) impairs IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), increases ROS/UPEs, and activates thanatotranscriptomic genes, exacerbating autism and EDS risks, whereas melanin and red/NIR light (e.g., via methylene blue) restore hemiiflusome function, supporting myelination and bioelectric currents (Yang et al., 2014; Agan et al., 2025).

The origin of carbon through the rare triple-alpha process in collapsing stars, where three helium nuclei collide to form a threshold where chaos meets meaning, underpins the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, reflecting life’s asymmetric choice: Carbon’s unique ability to bend light into structure (via melanin and porphyrins), hold spin without collapse (enabling quantum coherence in mitochondria), and shape time into memory (through DNA and epigenetic light signals) drives hemiflusome function, TCA cycle regulation, and thanatotranscriptomic suppression, countering the ROS/UPE chaos from blue light therapy in jaundiced neonates (Nunnari et al., 2019; Van Wijk et al., 2008). This stellar legacy, fine-tuned by the Hoyle state, enhances red/NIR light’s role in restoring IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), supporting myelination and bioelectric currents, and mitigating autism and EDS risks in Rh-negative babies stressed by RhoGAM and hemolysis (Agan et al., 2025; Ferguson et al., 2019). Sunlight exposure, rich in UV-A/B and red/NIR, offers a decentralized solution by upregulating POMC and melanin to chelate toxins, restore CCO/VDR functionon the IMM, and support mitochondrial health, mitigating the long-term consequences of blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Oláh et al., 2013).

The Vitamin K shot, often mislabeled as a vaccine, introduces an additional mitochondrial stressor in jaundiced newborns, as its package insert explicitly lists jaundice as a side effect due to hemolysis, particularly in vulnerable neonates: Hemolysis from the shot increases bilirubin and free heme, generating ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further damaging mtDNA, raising heteroplasmy, and impairing OXPHOS, which reinforces the Warburg-like metabolic shift and slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizing cristae and contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and autism risk (Agan et al., 2025).
>
> Archimedes' principle and maternal light environment further exacerbate mitochondrial stress in jaundiced babies, influencing birth timing and long-term health outcomes: Fatter babies, often from sunlight-deficient mothers with gestational diabetes, exhibit negative buoyancy due to increased fat mass, delaying pelvic engagement and prolonging gestation, which increases hemolysis, bilirubin levels, and jaundice risk, while maternal blue light exposure disrupts EZ water and accelerates ubiquitination rates, amplifying mtDNA stress and heteroplasmy transgenerationally via blood UPE signaling (Sohns et al., 2004; Wallace, 2010). This circadian mismatch, reflected in the mother’s SCN clock speed and impaired DHA levels, slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and contributes to autism, obesity, and skin cancer risks, underscoring the need for sunlight exposure to restore mitochondrial function and buoyancy dynamics, mitigating these GOE-like stressors (Oláh et al., 2013; Agan et al., 2025).
>
> The evolutionary divergence of Rh-negative blood types, lacking the RhD antigen, reflects a trade-off that increases jaundice risk and mitochondrial stress in modern newborns, amplifying the GOE-like atavistic state: Rh- babies, more prone to hemolysis from Rh incompatibility, face heightened bilirubin and ROS/UPE production, exacerbated by RhoGAM, the Vitamin K shot, and blue light therapy, which impair OXPHOS, raise heteroplasmy, and slow IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and risks of autism, EDS, and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025; Flegr et al., 2015). The Rh- mutation, likely selected for pathogen resistance or hypoxic adaptation, does not involve copper-based blood, a common myth, but underscores the need for careful management of environmental stressors like blue light, advocating for sunlight exposure to mitigate these effects (Ferguson et al., 2019). The decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis is reinforced by red blood cells donating electrons to methylene blue-mediated reduction of methemoglobin compartmentalized in liposomes, enhancing the hemiiflusome’s role in mitochondrial function and oxygen delivery in jaundiced neonates:
>
> This electron transfer, amplified by red/NIR light (660–700 nm), reduces MetHb to functional hemoglobin, stabilizing heme metabolism, restoring TCA cycle oxygen utilization, and increasing IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), countering the Warburg shift and ROS/UPE production from blue light therapy (Yang et al., 2014; Sohns et al., 2004). In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, this process mitigates mtDNA hypoxia, supports myelination, and boosts bioelectric currents, reducing autism, EDS, and dysautonomia risks (Agan et al., 2025). The hemiiflusome, a light-sensitive hub for proton flux and heme synthesis, evolves from GOE-era adaptations, with UPEs from MB reduction serving as a diagnostic marker to guide decentralized interventions like sunlight exposure (Ferguson et al., 2019). The confirmed existence of hemiflusomes, newly identified recycling hubs within mitochondria, and the role of thanatotranscriptomic genes enrich the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, linking light-driven bioenergetics to neonatal health:
>
> Hemiflusomes, integrating proton flux, heme synthesis, and TCA cycle activity, suppress thanatotranscriptomic gene expression during the day to manage photonic/metabolic chaos and minimize UPE over-emission, while at night, low-level expression complements melatonin’s repair, reflecting an evolutionary adaptation to diurnal cycles (Ferreira et al., 2018; Nunnari et al., 2019). In jaundiced neonates, hemiflusome dysfunction from blue light therapy and hemolysis (e.g., RhoGAM, Vitamin K shot) impairs IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), increases ROS/UPEs, and activates thanatotranscriptomic genes, exacerbating autism and EDS risks, whereas melanin and red/NIR light (e.g., via methylene blue) restore hemiiflusome function, supporting myelination and bioelectric currents (Yang et al., 2014; Agan et al., 2025).
>
> The origin of carbon through the rare triple-alpha process in collapsing stars, where three helium nuclei collide to form a threshold where chaos meets meaning, underpins the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, reflecting life’s asymmetric choice: Carbon’s unique ability to bend light into structure (via melanin and porphyrins), hold spin without collapse (enabling quantum coherence in mitochondria), and shape time into memory (through DNA and epigenetic light signals) drives hemiflusome function, TCA cycle regulation, and thanatotranscriptomic suppression, countering the ROS/UPE chaos from blue light therapy in jaundiced neonates (Nunnari et al., 2019; Van Wijk et al., 2008). This stellar legacy, fine-tuned by the Hoyle state, enhances red/NIR light’s role in restoring IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), supporting myelination and bioelectric currents, and mitigating autism and EDS risks in Rh-negative babies stressed by RhoGAM and hemolysis (Agan et al., 2025; Ferguson et al., 2019). Sunlight exposure, rich in UV-A/B and red/NIR, offers a decentralized solution by upregulating POMC and melanin to chelate toxins, restore CCO/VDR functionon the IMM, and support mitochondrial health, mitigating the long-term consequences of blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Oláh et al., 2013).

“In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, \[…\]”
You are getting it wrong when you are claiming that RH-negative babies are the ones effected by any of this. This is not how it works. Rhogam is given to the MOTHERS that are Rh-negative when their babies are RH+…. the babies that are RH+ birthed to RH- mothers are the ones that are affected. I’m surprised you didn’t get this right because there is a real lack of understanding of this topic across the board which leads to Rh- women being forced to get the Rhogam shot.

# Thread: Heat exposure & cataract

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### AntonisK — 2025-08-01T14:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357322>

In Dr. Becker's book Cross Currents he says that " chronic exposure to heat produce cataracts. Therefore, the lens of the eye appeared to be more susceptible to heat than any other part of the body".
Of course, Dr. Becker refers to microwave radiation, but heat is heat after all, no matter the source.
So, my question is this: should we worry about heat (via infrared radiation) exposure regarding eyes during midday sun, especially in the Summer and/or sunny days?
Should this mean we shouldn't stare at the suns' direction after sunrise?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-01T15:39:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357327>

He is correct. This is why we have tears to cool the eye.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-02T02:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357356>

From the current blog: DM #64: Dry eye syndrome is a common condition in California, with prevalence varying based on screen abuse, climate, ethnicity, lifestyle, and cultural factors. Dry is is related to blue screen technology and as the eye dries, Carnot's theorem kicks in and the heat sink for semiconduction decreases and this is why dry eye and cataracts are linked. Becker showed in Cross Currents that heat exposure causes cataract formation. Excessive chronic blue light exposure causes dry eye by disrupting hydration signals and vitamin A metabolism. Reduced water production at CCO alters the heat sink (per semiconduction principles) leading to heat buildup. Heat exposure, as noted by Becker, contributes directly to cataract formation.

### Sirena — 2025-08-02T04:53:17+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-02T17:00:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357392>

Does equatorial heat matter if it also makes tears? Why are you so linear in in your thinking? Equatorial heat comes from the sun and sun also makes your eyes tear via the lacrimal gland. Why is linking lessons so hard for people?

### Sirena — 2025-08-02T22:09:58+0300

Had no idea the sun makes tears. Ok. That makes sense. However we are speaking of heat. If heat exposure effects cataracts and it doesn't matter the source of heat, then I'll take the fact that sun makes tears and be happy to hear this fact.
Made my day.
Thanks Jack.

### Jacks — 2025-08-03T06:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357420>

Yep. An old friend of mine lived in Orange County for ~25 years, until about 5 years ago. They often complained of dry eyes. Now I know why. At that time I thought it may have been related to them wearing sunscreen/sunglasses and not getting enough Vitamin D.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-05T17:21:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heat-exposure-cataract.31417/post-357595>

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324)

Changes in light input via the lens predict epigenetic programming. Blocked sunlight disrupts histone modifications and DNA methylation, contributing to modern diseases MAHA could never solve because of their myopic focus on food.

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

# Thread: Improving search/discoverability of Jack's content + forum
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-08-01T19:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-357340>

There is a ton of excellent information here: in Jack's blogs, in the webinars, in forum posts from all the members.

However, it takes a lot of time to ramp up, connect the dots, and find what you are looking for. I know that there is Search functionality on the forum and Search functionality on the blogs, but they are very crude tools.

I think that AI could help in discoverability of Jack's content. Recently, I've been doing a deep dive into Vitamin D synthesis, and I manually searched for Vitamin D posts on the forum, searched for Vitamin D blog posts, etc. I then copied bits and pieces of Jack's content into ChatGPT and repeatedly iterated with the LLM to ask critical questions that didn't make sense to me.

Of course AI cannot replace reading the blogs and forum posts directly, but they can aid a lot in even identifying the right blogs/forum posts to read. For example, as a beginner, if I read something about Vitamin D, it'd be nice to easily find posts on the forum about other people with similar Fitzpatrick skin type, haplogroup, etc. surface. If I'm reading 1 of Jack's blogs, and it has some side note about eczema, it would be great if I can see how that ties into other blog posts.

Does anybody else feel this way? Or am I just not using the existing tools well enough? It just seems like there is so much knowledge here and there is a missed opportunity to aid the reader in finding the right dots and connecting them faster.

I've been doing the learning by subjects for months now, and I realize that's not the best approach for me
I was trying to be efficient and learn about specific topics and diseases but, although I've learned a lot, I totally missed some previous foundational info, and that makes some of the newly learned info irrelevant or unusable because parts of the foundation is still flawed/left unquestioned
So I started to go back and follow the chronology of the posts, as much as possible,
this will need time

The analogy that comes to mind about my process with this information is that it's like renovating a house in Costa Rica; you redo the floor, change the tiles, fix the damaged gypsum, windows, fix the electric system, repaint inside/out, hang the picture frames.... then dry season comes, and the clay soil cracks and opens everywhere, opening gaps between the base of the walls and the floor, stressing the poorly done foundation that you never even questioned in the first place

### AlbertW — 2025-08-03T21:47:10+0300

Yes, good analogy.

For me, I’ve realized that I can’t do bottoms-up or top-down in isolation. Learning the material by reading only quantum mechanics yields no intuition about how it’s relevant to disease. Learning disease first also provides not enough of the foundation, as you say.

I may end up agreeing that reading the blogs in chronological order may be best as it more closely reflects the investigator’s (Jack) learning process combining bottoms-up and top-down knowledge accumulation. It allows the reader a chance to see if they can make the same connections that Jack did during his learning journey.

Having some AI tooling would be amazing for this, to aid in recall of previously-learned subjects and connecting to future related topics, if desired.

### AlbertD — 2025-08-13T17:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/improving-search-discoverability-of-jacks-content-forum.31418/post-358126>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=357477)
>
> Yes, good analogy.
>
> For me, I’ve realized that I can’t do bottoms-up or top-down in isolation. Learning the material by reading only quantum mechanics yields no intuition about how it’s relevant to disease. Learning disease first also provides not enough of the foundation, as you say.
>
> I may end up agreeing that reading the blogs in chronological order may be best as it more closely reflects the investigator’s (Jack) learning process combining bottoms-up and top-down knowledge accumulation. It allows the reader a chance to see if they can make the same connections that Jack did during his learning journey.
>
> Having some AI tooling would be amazing for this, to aid in recall of previously-learned subjects and connecting to future related topics, if desired.

1 (Why QM). Learn quantum mechanics first. Because quantum mechanics are the basic rules of the subatomic/atomic world. Then you transition to branches of physics of relatively larger scales (solid state physics - structure/properties of crystalline materials) or condensed matter physics (atomic interactions of solid and liquids).

Chess Analogy: Once you learn all the basic rules of chess, you can learn the basic strategies/puzzles/openings. And you build on that with more advanced strategies, etc. I brute forced a QM textbook last month. The last time I took a math course was over a decade ago in high school. I never took linear algebra/differential equations. AI helped a ton. I still cannot really derive most of the equations outlined in the book or solve many of problems without going back and forth with the solutions line by line, but the goal was to learn the basic concepts.

Me learning QM: I got some pieces of how it ties to disease because learning QM explains for example what a magnetic field is at the subatomic level or how sleeping on a magnetico relates to the Zeeman effect. But ultimately, the basic concepts/connection to diseases will be more familiar when I get to more advanced physics topics. QM is the major stepping stone, because these concepts are quite different from any other scientific field. But my intuition is the others are much more palatable because we're building on what we already learned in QM and it is not from the ground up. I think learning QM is the hardest, but it is also the most important.

2(Why QM + use AI). With your background, generating the right AI prompts will expedite your learning of foreign topics. (I have no background in physics when I started this journey). ChatGPT/Perplexity are incredible information retrieval applications. But it has no sense of creativity and I doubt that it will make ties in the way that Jack could. I mean most humans can't. Also, centralized medicine can be extremely controversial. Two highly touted studies from prestigious journals conducted by researchers from highly prestigious institutions can yield completely opposing results, while for quantum physics that isn't really possible.

3 (Reading blogs). Reading the blogs will show you some of the connections. My takeaway from reading a little over 400 blogs is that there is too many missing pieces to this puzzle. I could read all of them (almost 760 total now?) and still miss the connections/rationale behind them. Because I don't know solid state physics. I don't know non-linear optics. I don't understand quantum coherence, quantum optics, quantum magnetism, etc. Many of the excerpts in the blogs comes from the books by Becker/Mae-wan Ho/Ling. They leave breadcrumbs of the missing scientific disciplines that are overlooked in the medical community... Reading the blogs just taught me how little I really know and hints at why all these seemingly unrelated disciplines all tie into this story. If I were you, I would still read the blogs (in chronologic order ideally). In all likelihood, I have to re-read the blogs once I've learned all this physics. I despised physics in school because I didn't know it was important. Now I do.

Do you have a way to measure the electric field and magnetic field? Or is the only meter you have the Safe and Sound Pro II?
The Safe and Sound Pro II is only measuring RF.

There is a small chance that someone had a cordless phone system installed in the walls of the house. I've seen that once before.

Use the max reset button on the Pro II often and see if you can get to the exact location of the RF.
Remember that the Safe and Sound Pro II's areas of highest sensitivity are out the front and the back if the meter is held upright.
If you hold the meter like this <||>.

Hope this helps.

# Thread: parents of kids who have had reactions to bee stings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kel — 2025-08-04T05:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357504>

My daughter has been stung 5 times by bees. She is 9 years old. The first two times there was no reaction other than some swelling. The third time it was on her thumb and she had an anaphylactic reaction and had to be injected with epinephrin at hospital. The fourth time she was fine again with just some swelling. And then the 5th time she had the anaphylactic reaction and went to hopspital again.
We have epipens and are now prepared if this happens again. What I am wondering is why she sometimes reacts so extremely and other times is fine.
I asked Dr Jack and he said to build immune system with grounding and more sun, etc. We are doing this.
Just curious if anyone has any personal insights or ideas on what else we can do as well. And also am curious if anyone has kids who had anaphylactic reactions that they then grew out of.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-04T16:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357522>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357536>

In this particular scenario should I supplement witb Quercetin to lower histamine in her body? Or is this a falsehood?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-05T01:51:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357552>

### Kel — 2025-08-05T02:18:44+0300

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=357552)
>
> LOL. You need to read more and stop supplementing

Agree. I actually have thrown away my supplements since finding you. I was just double checking there isnt more I can do now, faster, after the anaphylaxis scare...Ok, message recieved. Sorry for asking! Off to beach now!!

### Kel — 2025-08-09T03:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357839>

Dr Jack mainstream doctors are telling me that if she has the anaphylactic reaction again I should use the epipen but then head straight to the hospital. Would you generally agree with that or does the epipen stop the reaction usually? I ofcourse want to do all I can if this happens again, but dont trust the hospital to not give her stuff she doesnt need.. Also just trying to evaluate how we continue to live our life in a semi-rural area. We go on lots of surf trips to pretty isolated places. Would Benadryl be ok to take along with the epipen, to help combat the puffy eyes and rash we experienced last time? Or should the two medicines not be mixed? Through redox is it possible to grow out of the reaction?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-09T05:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357846>

Precautions are wise, but if you want the immune reaction to vanish built the kids redox power with the sun and it happen again.

### Kel — 2025-08-09T15:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parents-of-kids-who-have-had-reactions-to-bee-stings.31431/post-357872>

Ok. Will do. Thank you
