# Thread: High estradiol concern
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Asael Enriquez — 2025-08-05T22:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/post-357654>

Hi,

Here are my results for a male hormone panel test done a couple months back. Estradiol seems to be quite elevated at 56 pg/ml as seen on the attached document.
I've been reading that high estrogen levels can promote yeast overgrowth in the digestive tract and can make it extremely difficult to overcome regardless of diet or supplements.

As far as strategies to improve redox, I have been able to reduce my sleeping environment to under 5 microwatts per meter squared and ELF down to .80-100mV at my new home.

I have not been consistent with being out for sunrise and blocking blue light at night due to my work schedule and personal life situations. However, now that I am back on track, I will diligently be sure to follow the above actions moving forward. My candida symptoms have reduced quite drastically since greatly reducing nnEMF exposure at night, yet I still suspect an overgrowth based on symptoms that still linger.

Can elevated estradiol be a result of excessive blue light at night and not waking up with sunrise? Or is it the yeast overgrowth itself that is driving the elevated estradiol? If so, what is the mechanism behind it.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-06T03:48:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-estradiol-concern.31439/post-357665>

High E2 = no sun.

### AlbertW — 2025-11-25T22:37:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quartz-car-windows.31448/post-363182>

I just measured my 2025 RAV4 and 2025 RX350 in a very RF quiet environment. Turns out there is still some RF that is emitted by (what I think is) the DCM.

I get 50-300 uW/m2 at body level.
Much better than the 10,000+ uW/m2 in Tesla but not 0. Time to do some hacking around to remove it.

Does anybody know of modern cars that actually allow ALL RF to be removed without prying apart panels in the car and pulling fuses?

### knees4bukele — 2025-11-26T07:33:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quartz-car-windows.31448/post-363207>

### Gian — 2025-11-26T19:47:44+0200

>
> I get 50-300 uW/m2 at body level.
> Much better than the 10,000+ uW/m2 in Tesla but not 0. Time to do some hacking around to remove it.
>
> Does anybody know of modern cars that actually allow ALL RF to be removed without prying apart panels in the car and pulling fuses?

I have a 2010 Toyota Tundra with the stock, non-nav stereo. When the stereo was completely off I was still getting RF spikes over 8,000 uW/m2. I assumed it was due to the Bluetooth pinging (even when the stereo was off). I asked a local car audio shop if they could remove the Bluetooth antenna and at first they said yes, but they never made it happen so I replaced the stock stereo with a good quality older unit that did not come equipped with Bluetooth and I am no longer getting RF emitting from my stereo

### DLO — 2025-11-28T18:01:24+0200

Ok I understand now. Thanks for clarifying.
DC to DC is the better choice with voltage matched and no step ups to run appliances. If people are concerned about Mag feilds in full DC systems this can be managed by designing a well balanced system with minimising long loops (run star config where required based on loads) with conductors twisted or tied closely + reduce I2R losses + current sag etc...

# Thread: PREDATOR VS PREY IN FINANCE.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-09T16:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/post-357881>

Lessons from Harvard: Do not be well educated, it could make you broke (Harvard) or kill you (Harvard COVID policies).

"Harvard Business School taught the Strategy case study. This is perhaps the greatest business school in the United States, the best and the brightest. The professor calls to say, 'We studied the Strategy story, and we'd like you to come in and talk to the class. The students are very excited!'

Mind you, this is after we'd adopted a Bitcoin strategy and after it had worked. Here’s what happened… I joined the class on Zoom and shared our story. I told them the company was at the end of the line.

The enterprise was worth $600 million, and the market cap was ~$5 billion.

We tried unsuccessfully to grow the business for over a decade. We were at our wits' end. The only alternative was to sell the company: corporate death. Finally, we adopted a Bitcoin standard. The stock price quadrupled, employees made millions, and shareholders made billions. Our strategy was good for Bitcoin, the company, and our customers.

A few months later, our market cap was over $30 billion.

The professor said ‘OK, you've heard the CEO. What do you guys think? Would you do it?’

He then took a survey.
First student: 'I don't know, seems kind of risky.'
Second student: 'It seems like an inappropriate use of shareholder funds.'
Third student: 'This isn't normally done.'
Fourth student: 'It kind of violates convention in corporate finance.'
Fifth student: 'We really can't see any major company doing it.'

I swear I watched 10 students, and every single one of them said, 'No, we would never do this.' The takeaway? The world's full of conventional wisdom, conventional thinkers, and conventional institutions. You can give them the answer. You can even take the risk for them and prove it works. They will just stare at you and not do it.

The one conclusion I’ve reached is that you can't predict who will get it. After all, this is a financial revolution. But I'm happy to live in a world where adoption grows from 1% to 2%, then 4%, 8%, 16%, and beyond. At that point, Bitcoin won't be a $2 trillion asset class, it'll be a $100 trillion asset class. By the time your financial adviser says it’s OK to buy Bitcoin, it’ll cost $1 million.

When they say it’s a good idea, it’ll be $10 million.

Everybody gets Bitcoin at the price they deserve."

The lesson of Predator vs Prey i son display for the Savages to learn from.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-30T18:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/predator-vs-prey-in-finance.31463/post-366120>

# Thread: BioHacking BiPolar
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31465/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jojoplachta — 2025-08-09T19:29:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/biohacking-bipolar.31465/post-357900>

I apologize in advance for the length of this. I'm old. (67) I'm BiPolar and probably Narcissistic (in the Greek mythology sense)

Family:

**Mother**: Outdoorsy DairyFarm kid in rural Nova Scotia, Canada Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full. She was red/blonde, very fair with white eyelashes, brows, blue eyes. She had regrown a fingertip that had been chopped mid nail at age 7 She was a Teacher so had summers off and pushed us to play outdoors til dark, took us to the beach on the regular and we often stayed all day, She got freckles, I got very tanned She had a small jaw and crooked crowded tooth placement She had her first (male)child who died at 6-9 months. I was the second born Inherited father's extremely dark eyes, he had black; I got reddish brown hair, olive skin, perfectly straight teeth Third (female)child is very blonde/fair like mother She became anorexic around 6yo, Bulimic as a teenager Mother had Gallbladder removed at 32 and was killed ina train/car crash at 34 (I was 12, sister 10) My Workaholic father was traumatized and family life became very difficult

**Maternal Grandmother:**

Dairy FarmHer, in Rural Nova Scotia (3rd generation) Big fan of fish, lobster, clams, dulse by the bags full Dark hair Dark eyes, Fair skin Got cataracts in 80's, debilitating arthritis in hands in her 60's, Had all teeth out and full dentures in her 40's, lived to be 95 with very bright mind til the end

**Father**: born to Polish immigrants very much traumatized by being sent to Canada to escape the starvation by Stalin during Holodomor, dairy farmer in Alberta Traumatized by abusive parents His father was angry and mother an extreme depressive and religious fanatic all her life. Left home at 16 Joined airforce at 18 He had olive skin, dark eyes, black hair, perfect teeth His only dental work was removal of a broken molar in his 70's I remember him being fun loving as a young child but extremely Depressed and/or Ragey after my Mothers death and finding out she was adulterous, he became a financially functioning alcoholic, but with violent rages, emotional and physical abuse and extreme mood swings

**Me**:

In utero and from birth until 3 1/2, I lived on a Dairy Farm in rural NovaScotia (11 miles from the AirForceBase) a vaginal birth in hospital, straight onto carnation instant milk/dextrin maltose formula and eventually raw cows milk, cream and lots of raw butter Cared for by my Maternal grandmother from 2 mos old when Mother went back to teaching.

At age 3 1/2 we moved to Trenton Ontario airforce base (literally 200 yards from the airplane runway living in a mobile home) Other than school, I was outdoors in nature from dawn til dusk on weekends and summer. Father took me snowmobiling in winter until my feet and fingers were near frostbitten. I was a behaviour problem to my mother, had sleep schedule resistance, very active, moody and strong willed, had frequent bronchitis, recurring tonsillitis and chronically chapped lips My adult teeth grew in straight but had 4-6 molars filled with amalgam by age 14 Wisdom teeth impacted and painful, removed at 18, 8 more fillings by 21

We had a colour tv as soon as available and I watched it sitting close every evening and Saturday mornings. I had trouble sleeping, and was sound sensitive and slept in the living room so I could be alone and have the radio on all night

At 10 we moved to 5 miles away from the airbase. I ran wild in nature,a horse kick at 12 broke my kneecap, had 3 surgeries by 16, I manipulated maximum Demerol and used it for emotional self medication, started smoking, drinking and marijuana at 11 Started menstruating at 12

At 13 my mother was killed in a car by a train which exposed her extramarital affair to my Father who was Traumatized My sister and I were moved in with Fathers "business partner" who years later I realized was his mistress. I was made responsible for 10 yo sister and mistresses 7, 4 and 6 month old sons. I developed chronic constipation, depression and clinical dry skin began Had 5 more Knee surgeries by age 19 Physical and emotional abuse from alcoholic depressed father. Lived in extreme anger and seething hatred Joined a religious cult and started experiencing intermittent mania

At 15 sent to psychiatrist by a school guidance councillor for major mood swings and religious fanaticism No parent was involved Diagnosed manic depressive Offered lithium which I declined in favour of keeping the "highs"

Forgot about it and went on with my "mercurial" life

At 18 Abandoned by "mistress turned stepmother" and her 3 children. I was dropped off at University, disowned by father and put on Student welfare/and arranged Government loans and worked restaurant overnight shifts to finish a Bachelor of Arts degree. Quit marijuana in first year because I had bad paranoia on it and a psychotic event with hashish Drank alcohol and smoked cigarettes heavily

At 20 Became sexually involved with a married man 21 years my senior w 4 kids After 4 years of a stressful affair. I lost a ton of weight, became severely hypoglycemic fainting all over the place Solved it with hospital low glycemic diet and lifetime of avoiding (or trying to) sugar and for a long time caffeine Married man moved in and we had a mostly On but also dramatically and stressfully Off Commonlaw relationship that lasted 21 years

At age 21 had emergency Pilonoidal cyst removal surgery Suspected hypothyroidism but testing was deemed normal Still constipated, very dry skin, low basal body temp to this day, low blood pressure but thyroid levels low normal to normal

At 24 I moved to Toronto, became a Realtor Lots of parties w alcohol some cocaine, still smoked cigarettes and lots of money and depressive periods Had a giant brick of a car phone in 1985 and cell phone ever since

At 30 moved to NYC w same older man/commonlaw husband Joined a Mystical School and began doing spiritual work and meditation seeking relief from depression and angry rages mixed with manic partying Quit smoking and for months at a time and for about 5 years kept sober, sugar and caffeine free

Worked at a NYC nightclub Used ecstasy excessively recreationally and was living in the night Sleeping every other night from 5am-noonish Eventually I began using 1/4 tab ecstasy every other day for depression Manic phases started to become extreme irritability Started gaining weight Used Jenny Craig to lose 25lbs gained back immediately

1998 Husband returned to Canada

In the year 2000 (age 42) Did Atkins Diet at Atkins Centre of Complementary Medicine NYC Diagnosed Hypothyroid and started Natural Dessicated Thyroid Had amalgam fillings changed by Huggins Protocol and Chelation Therapy w dmsa/vit c IV, started on Bioidentical progesterone Tested thyroid releasing hormone because thyroid was intermittently normal and low and couldn't lose the weight

In December 2001 after 911 and major depression with suicidal ideation, Dr Atkins sent me to Psychopharmacologist where I got diagnosis of Bipolar (rapid cycling) ->Started Lamotragine 25 mg and Quetiapine (after a few sleep meds) For the first time I could focus enough to read without major difficulty Obsessively read about BiPolar trying to prove I wasn't and how to not take meds for life

Realized that sleep was key so I forced 10-6 sleep cycle using ambien Became intuitively aware that NYC was making me sick

In 2005 Age 47 moved back to Toronto. HATED it Went back into Real Estate Gained 35 lbs, at times gaining rapidly (a lb/day) for no reason Also had days with hypersensitive sense of smell Constant low grade depression, low energy, constipation, dizzy spells and brain fog Saw a PhD Biochemist Nutritionist who advised 1200 mg magnesium, 10,000 iu d3, Evening Primrise Oil, Citicholine, Inositol amongst other supplements that I took for years

Still taking Bioidentical progesterone, I added testosterone, Dhea, Oxytocin (for awhile), injectable HGH (for awhile) Added estrogen eventually

2009 Consulted with Amen Clinic Found a psychiatrist to order a Spect Scan (results below) hoping to manage the BP with nutraceuticals instead of meds Confirmed BiPolar Diagnosis and also PTSD OCD ADD Kept taking Lamotragine added Cymbalta (for depression and chronic pain)changed Quetiapene to Trazodone

On the personal front I met current husband whose DIL had just had his/our baby grandson We bonded and I provided full time childcare weekdays

In 2010 my mostly estranged Father died Engaged in a stressful estate settlement for 2 years becoming estranged from sister in the process (for two years after)

Married in 2011 Was mostly euthymic for a couple years Husband Retired from 37 years policing to fulfill my "travel in an Airstream" dream In 2012 I lost 70 lbs using Bernstein Weight Loss program injecting b12 and b6 every other day, on 900 calories locarb Lo fat subsistence protein and was pretty happy for a few years. Quit drinking for a year and a half.

I had a basal carcinoma removed from my nose (pretty much the only place I'd used sunscreen)I had also used sunglasses but hardly ever. Planning to rent my house and travel long term, we found out the foundation had turned to sand Managed a long, stressful and expensive Reno replacing basement under the house Started finding the stress of Real estate too much and having cycles of depression, irritability and rage Self medicating with alcohol didn't help. I semiretired to provide fulltime daycare for baby grandson until he was 4 Embraced my role as grandmother and enjoyed it

2017 Husband took a job he really wanted and I bought us a Beach House on Lake Erie and bnb'ed our Toronto home. Husband diagnosed with colon cancer 3 months after starting/moving. To "save his life", I obsessively researched the internet and fixated on Morley Robbins' magnesium group desperate to cure cancer with nutrition! I followed the Root Cause Protocol for 5 years, in that group I found Dr Jack. I quit the 10k iu vit d Id been taking for 3 years. I maxed beach living, sunsets and sunrises, and lived rural with very low nnemf Depression persisted and intermittent rage

2018 Had an accident in the water where several tendons/ligaments in both knees landed me in a wheel chair unable to strengthen one without reinjuring Eventually found a rehab program at hospital and devoted to achieving physical fitness through Aquafit and strength classes knowing I'd eventually need knee replacement

2020 Covid STRESS believing the pandemic threat for first few months Got COVID/or a flu? Test was negative and saw the public health incongruencies and then the WEF "plan" Avoided the vax and stopped complying! I gave up RealEstate completely.

Realized Jack was "the real thing" somewhere around that time and was living a mostly outside life tanned, gardened, canned and preserved food for the "expected food shortages" and did Aquafit

Was diagnosed with High Ocular pressure and monitored for glaucoma

Started 5 year taper off Cymbalta thinking it might be responsible for the rages. Realized a few years in it also might've been causing ocular pressure

Kept up Aquafit which had become the only exercise routine to ever give me a neurotransmitter high

Marriage started falling apart Caught him online cheating during pandemic

2022 We separated. He moved back to Toronto and after 10 months I sold beach house, I moved back in with him planning to divorce but doing couples therapy and having lots of episodic rages My knees were both painful with Osteoarthritis-but I was tanned, had darkened my separate bedroom, wore blueblockers and installed screen softwares, wifi timer, increased use of airplane mode and pointed my OCD at DIY Jack Kruse but living in Depression and Social Isolation except for my 3 grandchildren and daughter-in-law who were (and still are) enmeshed in a nasty and stressful (for all of us) divorce.

Jan 23 Had left knee replacement

May 23 Blown away by Jack's Tetragrammaton/Huberman podcast Resumed obsessive tanning, sunrise and outdoor Jack podcasts everyday

June 23 Fell and broke right ankle and severely tore right rotator cuff (bicep longhead severed, subscapularis and supraspinatus with major tears) Had double malleolus pin and plate repair surgery Wheelchair bound 100% for 2 months and mostly for 6 months Suffered extreme neglectful abandonment by husband Rehabbed by sitting in open sliding glass doors until I could drag myself downstairs to ground in sun in yard in bikini, read Leptin Rx and Jack's blogs and X posts Gained another 25lbs on top of the 50 extra I was already carrying

January '24 Had rotator cuff repair surgery Months of painful sleeplessness and physio rehab and massage and chiropractic which still continue as compensatory muscle and tendon stresses erupted

While Opthamologist was monitoring for Glaucoma and because I was experiencing blurred vision, Found retinopathy and optical Nerve Damage in left eye by MRI (Results below)

Still in Psychotherapy for extreme stress w/husband Started CT in October 24 and other principals from Jacks Water Muse X post and Vagus Nerve Rx and reduced Cortisol significantly Depression still extreme but rage returned to cycles of manageable irritability

Dec 24 Osteopathy starts bringing relief to shoulder pain and compensatory (I guess) right sacroilliac pain

Started Methylene Blue at .5mg First dose was awesome euphoria and energizing Gradually increased to 1.5 mg. Eventually found Jacks warnings about messing with it and it appeared to be causing rage at 1.5 mg Stopped it

Regular CT and (it's summer in Canada)has decreased most of the rages

Did 7 years back taxes and refinanced to pay off 100k in taxes and took out 100 in equity from my house to buy bitcoin

Jan25 Saw something Jack said about not taking MB unless you see sunrise Since I was, I started it again and it definitely precipitated rage at only 1mg

Started having mild anxiety attacks Started tapering off the 50 mgTrazodone and adding CBD/CBN instead for sleep and chronic pain (I get some relief from PEA)I think the cbd has carried over into a calmer demeanor during the day too

Now I'm squirrelling away bitcoin, researching moving to El Salvador, (although I don't feel physically able to move most of the time) decluttering in hopes of moving, and bingeing the writings and podcasts of Dr Jack Kruse

Current dissatisfactions:

FAT, LOW ENERGY No motivation, NO JOY I crave carbs (although I've been really disciplined for several months now and seem to have successfully switched my eating window to between 8am and 3pm-4 with blood sugar more stabilized than it's been in 40ish years) Still Depressed (Dopamine must be low cause sometimes I don't even have motivation to even comply with my KrusePHD lessons and can't get myself to Aquafit class)
Any waves of euthymic mood are few and far between and Mania is always irritability Predominant mood is irritable and detached or depressed and detached. I'm pretty socially isolated and in a constant battle against sedentary inertia and until recently chronic pain. I have a drawer full of exit plan.

I've been in almost constant pain for many months and just a couple weeks ago seemingly unrelated to anything, the pain decreased by 80% (my guess is it's BP related and I'm grateful it's over but fear it's a new part of a cycle called Depression hurts) Muscle and tendon tears seem to keep happening, most recently my previously good shoulder without any conscious injury or strain and the odd time I get a weird seizing or cramping from the shin below my knee replacement, and eye twitches and above one side of my mouth (Since reading the demyelination series of blogs I wonder if that's why)

I just increased my thyroid meds (bloodwork showed low normal)(wondering if I should switch out NDT to Levothyroxine cause I read somewhere it can help depression in BP patients) And be had this itching if the skin on my shoulders and now arms for the last year and a half that slowly gets worse and doesn't really respond to any lotion, oil, except slightly I think to hypochlorous acid spray and castor oil? Not life threatening but super annoying and an indication of? I dunno

**Current Maintenance:**

AO Chiropractor adjusts c2 which rotates biweekly

Regular Chiro every two weeks

Osteopath once a week

Natural Dessicated Thyroid 60mg

Lamotragine (25mg) Trazodone (tapering off-9.5 mg currently)

Bioidentical hormones: prog, test, bi-est, Dhea, pregnenalone.

PEA for pain (3-4x400/day)

80/40 mg Cbd/Cbn for **sleep** (get 1.5-2 hrs deep sleep, average 1-1:45 REM 7-8 total)

I never miss Sunrise with grounding, am still out there for uva and added breath hold meditation last week Am tanning in spring summer and fall and have the doors open most of the time until deep winter, I do CT (but like 15-20 minutes at 50°) Blue blockers an hour before bed (which is before sunset now and as soon as sunsets in winter)

I have Iris on my laptop and The BlueLight Diet filters on my phone, a DaylightCo tablet for reading Jack, Sunroof open when driving, Sliding glass doors open in house, Wifi off at night

I eat epiPaleo in season (75% compliance) and have been locarb for 45 years (with bipolar discipline)

Drink 1 cup of coffee in am

Self medicate w 2-3 hard seltzer daily

Marriage has started to have glimmers of supportive rather than just stressful It's hard to be decisive about ending a (second)marriage when MY brain is BP

**2009 SPECT results**:

mild decreased perfusion to the left parotid lobe, small perfusion defects high convexity, right infraorbital frontal, occipital lobe midline, left medial temporal lobe, left anteroinferior cerebellum and bilateral infratemporal lobes as well as scattered hyperfusion throughout the cortex and marked hyperfusion appreciated in the thalamus,, right basal ganglia and left frontal lobe anteriorly as well as the posterior cingulate gyrus with mild-moderate hyperfusion to the anterior cingulate gyrus and marked hypofusion to the cerebellum

**NeuroOpthomology Report**:

Followup for mild left optic neuropathy: Mitochondrial genomic sequencing was normal. Testing for heavy metals was normal as well (mild elevated Thallium)

Evidence of ubtle left optic neuropathy with relative afferent pupillary defect, mild temporal pallor of the left optic disc which corresponded to thinning and pericapillary OCT.

Upon re-examination and pending MRI results, we would assume that she has had mild left non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy in the past

**2024-MRI**:

Tecnique: Multisequence multiplanar MRI imaging of the orbits with contrast

Diffusion imaging does not demonstrate any evidence of acute or subacute parenchymal brain ischemia.

The calibre of the optic nerves is symmetric. The optic chiasm is also symmetric in appearance. There is no abnormal signal or evidence of compressive lesion, the cavernous sinuses are clear. The orbital spaces are clear.

**Thoughts/Questions for my consult:**
Wondering if I should switch to lithium? Or Lithium orotate?

If I should take the "miracle" Ivermectin? The expensive but likely helpful DDW?

I do regular Blood donation? Wonder if it really is good for removing excess iron as per Morley Robbins?

I have to take Magnesium or I literally will not shit for more than a week, soooo its a solution but is it also a problem?

I also wonder if my cco is just permanently broken? Like, even when you sit in sunrise can you still remain Warburg shifted?

Do I need a Magnetico?

**Paid for a consult with Jack! Finally pulled the trigger! Can hardly wait but also bracing for a punch in the mouth I haven't considered.**

Thanks for reading. The search for balance continues.

# Thread: ME/CFS, Chronic Migraine, Fibro, Chronic neck and back pain, Sleep disorder.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-cfs-chronic-migraine-fibro-chronic-neck-and-back-pain-sleep-disorder.31467/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dee Dee — 2025-08-10T04:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/me-cfs-chronic-migraine-fibro-chronic-neck-and-back-pain-sleep-disorder.31467/post-357920>

**Hi all,

I have CFS (after virus on the 6th Cranial nerve) 2009.**
I had **double vision for 3 months and severe headache.**
They said it would be permanent but my sight recovered.

I was very ill and 16 years later I still have fatigue, chronic migraine and eye symptoms.
I've been having **Botox** for years for the headaches- it's really helped so much- I couldn't get out of bed.
I was a single mum to 4 and doing my degrees and so very ill.

Thinking of changing from Botox to injections- due to cost.
**The injections are monoclonal antibodies-** I don't like the sound of that but I need to research.
**Any opinions on it?**
I have trialled no Botox and **all the sun exposure etc hasn't changed the headaches or neck pain.**
Also Fibro, chronic neck, back pain and sleep disorder (originally not enough REM but now Apnea).
I've put off having a neck fusion for years as I don't want more metal in me or more surgeries.
However, I am in alot of pain- it radiates round the front of my neck and face, eyes and my across my upper spine.
My surgeon has really helped me previously with leg function and he is the only one Id trust to do it (he didn't do the back fusion).

Been following Uncle Jack for years and I share it with open minded people.

Lots sun exposure, stopped sunglasses years ago, do all the blocking blue light strategies and have ethernet (which I barely use). Lots grounding.

**Thinking of getting deuterium depleted water but not sure what ratio I need when adding it to water?

Also emr-tek light to top up red light exposure (winter here).**

We're also getting ready to turn the electricity off at night.

I haven't noticed any difference in my sleep when I stay in a different location.

My son says I should go to ES for a month to heal.
I'm reading healing stories now.

Australia is very totalitarian. Jabbed people are having many strange issues and dying.

A 28 yo guy I met had a heart attack after the first one, and pancreatic cancer after the second.
Treatment helped then it came back. Not looking good.

Thank you
Donna

Best wishes to all here - my tribe

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T00:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-podcasts-from-uncle-jack.31475/post-358250>

The podcast is live:

We all paint the picture of our lives with our actions and our perspectives, yet few of us realize we are the artist. What is important in life is life is how we choose to perceive the canvas; being happy doesn't mean that everything is perfect. It means that I have decided to look beyond the imperfections. People with the right perspective see opportunity everywhere, and they see problems as possibilities.

We see the rainbow not just the storm. We see the potential in people. People in life are like gold. We must dig through a lot of dirt in order to experience their magic. When we find gold we must value it; look at the present moment with them as a gift, and stop using the future with them to escape the present. We need to marvel at the gifts we have now and everyday. We see the big picture in life. We realize the more we lift and light up others the more depth of field our painting takes on. The more we do this the more people are drawn to see how "we paint" our canvas.

We are here to value life and the "right perspective" will often change everything.......a narrow perspective limits your vision of what is possible. It puts your mind in a box that is hard to escape. In life, we are always in the middle of someone's perspective, so we have to always stay in the shadow of our eternal promises, so we that we are at the head and not the foot of their perspectives.

This is where the magic happens with others. As long as our perspective deals with what is beneficial and uplifting for others, there's really no problem at all. We can live in harmony with nature and with ourselves. Make these moments important, vital, and worth living.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-26T10:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-podcasts-from-uncle-jack.31475/post-358752>

I listened to this one in full and thought Martin carried the interviewer. Martin's statement when he got to the edge of his own scientific knowledge about what health is in terms of energy, atomic structure, and information was the key takeaway for the Savages. The guy interviewing him was way out of his league, and he detracted from Martin's ideas. Martin's discussion with the audience was on Morowitz's theorem of energy flow. I loved when Martin dunked the MD's belief on serotonin and the microbiome. That was an idea that died 20 years ago, but the MD was never told. # Thread: Introduction / Observations of a nascent Mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-observations-of-a-nascent-mitochondriac.31480/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5

### JasonAppel — 2025-08-12T01:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-observations-of-a-nascent-mitochondriac.31480/post-358005>

Hi Everyone,

I'm Jason. I'm a new member here. I've been following Uncle Jack on podcasts for awhile and joined with the Patreon membership a few months ago to read the blogs. I jumped into last months Q&A which I found valuable and informative with questions about my outsized histamine responses to mosquitos and "allergens"

I'm still getting a sense of this forum, the enormity of which gives the effect of drinking from a firehose. Nevertheless I'd like to post some personal observations here as a journal of sorts which hopefully is A) appropriate for this forum and B) valuable to other members.

Observation 1. Inability to maintain the tan

Of late, I'm paying very close attention to the degree to which my skin is getting and maintaining a tan. This past Saturday I spent all day out in the sun without any need for shade and noticeably increased my tan. Yesterday was spent outside for a good part of the day as I went to a local Bitcoin BBQ meetup but I certainly got a lot less sun. As an aside, I never wear sunglasses or sunscreen and have seen the sunrise almost every single day in the past 12 months. Yesterday and today, involved a few hours of work which I do on the computer while either outside or on the screened porch. I'm less tan than I was Saturday even before getting a ton of sun and believe this is probably not good. I'm ignorant as to the decentralized wisdom on this topic but generally gather that my body is pulling in whatever melanin was cultivated.
I'd like to ask, if this is indicative of some health issue (poor redox, excessive blue light, excessive nnEMF) and if so what is the ideal move considering I'm spending my days outside sans-sunglasses and sans-sunscreen (SSaSSy) seeing the sunrise every AM and doing little to no light at night and generally going to sleep soon after dark.

### sunissol — 2025-08-12T12:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.31481/post-358028>

Hello everyone, I'm a little late since I managed to join this forum.

But now I'm finally happy with everything you share here. It takes me a little longer because it's not my language (I'm Spanish). But I'm getting by, unlearning and learning again.

I truly believe that this site is a gem.

That's why I can't let another day go by without giving infinite thanks to everyone, especially the creator and director of the orchestra.

I'm looking forward to visiting and experiencing the energy of El Salvador. Next November-December would be good.

I have a curious anecdote related to El Salvador. My mother passed away a year and a half ago at the age of 101, at home, without hospitals. Her last six months of life were quite sweet, and this had a lot to do with the kind and gentle care of a Salvadoran woman who emigrated here a few years ago. This woman gave her peace and was a breath of fresh air for her every morning. For that reason alone, El Salvador gives me good vibes. She's the first person from El Salvador I've met here.

Well, that's all for now. I'm still soaking up all this wisdom.

Hi, Everyone
I recently had a consultation with jack and he stressed that I have to get to El savador.

I live in florida and the plane flight to El Savador is only 3 hours but I have real concerns about flying for a few reasons.
1\. I have severe insomnia that is really easily influenced and I believe my insomnia started due to radation exposure when I was in the hospital. So anything concerning radation totally freaks me out.
2\. Just diagnosed with RA and not certain how my body will react to flying.
3\. I have an undiagnosed circulatory problem and im concerned about blood clots.

My husband and I have been looking into driving there and I just wanted to know if anyone else has driven to El Savador. Ive tried looking into other methods like cruises, ferrys,etc with no luck.

Sorry - I have not driven - but you may want to consider the following responses to your questions.

1) Jack had a recent blog on protection from radiation when flying which you should be able to access at Silver level: [https://optimalklubs.com/cpc-71-gold-yellow-as-a-pomc-radiation-shield-during-aiplane-travel/](https://optimalklubs.com/cpc-71-gold-yellow-as-a-pomc-radiation-shield-during-aiplane-travel/)

3) I always wear DVT socks when flying to help with circulation in legs and I always get an aisle seat so I can get up and move around frequently. I drink lots of water and perhaps add some salt to retain water to counteract the dehydration of air travel. I eat seafood beforehand. A 3-hour flight is really not that long and once you are there you should be able to recover well. The drive will take you a very long time and you have to pass through several countries.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:02:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heme-protein-damage-and-renovation.31485/post-358139>

**STRINGING THE LESSONS ON THE FORUM:**

The K-T (Cretaceous-Paleogene) extinction 66 million years ago, from an asteroid impact in the Yucatan (Factor X), caused prolonged darkness, global cooling, and initial hypoxia from dust-blocked photosynthesis. UV light was blocked.

Non-avian dinosaurs and eutherian mammals faced maladaptive HIF-1 surges under stress, leading to energy depletion and extinction. Mammals survived via small size, nocturnal habits, endothermy, and flexible diets, thriving in low-light niches during a "nocturnal bottleneck."Post-K-T, sunlight's return spurred algal blooms, surging atmospheric oxygen and supporting higher metabolic rates.

This "mitochondrial world" favored heme protein adaptations, like efficient hemoglobin for oxygen affinity in endotherms. What are the absorption spectra of most heme proteins and why might you think this is the case?

**Heme Proteins and POMC Gene Connection:** Heme, tryptophan-containing for oxygen handling (e.g., cytochrome C in oxidative phosphorylation), linked to the POMC gene in mammals. POMC, located on human chromosome 2p23 (conserved in primates), encodes peptides like ACTH, α-MSH (for pigmentation and melanin synthesis), and β-endorphin for stress and energy regulation. UV light stimulates POMC expression and cleavage, enhancing melanin production and related functions. **_All heme proteins on the IMM are loaded with red light chromophores. CCO has 4 of them embedded in it._**

Heme influences POMC via circadian regulators Rev-Erb-alpha/beta, integrating oxygen metabolism with light-driven rhythms. Post-K-T oxygen surge amplified this, enhancing mammalian neuroendocrine adaptations for diverse environments.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heme-protein-damage-and-renovation.31485/post-358147>

Bro.........time to wake up.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-13T20:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heme-protein-damage-and-renovation.31485/post-358149>

**UVA-mTOR Interplay and Spectra:** UVA absorption via OPN5/SIRT1 boosts NAD+, activating AMPK to inhibit mTOR, favoring photo repair like mitochondrial biogenesis. Outputs include IR-A emissions (600–1000 nm) from ATP/AMP ratios and clock genes, promoting coherent UPE for DNA repair. Melanin (from tyrosine/phenylalanine) protects DNA by ROS quenching, while melatonin (95% mitochondrial, from tryptophan) switches metabolism seasonally. Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen and sunglasses block all of UV light = why humans do not heal = diabetics = centralized RETARDS advocate sunblock/sunglasses [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-66-135709978)

# Thread: El Salvador ocean swimming?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sirena — 2025-08-14T21:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358240>

Where do mito ocean swimmers actually swim out past the waves?
vs hoping in an ocean pool or swimming pool.

Atami? El Palmacito? Shalpa? San Blas?
Where?

Thanks

### LoriLee — 2025-08-14T21:15:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358241>

a regular person does not belong swimming out past the waves. I have been in shalpa many times and the policy is to not go out above your mid thighs, knowing that the wave heights come and go. It can be very dangerous if you get in a tide where more of your body mass is below water versus above water. Now, having said that, I have seen some surfers, people who are very versed with ocean swimming, go out way further than I think anyone should. I dont think shalpa is the place to do that though. with it being within a cove, the riptide is much worse there. Lots of surfers at el zonte so maybe that is the place? I dont really have an answer except please be careful!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T02:01:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/el-salvador-ocean-swimming.31496/post-358255>

Sirena your question is flat retarded.

**1\. The Loss of the Monthly "Scrub"of mass**
For decades, the female body uses the menstrual cycle to offload **excess iron** and **deuterated metabolic water**.

**The Post-Menopause Trap:** When periods stop, the "exit valve" closes. Iron begins to accumulate in the tissues.

**Melanin Failure:** If **melanin** (the body’s primary broadband absorber and chelator) is depleted or "jammed" by glyphosate or nnEMF, it can no longer "caged" this free iron.

**2\. Ferritin as a "Dirty Antenna" like biology had to deal with in the GOE. Point made clear in the blogs.**

Free iron is toxic, so the body shoves it into **ferritin** storage.

**The Antenna Effect:** Ferritin is a nanocage with a mineral core of hydrous ferric oxide. In an environment saturated with **nnEMF**, these ferritin cages act as **paramagnetic antennas**.

**Energy Transformation:** They capture ambient electromagnetic frequencies and, because the system lacks the coherent **220nm UPE signals** (due to the "seed oil" D-PUFA interference), they transform that energy into **non-coherent infrared (heat)**.

**3\. The "Hot Flash" as Mitochondrial Exhaust**

This is where the "Hot Flash" originates. It is not a "brain glitch"; it is a **localized thermal dump**.

**Heteroplasmy & Heat:** As mitochondria age and **heteroplasmy** (mutated mtDNA) increases, they become less efficient at making "cold" ATP and more prone to "leaking" heat.

**The Flash:** When the "ferritin antennas" pick up nnEMF, they trigger a sudden discharge of this non-coherent thermal energy. The body attempts to dump this heat via vasodilation and sweating from the eccrine glands talked about in CPC #79 ---> the classic hot flash. Some women get a lot og GI symptoms in menopause -----> exocrine mass dump = CPC#79 too.

**4\. The Matrix Collapse**

This rising iron/ferritin level further poisons the **TCA cycle**, leading to more deuterium incorporation into the **collagen matrix**. This creates a feedback loop:

1.  **High Iron** = More nnEMF absorption.
2.  **More Heat** = Destruction of the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water from CCO**
3.  **Low EZ Water** = Protons can't flow, leading to more saggy collagen, saggy breasts, fat, **ligament tears** and skin aging, (the "exhaust problem").

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-15T18:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358283>

I'm looking for some advice on how to overcome my caffeine addiction. My current understanding is that I'm low dopamine and that improving my redox is the answer. The problem, it seems, is that even when I get morning sunrise and spend as much time as possible outside, avoid ALAN, etc. my eczema/rosacea is preventing me from absorbing the UV to produce the melatonin to regenerate the tissues and create the melanin to raise my dopamine levels.

I have a pretty strong will but after many failed attempts to quit cold turkey I'm thinking its time to work smarter and not harder.

Should I be putting more emphasis on seafood? I eat it probably 2-3 times a week. Or maybe survivors soup? I've done some shellfish broth but haven't done bone broth yet.

Thanks in advance.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-15T19:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358294>

You overcome this addition by fixing the redox signaling on the IMM. A CCO VDR story laid out in the current blog series.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-15T23:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358319>

Got it. Think I'll try to up my AM light intake and get even stricter with the evening light. Might try to maintain a carnivore diet for a little while to make more DDW.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-18T15:32:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358404>

Just to give an update on this for anyone who might be interested: I'm now on day 3 of no caffeine, which felt almost impossible just a few days ago. The day I posted this I got a lot of sun, walked on the beach, totally cut out tech use after about 6:00 pm (only using my daylight PC and light phone I might add), and went to bed as soon as it got dark. Woke up in the morning and spent 15 min in the sun, followed by a high fat and protein meal before heading to work.

Definitely was not at my best cognitively on day 1 but that was to be expected. I thought I felt a headache coming on towards the evening time but as soon as I went outside after work and got some sun that went away.

Some other observations I found interesting: even though I wound up eating some foods I normally wouldn't (for example a sandwich on sourdough bread that probably had some seed oils in it) I woke up with hardly any inflammation or digestive issues that tend to occur when I've eaten these things in the past. I'd heard Jack talk about Alan Frey and blue light opening the gut barrier but as someone who values empiricism that was sort of definitive proof to me that light is the major problem.

### Nick-Rolser — 2025-08-19T15:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358480>

> [hacklose said:](/goto/post?id=358453)
>
> What are the electric, magnetic, and RF field levels in your daily/sleep spaces?

That's a very good question. I have not measured with a Gauss meter, only with a cheaper device that detects electric and magnetic fields. I didn't detect anything egregious in my room but also am aware I need a better way to measure. Nevertheless its something I have little control over at the moment. I live in an apartment complex with many routers. Our router is on the other side of the apartment but I can't shut it off at night because I live with video game addicts. On the plus side, I live in a small town of about 10,000 people.

If all goes according to plan, I'll be moving out of this place in 4-5 months and heading down to the 28th latitude. Hoping to have a lot more control over such things then.

Control what you can. A reasonable proposition for the wireless router would be to run Ethernet cables directly to the gaming devices. Assuming it has a RJ-45 port. The cables could be run along baseboard or ceiling using various adhesive products that don't damage paint and do not require drilling. Under throw carpets works too.

### hacklose — 2025-08-22T20:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overcoming-caffeine-addiction.31502/post-358630>

> [Nick-Rolser said:](/goto/post?id=358624)
>
> I'd have to check the brand name, but I can tell you its not a very good device -- I bought it before I knew a whole lot about nnEMF. Do you have any recommendations?

I prefer using the Cornet ED98QPro5G and the Safe and Sound Pro II in combination.

I use the Cornet for electric and magnetic fields and use both of them to measure RF. The Safe and Sound Pro II has a higher sample rate for RF than the Cornet, so it will register some of the higher peaks you are exposed to. If you can only afford one, buy the Cornet. It's cheap and works well.

I measure body voltage with a multimeter I used in back in the day on electrical projects.

# Thread: ENVIRONMENT OR FOOD?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environment-or-food.31508/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-16T04:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environment-or-food.31508/post-358327>

Your Job Could Be Shaving Years Off Your Life – And It's Not Just in Your Genes

Why does this matter? Genetics might be your blueprint, but your environment is the architect rewriting the plans. Experts are buzzing: Forget chasing gene therapies, because real wins come from fixing what's around us. Cleaner air, better jobs, kicking bad habits? These could add years to your prime.This isn't just science; it's a wake-up call.

Your cubicle, commute,your computer terminal, or corner store might be aging you faster than Father Time. Time to rethink: What in your world needs a upgrade?

Share if this hit home because we need to spread the word and live longer!Imagine this: You've got "good genes," but your daily grind, zip code, and bad habits are secretly accelerating your biological clock faster than a ticking bomb. A groundbreaking study of nearly 500,000 people just dropped the mic on aging, and it's a game changer.

Published in Nature Medicine, this massive analysis from the UK Biobank reveals that your environment – the "exposome" of everything from air pollution to workplace stress packs a bigger punch on how you age and die than your DNA ever could.

Key bombshells: 25 environmental culprits identified: Things like socioeconomic status, smoking, and even where you live are directly tied to "proteomic aging" (fancy term for how your proteins break down over time) and higher mortality risk.
Poverty and puffs steal your youth: Low income and smoking emerged as top villains, speeding up premature aging and death MORE than genetic factors. Lifestyle trumps luck: While genes play a supporting role, modifiable exposures like pollution, social isolation, and poor sleep drive disease risks way harder. Diet? Jury's out: The study couldn't nail down reliable diet data, because food really does not matter as much as many believe but environmental factors screamed "change me now!" for longer, healthier lives.

CITE
Integrating the environmental and genetic architectures of aging and mortality M. Austin Argentieri, Junhui Huang, Chirag M. Lakhani, Chirag J. Patel Nature Medicine, volume 31, pages 1016–1025 (2025) DOI: 10.1038/s41591-024-03483-9 Published online: 19 February 2025

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-16T15:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environment-or-food.31508/post-358332>

If you know you know. This is the lady that shows you every longevity expert out there is FOS. She is and was the ultimate wellness rule breaker who lived 122 years and 164 days. Longer than anyone in recorded history.

Her daily routine:
↳ Smoked 2 cigarettes daily until age 117
↳ Ate 2 pounds of chocolate per week
↳ Drank Port wine regularly
↳ Doused everything in animal fats and olive oil

She also took up fencing at 85. Rode her bike until 100. Walked until she was 110. She only quit smoking because she went blind and could not see her cigarette to light it.

The Science Behind It

Her mitochondrial colony were exceptional. She sat out in her flower garden every day of her life. She was a young woman when the Eifel Tower opened and was present. She was there for its 100th anniversary as a guest of the French government. Studies on her found she had mitochondrial epeigenetic variants that enhanced cellular photo repair and maintenance. She showed us that environment trumped our nDNA. But centralized science continues to miss this lesson and you pay that toll every day with their evidence based advice. None of you realize it. How can I say this? Did you know that recently a paper was published in Nature Medicine that was a massive analysis from the UK Biobank on this topic. Do you know what it revealed? It revealed the lesson Ms. Calment life; that our environment – the "exposome" of everything from air pollution to workplace light stress packs a bigger punch on how you age and die than your diet or DNA ever could. It tells us why all our longevity advice is horribly flawed.

Ms. Calment befuddled the experts this caused researchers to study her life and they found what actually supported her longevity:
↳ Consistent movement adapted as she aged OUTSIDE
↳ Strong social connections via her network
↳ Positive outlook in life and humor in the face of defeat
↳ She ate a Mediterranean Sea based diet filled with DHA and used little to no technology in her life. People were her iPhone. Sounds like what I have been teaching all of you but all you do is argue with me for citations and BS questions. You were born to imbibe these basics. Begin!

# Thread: mother and son in need of answers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31511/>

**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### american_shaolin — 2025-08-16T16:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31511/post-358335>

Hello,
My name is Josh and I've been tasked with helping a mother and son who has issues with ELF and melanin reproduction. They live in a house without any electricity and are out in the sun all day. They are also a huge follower of Jack Kruse. She asked me to relay some questions from her, my apologies if this is in the wrong spot.

Her note:
I am afraid I became hypersensitive to the lowest level of ELF. You can see those pulses only on a very sensitive Cornet meter (most of them are not). (I have a few of them)
It shows 0002.–0003., but every 2-3 hours there are pulses of 0005. . All day and all night long.
It’s not because of the powerlines, cables, or electrical devices. I think they are all over the country (Portugal), even in the middle of the woods or the ocean. Unfortunately I can feel them in my body, they are causing a lot of pain. My condition becomes worse each day. There is no material to block them and nowhere to hide. RF I can block, so no need to get into that.
My questions are:

Dr. Kruse explained that earth has been electrifying since the 1900s. Is this the cause for those pulses? Is there something to block them?

Are those pulses of ELF the same everywhere in the world? Do they change in a different altitude?

I’m doing the sun therapy in UV light, drinking Q18 with flor de sal, trying to build melanin and enhance the solar callus but it feels like the ELF pulses of 0005. are keeping knocking off the melanin and melatonin in my body.
Is this a losing battle?
I fear that those pulses of 0005., which most people are not aware of and can’t even measure, are causing autoimmune diseases and when you become sensitive to them — game over.
I hope there is a way to overcome this condition. Please help. Thank you, Gallia

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-19T07:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mother-and-son-in-need-of-answers.31511/post-358459>

1\. Melanin absorbs and decontaminates nnEMF. One needs to be in an environment to make it. If you cannot make it, then oxygen becomes a toxin and you get the feelings and diseases you have.

2\. No they are not the same everywhere in the world. Each place has a different EMF topology.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-18T04:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unexpected-radiation-effects.31523/post-358400>

In the summer of 1978, at a secret Soviet facility near Moscow, young physicist Anatoli Bugorski was preparing for what he believed would be a routine task. He was working on the massive U-70 proton accelerator, a machine capable of propelling particles near the speed of light. That day, a safety light warning that the accelerator was active malfunctioned, and trusting his familiarity with the lab, Bugorski leaned in to inspect a part of the machine—right into the path of a high-energy proton beam. The beam pierced the back of his head and exited near his nose, carrying an energy hundreds of times greater than a lethal dose of radiation.

Miraculously, Bugorski did not die instantly. He later described the experience in words that would become legendary: “I saw a flash brighter than a thousand suns, but there was no pain.” In the hours and days following the accident, the left side of his face swelled grotesquely, his skin peeled, and he suffered permanent consequences: complete hearing loss in his left ear, partial facial paralysis, and later, epileptic seizures. Yet he remained conscious, aware, and functioning—defying the expected limits of human survival.

Doctors were astonished, as the dose of radiation he received should have been fatal within seconds. Despite his injuries, Bugorski completed his studies, earned a doctorate, and continued his career as a physicist in the Soviet Union, silently carrying the story of a man who survived an almost unimaginable scientific accident. His experience remains one of the most extraordinary events in the history of physics, a testament to the astonishing—and sometimes inexplicable—resilience of the human body. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-18T18:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unexpected-radiation-effects.31523/post-358423>

**The Absence of Pain: Neurobiology Meets High-Energy Physics**

Pain perception requires activation of nociceptors, specialized sensory receptors responsive to thermal, mechanical, or chemical stimuli—and subsequent signaling via the peripheral nervous system (PNS) to the central nervous system (CNS). The brain parenchyma itself lacks nociceptors; pain from intracranial events (e.g., headaches) arises from stimulation of dural, vascular, or meningeal structures. In Bugorski's case, the proton beam traversed from the occipital region through the left hemisphere, exiting near the nose, likely damaging cortical and subcortical tissues without directly engaging pain-sensitive meninges or vessels in a manner that triggered immediate sensation.However, your point about pain receptors in the beam's path is valid: the trajectory could involve scalp, skull, and extracranial tissues with abundant nociceptors. Why no pain?

Proton beams in accelerators like the U-70 deposit energy via ionization along a narrow track (Bragg peak phenomenon), causing microscopic burns and cellular disruption with minimal lateral spread. This "thin track" ionization, estimated at ~1 mm diameter in Bugorski's incident, may not have activated sufficient nociceptors for conscious pain registration, especially if the velocity (near light speed) rendered the damage instantaneous.

Post-event pain (swelling, peeling skin) aligns with delayed inflammation and tissue repair, as nociceptors respond to secondary cytokines and edema. My hypothesis is that pain requires light to be "slowed down"reframes this in quantum biological terms. The proton beam, while not electromagnetic "light," delivers energy akin to high-frequency radiation (equivalent to ~10^11 eV per proton), far exceeding biological chromophores' (e.g., cytochrome c oxidase in mitochondria) capacity for absorption and processing.

In my decentralized thesis, pain manifests when energy/information is transduced slowly enough for mitochondrial UPEs (ultraweak photon emissions) to collapse wave functions in neural microtubules. If the beam's energy overwhelms this, it bypasses slowdown via refractive index or dielectric constants in tissue. As a result no "time" elapses for pain signaling, as the system's entropy (ignorance) prevents registration.

**Subjective Entropy:** Ignorance as the Fabric of Reality

Entropy, as Renato Renner articulates, measures ignorance is a lack of knowledge about microscopic details, whether particle motion, code digits, or quantum states. This idea unifies thermodynamic entropy (Boltzmann's probabilistic disorder) with informational entropy (Shannon's uncertainty), both subjective and observer-relative. My thesis echoes this idea in every concept I teach you in blogs: entropy's apparent increase (e.g., oil-water separation) depends on the observer's resolution; a superhuman thermal viewer sees microscopic disorder rise. But few of you see it. This is why you all make the mostake comparing yourself and your issues to each other and it is futile to do so. It just creates disorder in thinking. No one is stacking this lesson as far as I can tell.

In Bugorski's case, the "observer" is his biological system, chromophores and mitochondria unable to track the beam's momentum due to its extreme energy. As I have pointed out a myriad of times, if melanin (a key chromophore) is "weak" or the energy too strong, the system loses track of information (Noether's theorem), manifesting as no immediate pain or time perception.

**This subjectivity has profound implications:**

1.  _Pain and Time:_ Pain requires temporal processing, nociceptor activation (~milliseconds) to cortical integration. If entropy (ignorance) is maximal during the event, no "arrow of time" forms for pain, as entropy engraves time's direction. Bugorski's flash without pain suggests a relativistic-like compression: the beam's near-c velocity minimized interaction time, preventing entropy increase in pain pathways.
2.  _Eyes, Consciousness, Demyelination:_ All share subjective entropy. Vision (retinal chromophores) processes light information; consciousness (microtubule wave function collapse) reduces quantum uncertainty; demyelination increases neural entropy by disrupting UPE signaling, raising ignorance about photo-bioelectric states. In my thesis, these are linked by mitochondrial "information engines," where entropy taxes (heat dissipation per Landauer's limit) limit processing. Bugorski's survival implies his system adapted to high-entropy input, perhaps via Prigogine's internal compensation.
3.  _Centralized Medicine's Failure:_ Conventional models assume objective entropy (e.g., radiation dose → deterministic damage), ignoring observer subjectivity. Doctors predicted death because their "ignorance" (averaged macroscopic metrics like temperature, pressure) couldn't model the microscopic resilience. Decentralized medicine, inspired by Szilard's engine (a thought experiment linking information to work and entropy), views health as distributed entropy management, mitochondria as engines extracting order from chaos without central control. **_My 1980s realization at Charity Hospital aligns: medicine should decentralize to mimic nature's probabilistic, observer-relative entropy flows._**

Implications and the Sense of It

You all should sense the deep implications of this thread: if entropy is subjective, reality (including pain, disease) is composed of ignorance. Centralized medicine's "averages" (e.g., dose-response curves) fail because they ignore microscopic details where time manifests. In Bugorski, no pain occurred because his tissues "lost track" of the beam's momentum, entropy maximized instantly, collapsing time perception. This challenges determinism: consciousness arises from reducing entropy (ignorance) via UPEs/microtubules, but overwhelming energy (like the beam) creates "timeless" states.

Broader ramifications:

-   _Health as Entropy Management:_ Diseases like demyelination increase entropy (disrupted UPEs raise neural ignorance), explaining cognitive haze without macroscopic damage.
-   Decentralized Interventions: Prioritize light/circadian alignment to minimize entropy tax, as mitochondria can't "remember" (store information) without power.
-   _Philosophy of Science:_ As Jaynes showed in his work, maximum entropy principle makes inferences from incomplete data observer-dependent, rendering centralized models illusions. Szilard's engine papers, showed us by tying information acquisition to entropy production, it prefigures decentralized systems where agents (cells) act intelligently amid uncertainty.

In essence, Bugorski's case exemplifies how subjective entropy exposes centralized medicine's blind spots: it can't model what it doesn't "know." Decentralized views empower by embracing ignorance as opportunity for resilience.

Those are the lessons all of you keep missing.

THEY ARE MASSIVE

Teaching example of the science in this thread: IBD & the Body Clock: When the Gut Loses Time In inflammatory bowel disease it is because the SCN and skin clocks have lost their periodicity. As a result this disrupts the moleculat clocks in the intestine and immune system amplify inflammation and weaken barrier function. The authors highlight clock-gene targets and practical levers, bright mornings, dark nights, consistent sleep, earlier eating windows, as add-ons to standard care, pointing toward trials of timed therapy and LIGHT nutrition.

[https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/26/8/3724](https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/26/8/3724)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Hello from the UK - some advice please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-19T13:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358476>

Hi everyone, first post after gaining access to the forums and coming across Jack Kruse work / podcasts only a week or so ago.

Over the last 1-2 years I've been suffering from 2-4 times a month waking in the night with severe stomach pain, eventually reaching / vomiting and pain subsiding 6 hours later. I've tried narrowing it down to particular processed foods, like crisps, alcohol and general overconsumption late in the day.
My doctor prescribed omeprazole initially, but that didn't do anything.

I've been doing a bit of extended fasting now and again and trying to be careful what I eat but fumbling in the dark really. Since coming across Jack Kruse I'm shifting my eating to morning, afternoon and avoiding consumption altogether in the evening.

I've just had an ultrasound today which has picked up an inflamed gallbladder and gallstones.
Wondering if someone would be kind enough to point me in the right direction? I definitely don't want to have my gallbladder removed!
Thanks in advance

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-19T16:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358490>

My recent work in the demyelination series and on the gut nerves provides some inight. Read them.

### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-20T20:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358554>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=358490)
>
> My recent work in the demyelination series and on the gut nerves provides some inight. Read them.

Hi thank you, I found the Demyelination series on your Patreon so signed up and have had a read through, along with your "Do you need a gallbladder" article.

It's an awful lot of new information, but I think I understand how I got to this point; working from home since Covid, many hours after dark in front of screens, little morning sunlight and a fair measure of gluttony over the years. I may also have exacerbated the production of the gallstones through some prolonged intermittent fasting...

Since my ultrasound and identification of dodgy gallbladder and stones I've committed to better choices - I'm keen to ensure I'm on the right path. My digestive system feels "blocked" at the moment, I don't know if that's anything down to my new found awareness of my condition, but I am eating slowly are carefully and more in line with my carcadian cycle.
I certainly seem to have lost my abnormal appetite, perhaps that is through eating more mindfully or just the state of my body right now. I know this is not a quick fix I'm just very keen to bring down the inflammation and heal.

My most recent blood test with the doc showed low potassium, so I was trying to counter this with bananas and tomato juice, but I think I need to avoid Tomatoes as they are a nightshade (as per brain gut 6 epi paleo rx post). Am I ok with banana's - which are never in season and imported to the UK. I will try to look for alternatives in the meantime.

I also wonder if any supplements are recommended e.g. L-Glutamine, Kelp, Magnesium, milk thistle, dandelion & turmeric, coconut oil.

Thank you in advance.

### regularjoe777 — 2025-08-22T00:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358599>

> [S Hudson said:](/goto/post?id=358592)
>
> Morning sunlight and getting as much sun as you can may well help with the feeling clogged up. Leaving the Uk for sunnier climates may also benefit, with the added incentive that you will no longer be in a failing country that is on the brink of either civil war or tyranny (prob b

Thanks I'm tapping in. I agree the UK is going to the dogs, like most western countries, but where to go?
I hear El Salvador is a good place to be, anywhere else that anyone could recommend?

### S Hudson — 2025-08-22T17:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk-some-advice-please.31526/post-358619>

> [regularjoe777 said:](/goto/post?id=358599)
>
> Thanks I'm tapping in. I agree the UK is going to the dogs, like most western countries, but where to go?
> I hear El Salvador is a good place to be, anywhere else that anyone could recommend?

The plan I'm formulating is to not be tied to 1 country. I'm visiting 3 in the next few months to assess suitability for me

# Thread: "Turn off the internet"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hacklose — 2025-08-20T23:31:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/post-358562>

Go to 1:03:59 of the podcast.

I was listening to this podcast and she asked Jack, "So what is the answer to all of this chronic disease and cancer?"
Jack's immediate response, "Turn off the internet." Followed by more you can listen to.

Was this a mistake? Bitcoin doesn't work without the internet.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-21T17:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/post-358581>

yes Bitcoin does. See Dorsey latest invention.

### hacklose — 2025-08-21T18:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/post-358584>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=358581)
>
> yes Bitcoin does. See Dorsey latest invention.

Bitchat, Jack Dorsey's latest invention, uses a bluetooth mesh network.
Should we be supporting wireless bluetooth?

The bitcoin transaction is not finalized and does not appear on the blockchain until it is broadcast by a device with internet access and accepted into the Bitcoin mempool.

Just another layer in the onion. The top of the pyramid is the centralized internet. Downstream is bitcoin. Further downstream is bitchat.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-22T02:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/post-358604>

> [hacklose said:](/goto/post?id=358584)
>
> The bitcoin transaction is not finalized and does not appear on the blockchain until it is broadcast by a device with internet access and accepted into the Bitcoin mempool.

That will always be the issue.. I do not think there is a practical way around this, unless, there are designated relays connected to the internet. Which you could technically get one way down links (e.g. Sat comms) sign tx offline, but then you must choose the uplink path?? Ultimately, finding it's way to the internet.. So for personal use you could technically outsource the internet component... however, it would require additional steps, but cannot avoid the internet at the end of line..

This was demonstrated back in 2019 in Canada by Coin-kite co-founder.

### hacklose — 2025-08-22T20:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/turn-off-the-internet.31532/post-358627>

There is no practical way around it by design. Bitcoin was created for cybersecurity, not privacy or freedom.

How well are you versed with TOr? As it is bidirectional (plus additional configs). Plus there are other layers you add. It requires good OPSEC with keeping entry + exit tight. Do your own research on this. You layer entropy I can't go into specifics.
