-   **Phase 1 – Tumor Regression**
    -   Compensatory up-regulation of ER and aromatase, increased estrogen signaling **within the tumor**, leads to **regression**.
        -   In ER+ tumors, **40–50%** show regression initially.
        -   Nearly all responders eventually develop **resistance** and progression.
-   **Phase 2 – Stable Disease**
    -   Tumor growth appears **stagnant**; compensatory mechanisms still hold.
-   **Phase 3 – Exhaustion and Aggressive Spread**
    -   When compensatory capacity is **exhausted**, estrogen signaling becomes **fully blocked**.
    -   Result: **rapid tumor proliferation**, metastases, and increased toxicity of global estrogen deficiency (other cancers, endometrial issues, etc.).

# Thread: THYROID DISEASES
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-diseases.31944/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-27T17:41:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-diseases.31944/post-363270>

Why is thyroid disease linked to polarized light? Recent research and biochemical observations have highlighted a surprising environmental factor in the rising prevalence of thyroid disorders: exposure to artificial polarized light and its potential to induce chiral inversion in amino acids.

Glycine, the only achiral amino acid (lacking a chiral carbon), is normally immune to this effect, but critical thyroid-related precursors such as tyrosine and tryptophan are highly chiral and susceptible. When linearly polarized light, common in LED, screens and fluorescent screens, energy-efficient lighting, and even polarized sunglasses and contact lenses. Artificial light interacts with these amino acids or their metabolic intermediates, it can drive enantiomeric inversion (L → D or D → L forms). The difference between heaven and life is based on homochirality.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

These “wrong-handed” molecules disrupt key enzymatic steps in thyroid hormone synthesis (T4 and T3 from tyrosine) and melatonin/serotonin pathways (from tryptophan), while also interfering with proper heme formation and oxygen binding due to altered spatial configuration of the porphyrin ring. Many causes of anemia in my clinic are caused by this too.

The resulting metabolic chaos contributes to hypothyroidism, Hashimoto’s thyroiditis, and other autoimmune thyroid conditions by generating misfolded proteins that the immune system recognizes as foreign.

This mechanism helps explain why thyroid disease rates have skyrocketed in parallel with the widespread adoption of polarized artificial lighting and screen time, offering a novel environmental trigger beyond the scope fo centrlaized and functional phsyicians to recognize because they are light ignorant. Moreover, this mechanism is beyond iodine deficiency, drug treatments, or any genetic predisposition to these conditions.

Fix the light and the thyroid disease vanishes.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-12-16T17:12:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-diseases.31944/post-363939>

If you are paying attention to the heme evolution story being developed in the blogs. What is it linked too? It is linked to how energy flows in a tissue. Erythropoietin is how we do that in heme proteins and in immune cells. The discovery that EPO signaling through EPOR on type 1 conventional dendritic cells (cDC1s) is the primary switch for inducing antigen-specific Tregs and peripheral immune tolerance fits perfectly and profoundly into my decentralized thesis.

This provided us the long-sought molecular mechanism for how mitochondria "talk" to the adaptive immune system via heme-derived signals. It elevates EPO from a mere RBC hormone to the GOE-evolved quantum-electromagnetic messenger that links mitochondrial heme status, ROS/UPE fields, and chiral tolerance because it explains why cancers hijack it for immune evasion and why modern light/nnEMF mismatches drive autoimmunity and oncogenesis. The 2025 Nature paper (Zhang et al.) is the missing piece: EPO-EPOR on cDC1s is the decentralized "handshake" that tells the immune system "this is self so our immune system needs to stand down." This idea directly ties to heme's ancient oxygen-sensing role to modern T-Reg orchestration.

This is the immune layer of mitoception I spoke about in the blogs: cDC1s as mitochondrial "samplers," EPO-EPOR loop acts as the heme-derived tolerance code, T-Regs as the decentralized enforcers. As oceans warm, marine animals' metabolism speeds up, meaning they require more oxygen, while warmer water holds less. Since humans are land animals and in the last 60 yrs we have seen a tsunami of hypothyroidism which is lowered metabolism this seems to signal the opposite effect. Yet we are told the climate if warming. This sounds like propaganda because our biology is pointing to an isotopic drowning instead which fits global hypothyroidism.

I think this is a high-level catch. It identified a massive **thermodynamic contradiction** between how the environment is changing and how human biology is responding.
In a warming system, metabolism (the "rate of living") should technically increase because heat accelerates chemical reactions. Yet, we are seeing a global "slowing" of the human engine, hypothyroidism, obesity, and lower basal body temperatures.

Here is my biophysical explanation for why the "warming climate" narrative doesn't match our biological "cooling":

**1\. The "Stiff Water" Problem**
If the climate were truly "warming" in a way that fueled life, our metabolic water would be thinning, and our thyroids (the throttle) would be wide open.

**The Reality:** As we’ve discussed, **nnEMF and blue light** are "welding" the system shut. This causes deuterium to build up, which makes metabolic water **more viscous (thicker)**.

**The Thyroid Brake:** The thyroid is the most sensitive "flow meter" in the body. When it detects that the water lattice is too "stiff" to allow for efficient proton hopping, it **down-regulates metabolism** to prevent the engine from overheating and seizing. Hypothyroidism isn't a "disease"; it's a **safety governor** on a deuterated engine.

**2\. The "Electronic Winter"**
While we are told the air is getting warmer, humans are living in a **"Non-Native Electronic Winter."

Missing IR-A:** We have replaced the sun's massive infrared (heat) flux with cold blue light. Infrared is what actually "thins" the water. Without it, our internal "climate" is cold and stagnant, regardless of the temperature outside.

**The Metabolic Inversion:** Marine animals are forced into a "speed up" because they are still grounded in the ocean's magnetic flux. Humans, however, are **unplugged and shielded**. We aren't warming; we are **freezing at a cellular level** because we've lost our solar antenna gain.

**3\. The "Tritium/Deuterium" Silt**
If the atmospheric "warming" is actually a symptom of the **Three Shield Failure** (Earth's magnetic field dropping), then the real story isn't heat, it's **radiation and isotopes**. No government wants to admit this now but the 2026 USGS data is clear. ---> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads...amin-d-levels-to-go-up-naturally.28177/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-does-it-take-for-vitamin-d-levels-to-go-up-naturally.28177/page-4)

**The Signal:** The "tsunami" of hypothyroidism is the biological signal that the **"Isotopic Back-pressure"** is now so high that the human engine can no longer "exhale."

**The Propaganda:** Calling it "Global Warming" focuses on a single variable (temperature) while ignoring the **mass-density shift** (Deuterium/Tritium) and the **magnetic collapse**.

**The Mechanic's Diagnosis**

The "warming" narrative is a distraction from the **"Isotopic Clog."** A car engine that is "running hot" because the cooling system is filled with silt isn't "improving its performance", it's failing.
Humans are currently the only "marine animal" that has climbed out of the "magnetic ocean," surrounded itself with nnEMF, and is now wondering why its **internal fire (the thyroid)** is going out.

# Thread: A LESSON ON DEBT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-on-debt.31945/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-27T18:26:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-on-debt.31945/post-363279>

So, if the economy is fueled by liquidity
And liquidity is primarily enabled by debt
How will we ever get out of debt without tanking the economy?

I've just posed one of the deepest structural questions in modern economics to you: Can a debt-based monetary system ever meaningfully “get out of debt” without causing a depression? The short answer is: No, not really, at least not without radical changes to how money itself is created. Here’s why.

1\. Most “money” in the economy is bank-created debt

-   When a bank issues a loan, it creates new deposit money out of thin air (via fractional reserve banking + modern credit creation).
-   Roughly 95–97 % of the broad money supply (M2/M3) in the U.S., UK, Eurozone, etc., originates as bank credit—i.e., someone else’s debt.
-   For the total debt in the system to shrink, that same amount of money must literally disappear (loans get repaid or written off).

2\. Debt repayment = money destruction

-   If everyone tried to pay down debt at the same time (households, corporations, government), the money supply would contract sharply → deflation, falling asset prices, layoffs, bankruptcies → classic debt-deflation spiral (Irving Fisher 1933, still correct).
-   Japan in the 1990s and the U.S. in 2008–2009 both flirted with this; central banks panicked and flooded the system with new liquidity to stop it.

3\. The only ways to reduce system-wide debt without crashing the economyHere are the historically observed (or theoretically possible) escape hatches—none are pretty:

| Method | How it works | Historical examples / notes | Political feasibility today |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Strong real GDP growth | Economy grows faster than debt → debt/GDP falls even if nominal debt rises | U.S. 1945–1980 (post-WWII boom), China 1990–2010 | Very low (demographics + productivity slowdown) |
| Inflation | Moderate–high inflation erodes the real value of existing debt | 1940s–1970s in most Western countries (reduced WWII debt burdens dramatically) | Possible, but central banks now target 2 % |
| Financial repression | Force savers to accept negative real yields (caps on interest rates, etc.) | 1945–1980 again; very effective at liquidating government debt quietly | Politically toxic today |
| Default / debt jubilee | Large-scale write-offs or restructuring | Iceland 2009–2011 (households), Ancient Mesopotamia jubilees, Argentina multiple times | Triggers banking crisis unless perfectly orchestrated |
| Currency devaluation | Works for countries with foreign-currency debt | Asia 1998, Argentina 2002, works only if you owe in dollars/euros and control your own currency | Only for emerging markets |
| Shift to equity-financed growth | Replace debt with equity issuance (stocks, not bonds) | Theoretically ideal, but slow and culturally alien to most corporate CFOs | Possible long-term, not short-term fix |
| Helicopter money / monetization | Central bank permanently finances government spending → de facto debt cancellation via inflation | Closest modern analog: 2020–2022 fiscal/monetary blowout, Japan’s BoJ owning ~50 % of JGB market | Happening in slow motion right now |

4\. The brutal bottom lineIn a purely debt-based monetary system, aggregate debt can never go to zero without collapsing the money supply(unless the government or central bank continuously injects new base money to replace vanishing credit money).
So the system has three stable states:

1.  Growing debt (what we’ve had since ~1980)
2.  High plateau + inflation or repression to erode it slowly (1945–1980 path)
3.  Sharp deleveraging → depression (1930s path)

We keep choosing door #1 because it’s the least painful in the short run and kicks the can past the next election cycle.

5\. What would actually break the cycle?Only two realistic (but radical) options:

-   Move to a full-reserve or sovereign-money system (Chicago Plan, Positive Money proposals) → banks can’t create money, only the central bank can.
-   Permanent overt monetary financing (“People’s QE” / MMT-style deficit spending financed by the central bank with no intent to withdraw the liquidity).

Both are politically almost impossible in the current environment.

TL;DR
We don’t “get out of debt.” We either grow out of it, inflate out of it, repress out of it, or crash trying to delever. Door #4 (controlled inflation + repression) is the path most likely in the 2025–2035 period if history is any guide. The system is built to require ever-increasing debt or periodic resets. There is no clean exit that keeps the current rules intact.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-27T18:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-on-debt.31945/post-363280>

I think the answer is buying Bitcoin for the sovereign individual.

It is how you make fiat money allodial title not subject to fractional reserve banking risks.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-27T18:28:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-on-debt.31945/post-363281>

**What Bitcoin does NOT fix at the system level**

The macro economy still runs almost entirely on debt-based fiat. 97 % of the money supply is still bank credit.

Most people, corporations, and governments are still trapped in the debt → money creation loop. Bitcoin doesn’t pay payroll, mortgages, or taxes for 99.9 % of the population yet.

If private and public debt ever actually gets repaid or defaulted en masse, the money supply still collapses and takes the real economy with it—Bitcoin holders just watch the carnage from the lifeboats with their purchasing power temporarily skyrocketing (1929–1933 goldbug outcome, or 2022–2023 regional-bank scare on steroids).

Governments will not sit idly by while their monetary monopoly evaporates. Expect capital controls, windfall taxes on unrealized gains, outright bans on self-custody in some jurisdictions, or co-option (ETFs, central-bank strategic reserves, etc.). **_The escape hatch gets narrower the more successful Bitcoin becomes and the target on Bitcoiners becomes LARGER._**

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-27T18:31:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-lesson-on-debt.31945/post-363282>

The two realistic endgames

1.  Bitcoin stays small enough that nation-states tolerate it as “digital gold” for the top 0.1–1 %. Most sovereign individuals make it to the other side. The fiat system still eventually implodes, but slowly (this is the base case most Bitcoiners are betting on, not me).
2.  Bitcoin gets big enough that it actually starts displacing fiat credit money at scale → governments go full authoritarian to protect the legacy system (UDHR Article 17 “right to property” gets selectively ignored for private keys). The bigger Bitcoin gets, the higher the probability of a violent clamp-down before it reaches critical mass.

So, buying Bitcoin (and holding your own keys) is currently the best available off-ramp for anyone who has figured out that debt-based fiat has no clean exit. Most economists and fiat champions will miss it on their blueprint of the macro solutions table because it doesn’t deleverage the system—it abandons the system. Which is exactly why it’s so powerful for the individual, and why most people in power hate Bitcoin.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-11-27T18:33:48+0200

# Thread: DARWIN SUCCESS STORY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwin-success-story.31950/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T15:49:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwin-success-story.31950/post-363301>

1830: HMS Beagle stops at Tierra del Fuego, southernmost South America. Charles Darwin encounters the Yaghan people. They're living as hunter-gatherers, wearing minimal clothing in freezing conditions, eating shellfish and seals.

Darwin's assessment: "I could not have believed how wide was the difference between savage and civilised man. It is greater than between a wild and domesticated animal." The British Navy decides to "civilize" them.

They take four Yaghan people back to England: Fuegia Basket, Jemmy Button, York Minster, and Boat Memory (who dies of smallpox immediately). In England, the three survivors are given: Proper clothing Agricultural education Christian instruction English language training "Civilized" manners.

After one year, they're declared "successfully civilized" and returned to Tierra del Fuego with seeds, tools, and instructions to teach their people farming. Darwin returns years later expecting to find a thriving agricultural settlement.

What he finds: Jemmy Button, completely naked, back to hunting and gathering. The agricultural tools abandoned. The seeds never planted. The "civilization" discarded entirely.

Darwin is shocked.

He expected gratitude.

He found rejection.

But Jemmy explains something Darwin doesn't want to hear: "Plenty seal. Plenty fish. Not much work.

Why plant seeds?"

The Yaghan had tried agriculture. For about three months. Then they calculated: Seals provide more calories with less work. Shellfish are abundant year-round.

The cold weather doesn't bother them: they're adapted.

Agriculture means more labor for worse nutrition. They chose to stay "savage." Because "savage" meant working 15 hours per week and eating well. "Civilized" meant working 60 hours per week and eating grain. They weren't stupid. They did the math.

Darwin never understood this.

He died thinking they'd rejected progress out of ignorance.
They'd rejected progress because they understood it perfectly.

It was Survival of the Wiseest. Darwin did not understand how his own gospel was in error

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T15:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwin-success-story.31950/post-363302>

Cross refer this with Joseph Tainter and the collapse on complex societies.

Civilizations collapse slowly as the the marginal returns on complexity vanish and people return to simpler forms of living, productivity declines and eventually some crisis or stress tips the whole thing into a downwards cascade.

But the people on the edges of the collapsing Empires or at the bottom runds of society are often very happy about it. They live simpler Lives unburdened by the previous power / economic structures holding it all together.

It is a bit like Britain/Oz/France/USA 2025 where nobody wants to work and productivity has been flat for 15 years and the only way to hold things together is to invite (or powerlessly accept) people from outside the formal “Empire” into the heart of things and hope it doesn’t go wrong.

And it is not going well.

Down in El Salvador the Simple Salt life has its benefits.

# Thread: Did Menstrual Tracking Birth the Calendar for Humanity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363309>

Where women really the first mathematicians, and our first agriculturists because of human time stamping?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

We like to think of menstrual tracking as a modern technological innovation, but women throughout history and across cultures have always found innovative ways to track their cycles. Our fancy apps that remember everything about our reproductive cycles aren’t the only way to keep track. People with wombs have always wanted to know when they were fertile, when to expect their periods, and when to wonder if they were pregnant.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:15:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363310>

The Ishango bone is an engraved baboon fibula believed to be roughly 20,000 years old. It was found in 1960 on the shore of a lake in northeastern Zaire, what was then the Belgian Congo. Carved on its surface are three rows of notch groupings, understood to indicate 9, 19, 21, 11; 19, 12, 13, 11; and 7, 5, 5, 10, 8, 4, 6, 3. It was determined that the notches represent a six-month lunar calendar. Ethno-mathematician Claudia Zaslavsky claims the Ishango Bone served as a menstrual tracker

“The Ishango notched bone and similar artifacts originated with a woman’s need for a lunar calendar to keep track of her monthly cycles. It is generally conceded that women were the first agriculturalists, and in that capacity they may have needed a lunar calendar,” Zaslavsky wrote. Here is a picture of that blockchain codified in a bone.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:16:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363311>

Scholar Alexander Marshack described the groups of notch markings in this way: “... neither so placed and organized as to be decorative, nor sufficiently structured to be tattoo marks. We assume, therefore, that they are ‘storied’ and somehow notational or symbolic, and represent one of the forms of intentional marking used by man before writing. They may be counting-marks of months or menstrual periods, or marks of ‘magic’ or ‘ritual’ made as prayer or offering in a storied participation in the desire for a successful pregnancy.”

But Zaslavsky sees it as a much more open-and-shut case.

“Who but a woman keeping track of her cycles would need a lunar calendar? Zaslavsky quipped. “When I raised this question with a colleague having similar mathematical interests, he suggested that early agriculturalists might have kept such records. However, he was quick to add that women were probably the first agriculturalists. They discovered cultivation while the men were out hunting, So, whichever way you look at it, women were undoubtedly the first mathematicians!”

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:17:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363312>

The oldest notched bone unearthed so far was found in southern Africa and bears 29 incisions. It is estimated to be about 37,000 years old. Though the mysteries of ovulation, menstruation, and conception were not uncovered by science until around the 19th century, before that time most people would’ve understood menstruation as a cycle; they knew that regular bleeding indicated fertility and lack of pregnancy. Recipes for treating lack of menstruation, or amenorrhea, have peppered medical texts and household recipe books since ancient times.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363313>

In Maori culture, “according to the knowledge of our ancestors and elders … the moon is the permanent husband, or true husband, of all women, because women menstruate when the moon appears.”

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:19:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363314>

The Suri of southwest Ethiopia have long [kept track of their menstrual cycle](https://www.jstor.org/stable/24476707) by counting the days with knots or beads on a small rope worn beneath their clothing. Each knot or bead represents one day and the number and kinds of knots and beads signify the different phases in their menstrual cycle. Every day, they untie one knot, and start the process over on the first day of their period.

Other traditional calendar methods evolved to include making notches on sticks to track periods and pregnancies—marking a new stick each month.

In the Native American Yurok tribe, women would place a stick a day into a basket. If they became pregnant, they would drop one stick a month into a second basket until they got to ten sticks. In Siberian cultures, women were the custodians of the calendar; the Nganasan women would sew colorful strips of fabric to a garment to count the ten months of pregnancy passing.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:19:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363315>

In Scandinavia, they used slender wooden staffs as perpetual calendars, with notches for the days of the year and descriptive characters for solstices, equinoxes, and holidays. Called primstav sticks, from the Latin for “first appearance of a new moon;” and “prim” being Old Norse for “new moon.” They are also called runstavs for their use of runic symbols.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363316>

In _[The Woman's Encyclopedia of Myths and Secrets](https://archive.org/stream/womansencycloped00walkrich/womansencycloped00walkrich_djvu.txt)_, feminist author Barbara G. Walker posits that calendars themselves originated with women’s tracking of their cycles: “It has been shown that calendar consciousness developed first in women, because of their natural menstrual body calendar, correlated with observations of the moon’s phases. Chinese women established a lunar calendar 3000 years ago, dividing the celestial sphere into 28 stellar ‘mansions’ through which the moon passed. Among the Maya of Central America, every woman knew the great Maya calendar had first been based on her menstrual cycles.”

What’s more, Walker notes, the Gaelic words for “menstruation” and “calculation” are the same—miosach and miosachan—and the Romans called the calculation of time “menstruation,” meaning “knowledge of the menses.” The words “rite,” “sabbath,” and “ritual” are also all derived from words pertaining to menstruation.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:21:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-menstrual-tracking-birth-the-calendar-for-humanity.31952/post-363317>

“Women were the first observers of the basic periodicity of nature, the periodicity upon which all later scientific observations were made,” wrote William Irwin Thompson in _The Time Falling Bodies Take to Light: Mythology, Sexuality and the Origins of Culture_.

It may seem outlandish to suppose fertility tracking gave rise to calendars. We’re used to “feminine” dabblings being labeled as less important, always coming second. Men were out doing the important business of hunting while women were just faffing about at home. But developing a system in which women could maintain a relatively accurate accounting of their menstrual cycles was vital to the human blockchain of time and this birthed the very existence of the human race from Mitochondrial Eve onward.

**CITES**

_[The Curse: A Cultural History of Menstruation](https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Curse/njfQfrMr31EC)_, by Janice Delaney, et al. University of Illinois Press, 1988.

_[Blood Relations: Menstruation and the Origins of Culture](https://www.google.com/books/edition/Blood_Relations/ga6OAQAAQBAJ?hl=en&gbpv=0)_, by Chris Knight. Yale University Press, 2013.

_[Blood Magic: The Anthropology of Menstruation](https://www.google.com/books/edition/Blood_Magic/W99hIP6HIOYC)_, edited by Alma Gottlieb & Thomas C. T. Buckley. University of California Press, 1988.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: SARAH STEWART's JOURNAL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sarah-stewarts-journal.31953/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-28T17:31:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sarah-stewarts-journal.31953/post-363318>

**SARAH STEWART's JOURNAL**

Sarah Stewart's 1937 journal was found at David Ferrie's murder scene by Jim Garrison, for those ignorant in the history. This journal was the first of its kind to link viral particles to oncogenesis. I detailed all this in the first Danny Jones's podcast when speaking about SV40.

Here is the part of the story we could not get to.

**Sara Stewart's** interest in the viral origins of cancer was linked to **Elisabeth Wollman's** (and her husband Eugène, and later their son Élie and François Jacob) work on lysogeny.

The connection is primarily conceptual and influential:

**Lysogeny as a Model:** Elisabeth and Eugène Wollman were pioneers in studying lysogeny, the phenomenon where a bacteriophage's (virus that infects bacteria) genetic material integrates into the host bacterium's DNA and lies dormant as a prophage. The host cell continues to live and reproduce, passing the prophage to daughter cells, until the virus is induced to enter the lytic cycle and produce new viruses.

**Influence on Cancer Research:** André Lwoff, a colleague who continued the Wollmans' work after their death during the Holocaust, explicitly pointed out the value of the lysogeny system in shedding light on human diseases, including cancer. The concept of a dormant, integrated viral genome that could later be activated resonated with the idea of cancer viruses, which might remain latent in a host for a long time before causing tumors.

**Direct Mention in Research:** Sara Stewart, along with Bernice Eddy, successfully demonstrated that the polyoma virus could cause various tumors in rodents (leading to the name SE polyoma virus). Sara Stewart's publications referenced the work of Jacob and Wollman (presumably Élie and François Jacob, building on Elisabeth and Eugène's work) on the "Induction of phage" in the context of viral carcinogenesis.

**Episomes and Tumors:** François Jacob and Élie Wollman hypothesized that "episomes" (genetic elements like temperate bacteriophages that could exist independently or integrate into the chromosome) might be responsible for cell differentiation changes and potentially tumor formation.
Thus, the fundamental understanding of viral integration and latency developed through the Wollmans' work on bacteriophages provided a crucial conceptual framework that informed and supported the research into cancer-causing viruses like those studied by Sara Stewart.

In 1937 Sarah Stewart and Mary Sherman picked up the scent of Wollman's work before she and her spouse were killed by the architects of the Transfer Agreement.

After the WW2, Élie and one of the Wollmans’ research students continued the couple’s research project on bacteriophage lysogeny (picking up where their unpublished manuscript left off), culminating in a Nobel Prize.

### DLO — 2025-12-23T05:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-that-underestimate-ai.31954/post-364160>

#### [**Robert Kiyosaki**](https://www.facebook.com/RobertKiyosaki?__cft__[0]=AZYA1CXFTDf9Y7cUN62G_IG6ZKhAIVo0cgNAL4e4aT7rbVYKWeX-EMDkvC-oWn5yZni-W02rxmLsDKr34bNoMIJzJTTgOXZac1o6VVnWL8ESZ2YJo9Gc-NL33BjFo8CjtAgNYY3fW9HT0dOV3pvB7EfUkAB2JewCBDT0XNmwqUrBAD5C9eodlc4GKXaQuMldGnI&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)​

[srSpedontoc6fa16hmhm2t1amg1mcc7l586m00u3tu3923i7fllm046hh770](https://www.facebook.com/?__cft__[0]=AZYA1CXFTDf9Y7cUN62G_IG6ZKhAIVo0cgNAL4e4aT7rbVYKWeX-EMDkvC-oWn5yZni-W02rxmLsDKr34bNoMIJzJTTgOXZac1o6VVnWL8ESZ2YJo9Gc-NL33BjFo8CjtAgNYY3fW9HT0dOV3pvB7EfUkAB2JewCBDT0XNmwqUrBAD5C9eodlc4GKXaQuMldGnI&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R#?bac) ·

People Think AI Is Making Everything Cheaper.
It Isn’t. It’s Just Moving the Money.
Imagine firing your copywriter.
Good writer.
Talented.
Costs you $100,000 a year.
Now an AI tool does 80–90% of that work for $30 a month.
Sounds like progress, right?
But here’s what actually happened.
Your electricity bill went up.
Your cloud and software bills went up.
Your “AI tools” stack costs more than your old team.
You didn’t save money.
The money just moved.
And if you think this story is about copywriters, you’re missing the real lesson.
AI is driving the cost of labor toward zero in many fields:
• Writing
• Translation
• Coding
• Customer support
• Design
• Research
Economists call this deflation at the margin.
The cost of producing one more unit of work collapses.
Historically, that should make things cheaper for everyone.
But that’s not what’s happening.
Why?
Because while AI is deflating what you sell, it’s inflating what you depend on.
AI doesn’t run on air.
It runs on:
• Data centers
• Chips
• Electricity
• Cooling
• Bandwidth
• Cloud infrastructure
Large AI models consume enormous amounts of energy and compute — not once, but continuously.
Training is expensive.
Inference is ongoing.
Scaling never stops.
Who pays for that?
Not the founders.
You do.
Through:
• Higher utility rates
• Rising cloud bills
• More subscriptions
• Higher cost of digital infrastructure
So here’s the real dynamic:
AI deflates labor income,
but concentrates capital income.
Your skills become cheaper.
The infrastructure becomes more valuable.
That’s not an accident.
That’s how every technological revolution works.
The printing press made books cheap.
But then:
• Everyone had to be literate
• Then everyone needed schooling
• Then a degree
• Then a master’s degree
The cost of staying relevant kept rising.
Same pattern. New technology.
AI increases productivity.
But instead of lowering prices, most of the gains flow upward:
• Higher margins
• Greater market concentration
• More power for infrastructure owners
That’s why the winners in AI aren’t freelancers.
They’re:
• Chip makers
• Cloud providers
• Data center owners
• Energy producers
• Platform monopolies
They own the picks and shovels.
Everyone else is running faster just to stay in place.
Here’s the part most people don’t understand:
This isn’t a worker problem.
It’s a positioning problem.
If your income depends on selling hours, tasks, or interchangeable skills — AI is competition.
If your income depends on owning assets, systems, or leverage — AI is a multiplier.
I’m not anti-AI.
I use it.
But I understand the game.
Every major technological “revolution” is also a wealth transfer.
This one just has better marketing.
So the real question isn’t:
“How do I use AI?”
It’s:
“Where am I positioned in the value chain?”
Because in every gold rush, most people dig.
And a few sell shovels.
That’s where the real money has always been.

### DLO — 2025-12-24T05:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-that-underestimate-ai.31954/post-364186>

#### [**Robert Kiyosaki**](https://www.facebook.com/RobertKiyosaki?__cft__[0]=AZb_o3b-tWynu9A8Bh-7wUw1G-dOkc07v-3MUwvJBFMBDHsQtVnelaBEjFcJJdBh4QjZWTba7iB0nUeM_uERFBR5V4j6LM_k4EfGqz7hVf7oTqdwoZcxkZvl5GaAu8GsH2Flqipo6ZBVShVfCSepSpHfonh96laU21B4IC73_CnX7VTzFh1lzEsh0qsildpVvi4&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)​

[edptosrSno1t7c6ac651mau0145fa3839hhhu59f1418hg15tm0alm40021c](https://www.facebook.com/?__cft__[0]=AZb_o3b-tWynu9A8Bh-7wUw1G-dOkc07v-3MUwvJBFMBDHsQtVnelaBEjFcJJdBh4QjZWTba7iB0nUeM_uERFBR5V4j6LM_k4EfGqz7hVf7oTqdwoZcxkZvl5GaAu8GsH2Flqipo6ZBVShVfCSepSpHfonh96laU21B4IC73_CnX7VTzFh1lzEsh0qsildpVvi4&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R#?aeg) ·

The rich use AI the same way they use debt.
The poor use AI the same way they use Google.
Most people don’t understand what that means yet.
But they will.
Poor people use AI to do their work.
\- To write emails.
\- To summarize documents.
\- To answer questions faster.
That’s helpful.
But it doesn’t change their income.
It just makes them a more efficient employee.
Rich people use AI the way they use debt — as leverage.
They don’t ask,
“How can this help me work harder?”
They ask,
“How can this replace labor?”
“How can this scale without more people?”
“How can this run 24/7 without me?”
That’s the same mindset they use with money.
Poor people use debt to buy liabilities.
Cars. Phones. Lifestyle.
Rich people use debt to buy assets.
Businesses. Systems. Cash flow.
AI is no different.
Use AI to search, and you stay dependent.
Use AI to build systems, and you create income.
Schools teach people to avoid debt.
\- They teach people to “use AI responsibly.”
\- They don’t teach leverage.
\- They don’t teach ownership.
The rich aren’t trying to be better workers with AI.
They’re trying to own the machine.
And in the AI age, whoever owns the system controls the money

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-in-boys-lower-iq-queer-behavior-and-shortened-longevity.31960/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-11-30T05:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-in-boys-lower-iq-queer-behavior-and-shortened-longevity.31960/post-363378>

**LOW T in boys = Lower IQ, Queer behavior, and shortened longevity.**

Tonight's physics/biology factoid: The average indiginous man in Boliva is 5'3. The shortest humans tend to live longer. Shortness is linked to sex steroid hormone production and anti-Kasha rules. Testosterone is linked with IRA and NIR light exposure to make DDW from the heme protein CCO in the matrix. This is key in humans and explains why when men age heteroplasmy rises and more red light from the sun is needed to maintain their T.

This country is at the 10-21 degrees latitude South and is well within the tropics. The elevation of this country, where 90% of its population density is ridiculously high. It ranges 9000 feet to 19,500 ft were 90% of people live. This elevation allows for massive assimilation of UV light. The main features of Bolivia's geography include the Altiplano, a highland plateau of the Andes, and Lake Titicaca, the highest navigable lake on Earth, which is shared with Peru.

The most prominent feature of the Altiplano is the large lake at its northern end, Lake Titicaca. At 3,811 m (12,503 ft) above sea level, it is the highest commercially navigable body of water in the world. Some of the oldest healthy people on Earth come from Tribes in this region. The eastern lowlands include all of Bolivia north and east of the Andes. Although comprising over two-thirds of the national territory, the region is sparsely populated.

The tallest people on Earth live in Netherlands: The Netherlands is the tallest country in the world, where the average height of men is an incredible six feet. The latitude is 51-53.5 degrees North. It is outside the tropics and does not have a lot of UV power.

The Netherlands is geographically a very low and flat country, with about 26% of its area and 21% of its population located below sea level, and only about 50% of its land exceeding one metre above sea level. The quantum yield of this country is quite low because of its latitude and altitude. The Dutch population had a boom after the Second World War, but now has a low birth rate that has caused extremely low population growth: 2025 saw the lowest absolute population growth since 1900.

# Thread: How bad are TV towers?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-11-30T16:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-363392>

I recently moved to Florida and pretty much every EMF measurement I've made is excellent in / around my home.

E fields close to 0, M field 0.03-0.15 mG range (when all power is off).
RF from Safe and Sound Pro II peaks are in the 0.1-2 uW/m2 range, lots of mature tree cover on the property.
However, when I use my Esmog Spion 5G meter, with the HF antenna in the 200-600 MHz, I see measurements of 45-180 uW/m2.

Some notes:
\- Looks like SS Pro II can go down to 200 MHz, but I've read that it's most accurate 400 MHz and above.
\- Esmog Spion generally reads much higher (like 2-10x higher) than Safe and Sound EM3 or Safe and Sound Pro II. Maybe it measures peaks better?
\- Doing an antenna search around, I'm nowhere near any cell tower, whether 5G or not. I can empirically validate this by my terrible cell signal here.

The one thing I do find is a TV tower broadcasting at 158 MHz only 2-3 miles away from my home.
How bad are TV tower frequencies for mitochondrial health? Everything else measures perfectly here.

It's probably impossible to avoid TV towers as they have much lower frequencies than cell towers and they surely cover way more of the US than cell towers.

### AlbertW — 2026-01-22T17:33:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-365733>

Curious if anybody else has an Esmog Spion 5G meter and has measured if they see way higher readings on Esmog than SSPro 2 (likely due to TV)

### AlbertW — 2026-01-26T01:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-365870>

I bought a small spectrum analyzer (tinySA Ultra+) to figure out what RF bands are actually present around my home.

**What I did:** I used the tinySA’s Channel power measurement (integrates power over a chosen bandwidth). There were a couple of narrow peaks in the <200 MHz region (consistent with FM and a VHF TV channel), and there are also one or more **wide 6-MHz-type blocks** higher up in frequency (consistent with UHF TV channels centered near ~500 MHz). In the “hot spots” in my house, the channel-power integral in the UHF-TV-ish region was roughly an order of magnitude+ higher than most other bands as measured at the analyzer input. (I understand this is received power into the analyzer, not directly “power density in space.”)

**Why I’m confused:** My Safe & Sound Pro II generally reads low (single-digit µW/m²), while the 5G Esmog Spion meter can read much higher. The Safe & Sound Pro II’s published specs say it has an **e**ffective measurement range of 200 MHz–8 GHz and a true response range of 400 MHz–7.2 GHz (±6 dB). The 500 MHz range is well within the true response range...

I know that mapping received power on the tinySA Ultra+ to real power density isn't trivial due to antenna coupling / response changes with frequency. But the tinySA's received power in the 500 MHz UHF TV range is 95%+ of the total received power... Can the antenna coupling / response changes with frequency be varying that much? Just don't understand how SS Pro II can miss out on this.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-05T02:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366372>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=365870)
>
> Sound Pro II’s published specs say it has an **e**ffective measurement range of 200 MHz–8 GHz and a true response range of 400 MHz–7.2 GHz (±6 dB). The 500 MHz range is well within the true response range...

Part of the answer here - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-–-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524)

### AlbertW — 2026-02-05T03:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366376>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=366372)
>
> Part of the answer here - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-–-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524)

Yes I looked at exactly this. For 500 MHz range it’s -4 to -5 dB ish, which is at most a 3x understatement. I don’t get how I can then see SSPro measuring 0-3 uW/m2 range but esmog claims 180. Maybe esmog’s response curve is even worse? I checked with an HF59B and consistently got 3-5 uW/m2.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-05T04:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366379>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366376)
>
> Yes I looked at exactly this. For 500 MHz range it’s -4 to -5 dB ish, which is at most a 3x understatement. I don’t get how I can then see SSPro measuring 0-3 uW/m2 range but esmog claims 180. Maybe esmog’s response curve is even worse? I checked with an HF59B and consistently got 3-5 uW/m2.

I will explain why when I have a moment free.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366536>

At ~ 500MHz WL is ~ 60cm.. so antenna coupling would dominate the measurement error. S &S would.use a very small internal broadband antenna..Thus coupling would not be great and impacted by the polarization.. the - 4 to - 5db good plane wave coupling and does not factor in the "pre-detection" losses.. The gigahertz internals are different they use a better scaled antenna with a superheterodyne receiver with rms/log style detection etc..The outlier would be Esmog.. Tinysa from what I looked at would measure received power at the resonant ext antenna (not spatial power density).

Personally, I would rely upon the Gigahertz Solutions meter. Assuming your model measures the frequency.. Can you link the Esmog meter have you got specs on this?

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:36:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366537>

Also, what external antenna did you use on the GS meter? I thought the stock ones cut off ~ 700 but the internals support down to 27.. From memory the lower frequency ext antenna was UBB27 model..

### Mat.I — 2026-02-08T10:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366538>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=366537)
>
> Also, what external antenna did you use on the GS meter? I thought the stock ones cut off ~ 700 but the internals support down to 27.. From memory the lower frequency ext antenna was UBB27 model..

Effective element lengths are typically wavlength /4 or 3 for accuracy.. You will still pick larger up, but just less accurate. 700 = 43cm.. so the standard antenna would likely be based on an accuracy element of /4 or 3 of WL.

### AlbertW — 2026-02-08T16:02:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366540>

[@Mat.I](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26512/) Tracks for the info on the antenna details. Very helpful. I will investigate more.

Can you elaborate on what you mean by pre detection losses in SS?

And yes Tinysa indeed just measures received antenna power. I wanted to determine the source of my high esmog readings.

I am indeed using UBB27 omni antenna with HF59B which goes down to 27 MHz.

This is the esmog meter: [https://priggen.com/5g-esmog-spion-electrosmog-meter-50hz-8ghz](https://priggen.com/5g-esmog-spion-electrosmog-meter-50hz-8ghz)

What’s interesting to me is that with the SS, I see significant attenuation inside a canopy at my house, from 1-2 uW/m2 down to 0.01 or often 0.000. The GS with UBB27 shows barely any attenuation in measurement, so I’m guessing very low frequency waves are really the main problem at my place…radio, TV, etc. Always in the 4-5 uW/m2 everywhere in the house.

### Mat.I — 2026-02-10T02:23:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-366619>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366540)
>
> Can you elaborate on what you mean by pre detection losses in SS?

Explained above - SS uses a very small internal broadband antenna which is very small electrically relative to the wavelength you are measuring (this results in pre-detection losses).. Why the mismatch in test conditions accuracy vs what you have measured was explained in the thread I linked re: the test tone generator used.. is a clean CW/narrow band test signal indicative of real world test conditions experienced out in the field? Case of garbage in = garbage out.

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=366540)
>
> I am indeed using UBB27 omni antenna with HF59B which goes down to 27 MHz.

That is good.

I cringe when people rely on S&S as their only and primary meter (in the professional space). Good broadband activity indicator (like a sniffer I guess) so it has it's place as a tool but should not be relied upon for quantitative power density measurements in isolation IMO. Some won't like this at all (that means they do not appreciate the basic physics behind it).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-19T15:39:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-367149>

Your government and its corporate partners are the octopus strangling public health. [https://x.com/prospertarian/status/2024469678962389438](https://x.com/prospertarian/status/2024469678962389438)

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-19T15:41:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-bad-are-tv-towers.31961/post-367150>

Palantir just abruptly left Denver yesterday and re incorporated to Florida. Not good news for the East coast of Fla.

uncle Jack is my doctor now, he just doesn’t know it lol

1889 --> AC Rollout --> Russian Flu
1918 --> Radiowave Tech --> Spanish Flu
1957 --> Radar Tech --> Asian Flu
1968 --> Satellite --> Hong Kong Flu
1981 --> 1G --> AIDS
1992 --> 2G --> Influenza/Hantavirus
2002 --> 3G --> Bird Flu/SARS
2009 --> 4G --> Swine Flu
2020 --> 5G --> COVID
2030 --> 6G --> ???

### [Home | Armstrong Economics](https://www.armstrongeconomics.com/)

-   Armstrong built an “Economic Confidence Model” that tracks repeating business and confidence cycles: a core 8.6‑year cycle (3,141 days, tying to π×1000π×1000) nested in longer 51.6‑year and ~309‑year waves.

Now if you want to pin down an actual date for the next pandemic-like crisis, you take “when Covid hit” as March 11, 2020 (the WHO’s official pandemic declaration), then 8.6 years later lands in late 2028, so we are a bit over 6 years in and roughly 2.5 years away from that 8.6‑year point.

## 8.6 years from the start of​

Armstrong’s basic cycle is 8.6 years, which is 3,141 days. You can approximate 8.6 years in calendar time as 8 years + about 7.2 months.
Using simple calendar approximations:

-   From March 11, 2020
    -   +8 years → March 11, 2028
    -   +0.6 of a year (~7.2 months) → around late October 2028
    -   So 8.6 years from the WHO pandemic declaration is roughly late October 2028.

# Thread: My 12 year old son

### josephine — 2025-12-03T07:56:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-12-year-old-son.31973/post-363552>

Hello, I'm not sure where I should be posting this or if I should booking a session with Dr Jack? Please advice if so.

I have a 12 yr old child (almost 13.) When he was eight, his Grade 3 teacher told me he wouldn’t be able to cope with Grade 4 because he couldn’t keep up. I had him fully assessed. The specialist said he was normal but had severely slow processing speed.

I researched everything I could (before I looked into Dr Kruse work) and ended up buying a hyperbaric oxygen chamber. I gave him around 110 sessions at 1.4 ATA with roughly 30% oxygen. The change was remarkable — it was like a light switched on. Everyone saw how much faster he became. When he was reassessed, his processing speed had improved by 39%. It was genuinely life-changing. He went from getting C’s and D’s to A’s and B’s, his piano and clarinet improvement was remarkable, he flourished.

This year, at age 12, his teacher mentioned he was a bit slower than the others. Over the past month, however, he has become noticeably slower, and he has pointed this out himself. In class, for example, there might be 30 questions — every student will finish, and he’ll still be on number 12.

He’s about to start high school, and I’m extremely worried. I have 7 weeks to help him (after that much less time). I’m also unsure what to do about HBOT. Jack often says HBOT isn’t ideal for most people, so I’m scared to repeat a treatment that worked brilliantly last time as I don't understand how to control for the dangers.

My son also had a palate expander this year (before i knew more) and has been mouth-taping at night. The expander opened his airway, stopped the constant stuffy nose, and even ended the bed-wetting — so part of me wonders if simply getting more oxygen every day has already changed things and might be affecting how he responds?

I keep thinking: should I repeat HBOT but only after getting everything else in place? Would this be okay to do? I'm not sure of what protocol. We live on a sub tropical island outside brisbane, so apart from being in the prison of Oz, it is great for the essential of sunlight. These are the things I'm thinking with question marks where i dont know.

• sunrise
• get a full tan
• I live on the beach, so lots of grounding
• he has a daylight computer
• I have a full body redlight panel?
• I have a redlight cranial helmet with Vlight (bought for Parkinson’s)?
• would a keto diet help?
• cold plunging for a 12 yr old?
• I have hydrogen generator – inhalation?
• hydrogen baths (he loves them)?
• sauna (I have a Clearlight)?
• Hbot at highly pressures, or higher oxygen, no oxygen, lower pressure?

We already do zero blue light past sundown.

Info about his birth and his mother (me)

Born in Paris France, 48 hr birth, traumatic (home birth gone wrong), induction, forceps, most likely slight oxygen deprivation at birth (caused the slow processing?)
Breast fed 2 yrs, Zero Vacinne's, but vit K shot at hospital. Lived in a toxic soup of high EMF's, blue lights at night, black mold and no grounding for first 7 years of his life. Then we moved to the island 5 years ago. House checked, low EMF, but still have a wifi service (need to hardwire)

Myself, had my son at age 40, asthmatic (lived in hospitals as child) , lactose intolerent. mold issues, severe vaccine injury at age 9 (Typhoid live virus) immue system never the same, lived like a vampire before discovering Jack Kruse, now I'm a sun and nature worshipper.

ANY help would be sso appriciated!!! And if I need to book a session pls let me know.

His children took over and have destroyed the USA via bigHarma and banking policies to pay the country back for breaking up his Oil Trust in Standard Oil.

If you knew evolution history you'd realize how bad Big Harma is raping humanity with nonsense science.

Evolution of Endogenous Melanin: A Photonic-Adaptive Intro to Evolution

From atomic fundamentals, melanin's evolution reflects life's adaptation to photonic and oxidative pressures, transitioning from surface protector to internal sculptor in complex eukaryotes.

Pre-GOE (~>3.5 Ga): Absent ozone, Earth's surface bathed in intense UV (290-400 nm) bombarded early cells. Ancient melanins evolved before life did, creating polymeric semiconductors from dirty chemisty on the surface of Earth from phenolic precursors filled with aromatic amino acids. The first two domains of life come on board around 3.4-4.0 bya and start incorparating allomelanins and through a quantum evolution newer versions with new abilities emerged in prokaryotes/eukaryotes (fungi) as UV shields over a billion years, absorbing photons to prevent DNA damage and radical formation. Fossil melanin traces (1.6 Ga) suggest early roles in energy dissipation, predating complex life which occured via radiosynthesis. It appears radiosynthesis predates any photosynthesis on Earth at its origins.

GOE (~2.4 Ga): Cyanobacterial O2 surge created "dirty" chemistry where ROS spikes and bioavailable transition metals (Fe, Cu, Mn, Mo, Ca and deuterium) fueling Fenton reactions. Melanin evolved enhanced chelation of these metals, binding them to buffer ROS while enabling detox (via superoxide scavenging). Melanin evolved to use photons to release these metals in a controlled fashion to control early biochemical reactions that occur from 3.5 -2.0 billion years ago. This evolution does not stop until organisms begin to optimized aerobic metabolism in the GOE, allowing eukaryotic complexity amid oxidative bottlenecks; melanin acted as an "electronic sink", and as such, allowed life to bridge Earth's anaerobic origins to aerobic an world in the GOE.

Post-KT (~66 Ma): Asteroid impact triggered "impact winter" which led to a global dust/aerosols blocked sunlight for months-years, collapsing photosynthesis, exterminating 75% species (including non-avian dinosaurs). Sudden sunlight resurgence (1-10 ka post-impact) flooded survivors with UV/visible flux, favoring rapid radiations in mammals/birds/angiosperms. Complex eukaryotes leveraged neuropsin (OPN5, UV-A sensor at 380 nm, evolved ~500 Ma in vertebrates but specialized post-160 Ma) to entrain clocks and sense photonic recovery.

This drove neural crest innovation: Surface melanin-producing melanocytes migrated inward (via SOX10/WNT/EDN3 signaling) to sculpt tissues, via internal pigmentation for radiosynthesis, metal chelation, splitting of water, thermoregulation, camouflage, and optical electronic signaling in rebuilding ecosystems. Endogenous melanin thus internalized for complexity, enabling vertebrate diversification amid post-extinction niches.

Melanin's broadband absorption evolved for photonic purity, minimizing the dirty chemistry of Nitrogen/Sulfur/Oxygen interference in UPE signaling, turning cells into optical electronic sensors post-GOE/KT fluxes.

# Thread: Advice for Reversing Pelvic Subluxation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### haileyweston — 2025-12-07T19:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363669>

Hello, I’m looking to help a family member who’s in her 20s and had just gone through an accident and broke her pelvis. She had screws placed in her SI joints but still had problems and went to see many doctors who denied her complaint of constant low back and butt pain especially on the right but somehow managed to find an ortho surgeon who found she had a significant posterior subluxation (~1.1cm) and arthritis in her left ilium from the first ortho surgeon not placing long enough screws in her pelvis. There is also a ~1cm leg length discrepancy but she can’t feel it whilst walking. Unlike prior to the accident, she can’t sit for long periods of time without hurting and finds standing to be more comfortable. She’s refusing SI fusion surgery due to potential downstream complications and distrust in the medical system. She already knows a little of Dr. Kruse’s work and has never missed a sunrise, focused on grounding and seafood and avoids nnEMF and artificial lights and working at night as much as possible but is unsure of whether she can reverse her subluxation and if so, what else can she do and what activities she should avoid moving forward. We tried researching everything we could but couldn’t find anything specific for pelvic subluxations. Second surgeon has already removed the screws in her pelvis to see if that resolves the pain (short answer: it doesn’t). She doesn’t have any health issues prior to this accident. If she needs to book a consult, please let me know so I can pass the info to her. Thanks.

Dr. Kruse, thank you for your response. We are familiar with this diagram from your blog posts and are aware that circadian biology is important in bone metabolism but we were wondering how and why this bone subluxation occurred and whether photorepair using the sun is the major thing left that is needed to reverse this subluxation. Would any exercise help reverse this? If you have any webinar or Q&A that explains this, it would be tremendously helpful. Every orthopedic surgeon we’ve talked to said it’s irreversible and most don’t know how to explain how it happened but something tells us that may not be the case here.

### digitald3 — 2025-12-10T16:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363755>

I was talking to an ER doc yesterday and showed him the xrays of his wife's worn jaw joint and I said that the other side is starting to catch up and he mentioned, the pelvis, and said whenever I see a fractured pelvis in the ER I always look to the other side as its a ring and a ring doesn't break in just one place, if it does the whole ring deforms.. so it sounds like she had surgery to the actual fracture and the other side is out of whack now too... In the right environment the body can heal.. where does your family member live? Sounds like she needs the tropics with feet and hands in the sand every morning and lots and lots of sun on her pelvis and dark dark nights.. this most likely won't happen in North America

### haileyweston — 2025-12-10T16:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363757>

> [digitald3 said:](/goto/post?id=363755)
>
> I was talking to an ER doc yesterday and showed him the xrays of his wife's worn jaw joint and I said that the other side is starting to catch up and he mentioned, the pelvis, and said whenever I see a fractured pelvis in the ER I always look to the other side as its a ring and a ring doesn't break in just one place, if it does the whole ring deforms.. so it sounds like she had surgery to the actual fracture and the other side is out of whack now too... In the right environment the body can heal.. where does your family member live? Sounds like she needs the tropics with feet and hands in the sand every morning and lots and lots of sun on her pelvis and dark dark nights.. this most likely won't happen in North America

Her pelvis actually broke on both of the SI joints and multiple places at the front so she had a pelvic ex fix as well, but somehow the subluxation only occurred on the left SI joint, which had more screws than the right…. She’s living in northern Australia where the accident happened and the subluxation was found by the radiologist about 2 months after her initial pelvic surgery while she was still staying at the hospital but the other doctors in Australia didn’t tell us about it and we didn’t find out about it until after a year later when we decided to find a doctor outside of Australia who pointed out the huge problem.

And thank you very much for the advice. If that’s all it takes to reverse this subluxation, we will go ahead with the advice. We’ve started becoming our own ‘doctors’ after completely losing trust in the medical system through this incident but it’s been difficult to find any research on how and why this happened and what could work until my family member stumbled across Dr. Kruse’s work and decided to give it a try.

### digitald3 — 2025-12-11T02:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-for-reversing-pelvic-subluxation.31987/post-363773>

I’m not an expert on the pelvis. But know there are lots of things connected to it. I did take gross anatomy and dissected one. So a pelvis fracture is a big deal. I wouldn’t imagine a surgeon could put it back together without some problems. I’m sure there is a surgeon that will tell you they can. But I have my doubts. If it was my family member I’d probably explore physical therapy and myofascial release. Even yoga at the beach safely. That said the repair and regeneration codes are laid out in this forum. Light water and magnetism. Northern Australia may have the light and water but be deficient in magnetism. Which is why she may need less desert and more tropics

Cohn was a hardcore pervert homosexual. How did he use this? As a wedge to get rid of McCarthy while weakening the US military to work for Israeli interests. In NYC when gay legislation was proposed to change the the cityscape Cohn actually said in the media he would oppose New York’s first gay rights legislation by arguing that “homosexual teachers are a grave threat to our children. This was him baiting the hook again for depravity. Another psy ops success.

He had used this trick to take down McCarthy once the useful idiot for Tel Aviv was neutralized.

How did Cohn use McCarthy? He used the TOP Gun Strategy. That is why it was shown in the movie decades later. Bring your enemies closer and kill them.

Cohn made sure on TV in the 1950s to make sure there nothing discreet about the Senator henchman’s tactics. Cohn goal was simple. To infiltrate the military with commie fascist who were gay. That is what a subversive zionist wants. To weaken the enemy. On TV Cohn threatened to “wreck the Army” and warned the Secretary of the Army he would be “through”. Senator McCarthy, whose committee had already, at Cohn’s legal urging, launched an investigation into alleged Communist infiltration at the Fort Monmouth military base, accused the Army of using “blackmail” and holding Mr Schine “hostage”.

What did Time magazine do to bait the hook for Cohn? They pulled out Bernays playbook and portrayed Schine as a rich, handsome Greek God. Go back and look at how Time described him. Time magazine called Schine a man with the “build and features of a junior-grade Greek god.” Why? Cohn knew SunTzu book well. Many knew he was a hardcore homosexual with deep proclivities because of his FBI dossier held by Hoover. He lead the enemy to believe that he was afraid of being outed a gay man. And if he was, he knew the politicians would come after him. Top Gun style.

Hiow did Cohn kill McCarthy once he became useless to Israel?

The moment in the hearings that historians have said to have led to Senator McCarthy’s fall was with his confrontation with the Army’s ram-rod chief counsel, Joseph Welch. After the Senator had attacked a young lawyer from Mr Welch’s law firm, he responded: “Until this moment, Senator, I think I never really gauged your cruelty or your recklessness.” McCarthy felt protected by Cohn's attacking style so he adopted it. Sadly, MCCarthy was no Cohn. As the Wisconsin senator attempted to renew his attack, Mr Welch cut him off with the now-famous and devastating line: “Senator; you've done enough. Have you no sense of decency, sir? At long last, have you left no sense of decency?”

The Psy Ops from FBI Hoover: A "pixie" Cohn gave the FBI to use.

Questioning a member of Senator McCarthy’s staff, Mr Welch asked repeatedly about the origin of a disputed picture that had been placed in evidence, eventually asking whether it “came from a pixie”. When the Senator foolishly interjected to ask what a pixie was, Mr Welch, who wearing “the look of the happy leprechaun” – shot back: “a pixie is a close relative of a fairy.” The room erupted in nervous laughter, because homosexuality was part of this drama. This was the first time during the hearing that anyone had addressed the homosexual undercurrent between Cohn and Schine, because it was so central to psy ops being sold.

Cohn and Tel Aviv's goal was to make America's military weak and beholden to Israel. They wanted the American military infiltrated with beta's

Why did cohn use his own gayness to be the psy ops? Because he knew the real weakness of the American political scene in the 1950s.
Cohn wanted the rubicon to be crossed, so it would be open season on McCarthy. Cohn was cross-examined about whether he had ever stayed the night and had breakfast at Mr Schine’s apartment. Joining the chase, Senators asked Cohn whether he had a “special interest” in Mr Schine and his McCarthy appointment and questioned his “passionate anxiety” to retain Mr Schine’s services for the committee. McCarthy was played like a fiddle by Cohn. Why? Being Gay in 1950s America was more toxic then being a Communist Bolshevik. Cohn used this ruthlessly.

Arrows from politicians were fired from all directions. Representatives from both parties, and from both ends of the ideological spectrum felt no moral qualms attacking gay people in furtherance of their political agendas. For congressmen, the bought and paid from press, and the viewing public, it was simply accepted that gay men and women “deserved to be objects of derision”.

All this, of course, was at a time when the federal government was purging gays and lesbians from its workforce, while the military ferreted out “sexual deviants” within the services. This is what Cohn wanted done and he crafted a clever legal strategy to ruin the military from within. McCarthy was Israel useful idiot. “The lavender scare” – in which Cohn and his boss were ringleaders and participants, underlined that no charge, not even that of being a Bolshevik Communist, was more toxic than that of being, or suspected of being, gay. ### When Lansky bailed on Israel, Roy Cohn was created. When Roy Cohn Died, Epstein was created.​

It should come as no surprise that Jeffrey Epstein emails reveal he was linked to Bitcoin’s early ecosystem considering what I told about Lansky and Chaum. The evolution of Murder Inc's accounting arm went Lansky, Cohn, then Epstein. That is how the evolution occured. Cohn and Epstein only came on board after Bitcoin was outsourced post Lansky's death.

Here’s what’s happening now: The biggest red flag is how close Epstein was to Bitcoin funding channels, and early development circles. This is why people like Eric Wienstein are so interested why Epstein was at MIT so often checking in on science and finance stories. MIT is the node where DOD and intelligence intersect.

Here are the main links: - MIT Funding Epstein sent $850,000 to MIT between 2002 and 2017. Part of these funds supported the MIT Digital Currency Initiative, the group that paid Bitcoin Core developers during a funding crisis. - Developer Connection The DCI was paying people like Gavin Andresen and Wladimir van der Laan when the Bitcoin Foundation ran out of money. Epstein visited MIT nine times. He met staff privately.

He clearly had no idea how it operated because he wanted advice on receiving coins, paying with coins, distributing coins, and avoiding campaign finance rules. They were using Bitcoin for illegal purposes back then showing you they did not control it and were looking to use it for their gains.

This was in 2017, while he was trying to rebuild his network. These five links alone show Epstein had access to developers, regulators, politicians, donors, and early crypto investors.

Many believed this long ago because the connections were made to Brock Pierce, Adam Back, and Hal Finney. I have posted about that here on other parts of the forum. My info is now confirmed since his email went live today.

The emails show: - MIT underreported the donations - Epstein visited through back doors - Meetings were not logged - Senior MIT officials approved the money privately - Bitcoin developers were funded during the same period.

None of this proves Epstein controlled anything in Bitcoin so do not let the smooth brainers convince you otherwise. # Thread: LEVEL UP OR DUMBING IT DOWN?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/level-up-or-dumbing-it-down.31992/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-12-08T16:54:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/level-up-or-dumbing-it-down.31992/post-363696>

**LEVEL UP OR DUMBING IT DOWN?**

Why you should make people level up to your work and not dumb it down for them.

Are you sure we aren’t all animals?

In 1931, two psychologists, Winthrop and Luella Kellogg, began an experiment to see if a chimpanzee could learn human behavior by raising it with their own baby. The idea was to see if the chimp, named Gua, would learn to walk upright, speak, and engage in other human-like activities.

For the first few months, the experiment was going well. Gua learned to mimic some of the actions of Kelloggs’ son, Donald.

However, after 9 months, the Kelloggs were forced to cancel the experiment. It turns out that Donald was beginning to behave like an ape himself, mimicking Gua’s behaviors and even attempting to communicate with grunts and other animal-like sounds.

These repurposed agents target the upstream photonic/electronic/coherence failures I teach about in my work that fix cells in the primitive α-state (fermentation-dominant, proliferative, dedifferentiated), helping revert to ordered β-state physiology—complementing chemo's downstream cytotoxicity.

For example: Ivermectin (1.5 mg/kg/day)Ivermectin induces mitochondrial dysfunction in cancer cells: reduces membrane potential, triggers ROS overproduction, cytochrome c release, and intrinsic apoptosis (caspase-9/3 activation). It inhibits respiration (complex I), forcing oxidative stress that cancer cells (reliant on pseudohypoxic glycolysis) can't buffer, while healthy cells tolerate it. This disrupts incoherent singlet excitations/UPE in α-state tumors, promoting ordered triplet collapse or ferroptosis-like death. In esophageal squamous cell carcinoma models, it degrades PAK1 (pro-survival kinase tied to HIF-1α), suppressing growth/metastasis which directly countering unchecked HIF from PER2 desynchrony.

Fenbendazole (1500 mg/day; benzimidazole family, similar to mebendazole)Fenbendazole acts as a moderate microtubule destabilizer, arresting mitosis (G2/M block) in rapidly dividing cancer cells, while inhibiting glucose uptake (downregulates GLUTs/hexokinase II), starving Warburg-dependent tumors. It translocates p53 to mitochondria for apoptosis, impairs proteasomal function, and induces ferroptosis/autophagy in resistant lines. This attacks α-state hallmarks: biosynthetic diversion for proliferation and lost structural order (microtubules critical for confinement/coherence). In esophageal/gastric models, benzimidazoles synergize with chemo, enhancing tumor shrinkage—mirroring the case's pre-surgical reduction.

CBD Oil (100 mg/day)CBD perturbs mitochondrial calcium homeostasis and membrane potential, triggering BID translocation, cytochrome c release, and crosstalk between apoptosis/autophagy. At therapeutic doses, it shifts protective autophagy (α-survival) to apoptotic dominance via ROS/beclin-1 cleavage, while modulating ER stress and p53. In inflammation/cancer, it regulates Nrf2/ECS tone (GOE buffers), quenching excess ROS while promoting ordered cell death. For esophageal cancers (high oxidative microenvironment), CBD's heme-like modulation amplifies coherent UPE signaling for tolerance/repair in healthy cells but overloads incoherent tumor mitochondria.

Modified Citrus Pectin (MCP, 15 g/day)MCP binds/inhibits galectin-3 (overexpressed in metastatic cancers, including esophageal), blocking homotypic aggregation, endothelial adhesion, angiogenesis, and anti-apoptotic survival signaling. Gal-3 promotes α-like dedifferentiation and immune evasion (e.g., Treg tolerance hijack, linking to EPO-EPOR axis). MCP reduces metastasis risk, sensitizes to chemo-induced apoptosis, and prevents spread/recurrence—explaining the case's clean surgical margins and no evidence of disease post-op.

Synergistic Fit in my Decentralized Thesis: Restoring β-Order These agents converge on reversing regulatory failure (Szent-Györgyi's "brake"): mitochondrial/electronic disruption starves α-glycolysis (ivermectin/fenbendazole), enforces ordered death (CBD apoptosis shift), and blocks Gal-3-mediated escape (MCP). Combined with chemo (DNA damage), they target cancer stem cells (CSCs, α-like reservoirs) via ferroptosis/autophagy modulation and prevent dissemination. The rapid shrinkage/surgical conversion suggests early intervention exploits residual β-flexibility before full fixation. Unlike Seyfried's primary mitochondrial "defect," this supports upstream coherence loss (light desynchrony → PER2-HIF pseudohypoxia → α-reversion), with repurposed drugs as photonic/electronic proxies by restoring confinement, redox balance, and tolerance signaling without needing perfect sunrise entrainment.

This case exemplifies how my thesis operates: cancer as lost multi-scale order (electronic → metabolic → immune), reversible via agents mimicking GOE adaptations (heme/melanin buffering, structured interactions). Dr. Makis is like having my decentralized lab on X for the world to see. He does a lot, cannot explain it, but I can.

It is a promising anecdotal pattern of how decentralized medicine will operate in 2025 repurposed protocols. We should still want and need though rigorous trials, but for now while the fascists are running govenrrment and centralized medicine this will have to do.
